theorems.source_text: abbrev Vec (n : Nat) := Fin n → Real
theorems.signature: ℕ → Type
theorems.proof_body: fun n => Fin n → ℝ
theorems.name: M2RNNComparison.Vec

theorems.source_text: abbrev MatState (K V : Nat) := Matrix (Fin K) (Fin V) Real
theorems.signature: ℕ → ℕ → Type
theorems.proof_body: fun K V => Matrix (Fin K) (Fin V) ℝ
theorems.name: M2RNNComparison.MatState

theorems.signature: {K V : ℕ} → (ℝ → ℝ) → M2RNNComparison.MatState K V → M2RNNComparison.MatState K V
theorems.proof_body: fun {K V} f H => Matrix.of fun i j => f (H i j)
theorems.name: M2RNNComparison.matrixMap
theorems.doc: Elementwise map over a matrix.

theorems.source_text:
/-- Elementwise `tanh` over a matrix. -/
noncomputable def matrixTanh (H : MatState K V) : MatState K V :=
  matrixMap Real.tanh H
theorems.signature: {K V : ℕ} → M2RNNComparison.MatState K V → M2RNNComparison.MatState K V
theorems.proof_body: fun {K V} H => M2RNNComparison.matrixMap Real.tanh H
theorems.name: M2RNNComparison.matrixTanh
theorems.doc: Elementwise `tanh` over a matrix.

theorems.signature: {K V : ℕ} → M2RNNComparison.Vec K → M2RNNComparison.Vec V → M2RNNComparison.MatState K V
theorems.proof_body: fun {K V} k v => Matrix.of fun i j => k i * v j
theorems.name: M2RNNComparison.outerKV
theorems.doc: Outer product `k v^T`, with shape `K x V`.

theorems.signature: {K V : ℕ} → M2RNNComparison.MatState K V → M2RNNComparison.Vec K → M2RNNComparison.Vec V
theorems.proof_body: fun {K V} H q => (Matrix.transpose H).mulVec q
theorems.name: M2RNNComparison.queryReadout
theorems.doc: Query readout `H^T q`, with shape `V`.

theorems.source_text:
/-- M2RNN candidate state:

`Z_t = tanh(H_{t-1} W + k_t v_t^T)`.
-/
noncomputable def m2rnnCandidate
    (W : Matrix (Fin V) (Fin V) Real)
    (H : MatState K V) (k : Vec K) (v : Vec V) : MatState K V :=
  matrixTanh (H * W + outerKV k v)
theorems.signature:
{K V : ℕ} →
  Matrix (Fin V) (Fin V) ℝ →
    M2RNNComparison.MatState K V →
      M2RNNComparison.Vec K → M2RNNComparison.Vec V → M2RNNComparison.MatState K V
theorems.proof_body: fun {K V} W H k v => M2RNNComparison.matrixTanh (H * W + M2RNNComparison.outerKV k v)
theorems.name: M2RNNComparison.m2rnnCandidate
theorems.doc:
M2RNN candidate state:

`Z_t = tanh(H_{t-1} W + k_t v_t^T)`.

theorems.source_text:
/-- M2RNN state update:

`H_t = f_t H_{t-1} + (1 - f_t) Z_t`.

Here `f` is scalar per head. Vector-gated variants can be represented later by
replacing scalar multiplication with row/column gating.
-/
noncomputable def m2rnnUpdate
    (W : Matrix (Fin V) (Fin V) Real) (f : Real)
    (H : MatState K V) (k : Vec K) (v : Vec V) : MatState K V :=
  f • H + (1 - f) • m2rnnCandidate W H k v
theorems.signature:
{K V : ℕ} →
  Matrix (Fin V) (Fin V) ℝ →
    ℝ →
      M2RNNComparison.MatState K V →
        M2RNNComparison.Vec K → M2RNNComparison.Vec V → M2RNNComparison.MatState K V
theorems.proof_body: fun {K V} W f H k v => f • H + (1 - f) • M2RNNComparison.m2rnnCandidate W H k v
theorems.name: M2RNNComparison.m2rnnUpdate
theorems.doc:
M2RNN state update:

`H_t = f_t H_{t-1} + (1 - f_t) Z_t`.

Here `f` is scalar per head. Vector-gated variants can be represented later by
replacing scalar multiplication with row/column gating.

theorems.source_text:
/-- M2RNN readout with residual value path:

`y_t = H_t^T q_t + w_r * v_t`.
-/
def m2rnnReadout (H : MatState K V) (q : Vec K) (v wr : Vec V) : Vec V :=
  queryReadout H q + fun j => wr j * v j
theorems.signature:
{K V : ℕ} →
  M2RNNComparison.MatState K V →
    M2RNNComparison.Vec K → M2RNNComparison.Vec V → M2RNNComparison.Vec V → M2RNNComparison.Vec V
theorems.proof_body: fun {K V} H q v wr => M2RNNComparison.queryReadout H q + fun j => wr j * v j
theorems.name: M2RNNComparison.m2rnnReadout
theorems.doc:
M2RNN readout with residual value path:

`y_t = H_t^T q_t + w_r * v_t`.

theorems.source_text:
/-- Delta-rule retrieval error:

`delta_t = v_t - H_{t-1}^T k_t`.
-/
def e88Delta (H : MatState K V) (k : Vec K) (v : Vec V) : Vec V :=
  v - queryReadout H k
theorems.signature:
{K V : ℕ} →
  M2RNNComparison.MatState K V →
    M2RNNComparison.Vec K → M2RNNComparison.Vec V → M2RNNComparison.Vec V
theorems.proof_body: fun {K V} H k v => v - M2RNNComparison.queryReadout H k
theorems.name: M2RNNComparison.e88Delta
theorems.doc:
Delta-rule retrieval error:

`delta_t = v_t - H_{t-1}^T k_t`.

theorems.source_text:
/-- E88 delta update in direct write form:

`H_t = tanh(lambda H_{t-1} + k_t delta_t^T)`.
-/
noncomputable def e88DeltaUpdateDirect
    (lambda : Real) (H : MatState K V) (k : Vec K) (v : Vec V) : MatState K V :=
  matrixTanh (lambda • H + outerKV k (e88Delta H k v))
theorems.signature:
{K V : ℕ} →
  ℝ →
    M2RNNComparison.MatState K V →
      M2RNNComparison.Vec K → M2RNNComparison.Vec V → M2RNNComparison.MatState K V
theorems.proof_body:
fun {K V} lambda H k v =>
  M2RNNComparison.matrixTanh
    (lambda • H + M2RNNComparison.outerKV k (M2RNNComparison.e88Delta H k v))
theorems.name: M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateDirect
theorems.doc:
E88 delta update in direct write form:

`H_t = tanh(lambda H_{t-1} + k_t delta_t^T)`.

theorems.source_text:
/-- E88's expanded delta transition:

`A_t(k) = lambda I - k k^T`.

With normalized `k`, this is projection/Householder-adjacent: it modifies the
key direction and leaves orthogonal directions controlled by `lambda`.
-/
def e88DeltaTransition (lambda : Real) (k : Vec K) :
    Matrix (Fin K) (Fin K) Real :=
  lambda • (1 : Matrix (Fin K) (Fin K) Real) - outerKV k k
theorems.signature: {K : ℕ} → ℝ → M2RNNComparison.Vec K → Matrix (Fin K) (Fin K) ℝ
theorems.proof_body: fun {K} lambda k => lambda • 1 - M2RNNComparison.outerKV k k
theorems.name: M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaTransition
theorems.doc:
E88's expanded delta transition:

`A_t(k) = lambda I - k k^T`.

With normalized `k`, this is projection/Householder-adjacent: it modifies the
key direction and leaves orthogonal directions controlled by `lambda`.

theorems.source_text:
/-- E88 delta update in expanded transition form:

`H_t = tanh((lambda I - k k^T) H_{t-1} + k_t v_t^T)`.

This is the form that makes the comparison to M2RNN sharp:

* M2RNN: fixed learned right transition `H W`.
* E88: input-dependent left transition `A_t(k) H`.
-/
noncomputable def e88DeltaUpdateExpanded
    (lambda : Real) (H : MatState K V) (k : Vec K) (v : Vec V) : MatState K V :=
  matrixTanh (e88DeltaTransition lambda k * H + outerKV k v)
theorems.signature:
{K V : ℕ} →
  ℝ →
    M2RNNComparison.MatState K V →
      M2RNNComparison.Vec K → M2RNNComparison.Vec V → M2RNNComparison.MatState K V
theorems.proof_body:
fun {K V} lambda H k v =>
  M2RNNComparison.matrixTanh
    (M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaTransition lambda k * H + M2RNNComparison.outerKV k v)
theorems.name: M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded
theorems.doc:
E88 delta update in expanded transition form:

`H_t = tanh((lambda I - k k^T) H_{t-1} + k_t v_t^T)`.

This is the form that makes the comparison to M2RNN sharp:

* M2RNN: fixed learned right transition `H W`.
* E88: input-dependent left transition `A_t(k) H`.

inductive RecurrenceClass where
  | affineMatrixState
  | nonlinearVectorState
  | nonlinearMatrixState
  | nonlinearDeltaMatrixState
  deriving Repr, DecidableEq
theorems.signature: Type
theorems.name: M2RNNComparison.RecurrenceClass
theorems.doc: Coarse recurrence class.

theorems.source_text:
/-- Coarse recurrence class. -/
inductive RecurrenceClass where
  | affineMatrixState
  | nonlinearVectorState
  | nonlinearMatrixState
  | nonlinearDeltaMatrixState
  deriving Repr, DecidableEq
theorems.signature:
{motive : M2RNNComparison.RecurrenceClass → Sort u} →
  (t : M2RNNComparison.RecurrenceClass) →
    motive M2RNNComparison.RecurrenceClass.affineMatrixState →
      motive M2RNNComparison.RecurrenceClass.nonlinearVectorState →
        motive M2RNNComparison.RecurrenceClass.nonlinearMatrixState →
          motive M2RNNComparison.RecurrenceClass.nonlinearDeltaMatrixState → motive t
theorems.proof_body:
fun {motive} t affineMatrixState nonlinearVectorState nonlinearMatrixState
    nonlinearDeltaMatrixState =>
  M2RNNComparison.RecurrenceClass.rec affineMatrixState nonlinearVectorState nonlinearMatrixState
    nonlinearDeltaMatrixState t
theorems.name: M2RNNComparison.RecurrenceClass.casesOn

theorems.source_text:
/-- Coarse recurrence class. -/
inductive RecurrenceClass where
  | affineMatrixState
  | nonlinearVectorState
  | nonlinearMatrixState
  | nonlinearDeltaMatrixState
  deriving Repr, DecidableEq
theorems.signature:
{motive : M2RNNComparison.RecurrenceClass → Sort u} →
  (ctorIdx : ℕ) →
    (t : M2RNNComparison.RecurrenceClass) →
      ctorIdx = t.ctorIdx → M2RNNComparison.RecurrenceClass.ctorElimType ctorIdx → motive t
theorems.proof_body:
fun {motive} ctorIdx t h k =>
  M2RNNComparison.RecurrenceClass.casesOn (motive := fun t => ctorIdx = t.ctorIdx → motive t) t
    (fun h => (h ▸ k).down) (fun h => (h ▸ k).down) (fun h => (h ▸ k).down) (fun h => (h ▸ k).down)
    h
theorems.name: M2RNNComparison.RecurrenceClass.ctorElim

theorems.signature: M2RNNComparison.RecurrenceClass → ℕ → Format
theorems.proof_body:
fun x prec =>
  match x with
  | M2RNNComparison.RecurrenceClass.affineMatrixState =>
    Repr.addAppParen
      (Format.nest (if prec ≥ 1024 then 1 else 2)
          (Std.Format.text "M2RNNComparison.RecurrenceClass.affineMatrixState")).group
      prec
  | M2RNNComparison.RecurrenceClass.nonlinearVectorState =>
    Repr.addAppParen
      (Format.nest (if prec ≥ 1024 then 1 else 2)
          (Std.Format.text "M2RNNComparison.RecurrenceClass.nonlinearVectorState")).group
      prec
  | M2RNNComparison.RecurrenceClass.nonlinearMatrixState =>
    Repr.addAppParen
      (Format.nest (if prec ≥ 1024 then 1 else 2)
          (Std.Format.text "M2RNNComparison.RecurrenceClass.nonlinearMatrixState")).group
      prec
  | M2RNNComparison.RecurrenceClass.nonlinearDeltaMatrixState =>
    Repr.addAppParen
      (Format.nest (if prec ≥ 1024 then 1 else 2)
          (Std.Format.text "M2RNNComparison.RecurrenceClass.nonlinearDeltaMatrixState")).group
      prec

theorems.source_text: deriving Repr, DecidableEq
theorems.signature: (x x_1 : M2RNNComparison.MatrixRecurrenceFeatures) → Decidable (x = x_1)
theorems.proof_body:
fun x x_1 =>
  match x, x_1 with
  |
  { recurrenceClass := a, transitionSide := a_1, learnedFixedTransition := a_2,
      inputDependentTransition := a_3, rawOuterWrite := a_4, deltaWrite := a_5,
      externalForgetCarry := a_6, residualValuePath := a_7, queryReadout := a_8 },
    { recurrenceClass := b, transitionSide := b_1, learnedFixedTransition := b_2,
      inputDependentTransition := b_3, rawOuterWrite := b_4, deltaWrite := b_5,
      externalForgetCarry := b_6, residualValuePath := b_7, queryReadout := b_8 } =>
    if h : a = b then
      h ▸
        if h : a_1 = b_1 then
          h ▸
            if h : a_2 = b_2 then
              h ▸
                if h : a_3 = b_3 then
                  h ▸
                    if h : a_4 = b_4 then
                      h ▸
                        if h : a_5 = b_5 then
                          h ▸
                            if h : a_6 = b_6 then
                              h ▸
                                if h : a_7 = b_7 then
                                  h ▸ if h : a_8 = b_8 then h ▸ isTrue ⋯ else isFalse ⋯
                                else isFalse ⋯
                            else isFalse ⋯
                        else isFalse ⋯
                    else isFalse ⋯
                else isFalse ⋯
            else isFalse ⋯
        else isFalse ⋯
    else isFalse ⋯
theorems.name: M2RNNComparison.instDecidableEqMatrixRecurrenceFeatures.decEq

theorems.source_text:
/-- Raw-write nonlinear matrix recurrence used as a common ancestor. -/
noncomputable def rawWriteNonlinearUpdate
    (H : MatState K V) (k : Vec K) (v : Vec V) : MatState K V :=
  matrixTanh (H + outerKV k v)
theorems.signature:
{K V : ℕ} →
  M2RNNComparison.MatState K V →
    M2RNNComparison.Vec K → M2RNNComparison.Vec V → M2RNNComparison.MatState K V
theorems.proof_body: fun {K V} H k v => M2RNNComparison.matrixTanh (H + M2RNNComparison.outerKV k v)
theorems.name: M2RNNComparison.rawWriteNonlinearUpdate
theorems.doc: Raw-write nonlinear matrix recurrence used as a common ancestor.

theorems.source_text:
/-- M2RNN with identity transition and no forget carry has the raw-write core. -/
theorem m2rnn_identity_no_forget_is_raw_write
    (H : MatState K V) (k : Vec K) (v : Vec V) :
    m2rnnUpdate (1 : Matrix (Fin V) (Fin V) Real) 0 H k v =
      rawWriteNonlinearUpdate H k v := by
  simp [m2rnnUpdate, m2rnnCandidate, rawWriteNonlinearUpdate]
theorems.signature:
∀ {K V : ℕ} (H : M2RNNComparison.MatState K V) (k : M2RNNComparison.Vec K)
  (v : M2RNNComparison.Vec V),
  M2RNNComparison.m2rnnUpdate 1 0 H k v = M2RNNComparison.rawWriteNonlinearUpdate H k v
theorems.proof_body:
fun {K V} H k v =>
  of_eq_true
    (Eq.trans
      (congrArg (fun x => x = M2RNNComparison.matrixTanh (H + M2RNNComparison.outerKV k v))
        (Eq.trans
          (congr (congrArg HAdd.hAdd (zero_smul ℝ H))
            (Eq.trans
              (congr (congrArg HSMul.hSMul (sub_zero 1))
                (congrArg (fun x => M2RNNComparison.matrixTanh (x + M2RNNComparison.outerKV k v))
                  (Matrix.mul_one H)))
              (one_smul ℝ (M2RNNComparison.matrixTanh (H + M2RNNComparison.outerKV k v)))))
          (zero_add (M2RNNComparison.matrixTanh (H + M2RNNComparison.outerKV k v)))))
      (eq_self (M2RNNComparison.matrixTanh (H + M2RNNComparison.outerKV k v))))
theorems.name: M2RNNComparison.m2rnn_identity_no_forget_is_raw_write
theorems.doc: M2RNN with identity transition and no forget carry has the raw-write core.

theorems.source_text:
/-- Direct and expanded E88/NDM delta updates are algebraically identical. -/
theorem e88DeltaUpdateDirect_eq_expanded
    (lambda : Real) (H : MatState K V) (k : Vec K) (v : Vec V) :
    e88DeltaUpdateDirect lambda H k v =
      e88DeltaUpdateExpanded lambda H k v := by
  ext i j
  unfold e88DeltaUpdateDirect e88DeltaUpdateExpanded matrixTanh matrixMap
  simp only [Matrix.of_apply]
  apply congrArg Real.tanh
  simp [e88DeltaTransition, e88Delta, queryReadout, outerKV,
    Matrix.mul_apply, Matrix.mulVec, dotProduct]
  have h_id :
      (∑ x : Fin K, lambda * ((1 : Matrix (Fin K) (Fin K) Real) i x) * H x j) =
        lambda * H i j := by
    rw [Fintype.sum_eq_single i]
    · simp
    · intro x hx
      have hix : i ≠ x := by
        intro h
        exact hx h.symm
      simp [hix]
  have h_k :
      (∑ x : Fin K, k i * k x * H x j) =
        k i * ∑ x : Fin K, H x j * k x := by
    rw [Finset.mul_sum]
    apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
    intro x _
    ring
  have h_sum :
      (∑ x : Fin K, (lambda * ((1 : Matrix (Fin K) (Fin K) Real) i x) - k i * k x) * H x j) =
        lambda * H i j - k i * ∑ x : Fin K, H x j * k x := by
    calc
      (∑ x : Fin K, (lambda * ((1 : Matrix (Fin K) (Fin K) Real) i x) - k i * k x) * H x j)
          = ∑ x : Fin K,
              (lambda * ((1 : Matrix (Fin K) (Fin K) Real) i x) * H x j -
                k i * k x * H x j) := by
            apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
            intro x _
            ring
      _ = (∑ x : Fin K, lambda * ((1 : Matrix (Fin K) (Fin K) Real) i x) * H x j) -
          (∑ x : Fin K, k i * k x * H x j) := by
            rw [Finset.sum_sub_distrib]
      _ = lambda * H i j - k i * ∑ x : Fin K, H x j * k x := by
            rw [h_id, h_k]
  rw [h_sum]
  ring
theorems.signature:
∀ {K V : ℕ} (lambda : ℝ) (H : M2RNNComparison.MatState K V) (k : M2RNNComparison.Vec K)
  (v : M2RNNComparison.Vec V),
  M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateDirect lambda H k v =
    M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded lambda H k v
theorems.proof_body:
fun {K V} lambda H k v =>
  Matrix.ext fun i j =>

                          (lambda ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * (H i j ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1))
                          (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_pf_zero_add
                            (k i ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * (v j ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1) +
                              (k i ^ Nat.rawCast 1 *
                                  ((∑ x, H x j * k x) ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * (Int.negOfNat 1).rawCast) +
                                0)))))
                      (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_congr
                        (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.sub_congr
                          (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_congr (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.atom_pf lambda)
                            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.atom_pf (H i j))
                            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_mul
                              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_add
                                (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_pf_left lambda (Nat.rawCast 1)
                                  (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_pf_right (H i j) (Nat.rawCast 1)
                                    (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.one_mul (Nat.rawCast 1))))
                                (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_zero
                                  (lambda ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1))
                                (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_pf_add_zero
                                  (lambda ^ Nat.rawCast 1 *
                                      (H i j ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1) +
                                    0)))
                              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.zero_mul
                                (H i j ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1 + 0))
                              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_pf_add_zero
                                (lambda ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * (H i j ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1) +
                                  0))))
                          (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_congr (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.atom_pf (k i))
                            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.atom_pf (∑ x, H x j * k x))
                            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_mul
                              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_add
                                (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_pf_left (k i) (Nat.rawCast 1)
                                  (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_pf_right (∑ x, H x j * k x)
                                    (Nat.rawCast 1) (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.one_mul (Nat.rawCast 1))))
                                (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_zero (k i ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1))
                                (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_pf_add_zero
                                  (k i ^ Nat.rawCast 1 *
                                      ((∑ x, H x j * k x) ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1) +
                                    0)))
                              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.zero_mul
                                ((∑ x, H x j * k x) ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1 + 0))
                              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_pf_add_zero
                                (k i ^ Nat.rawCast 1 *
                                    ((∑ x, H x j * k x) ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1) +
                                  0))))
                          (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.sub_pf
                            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.neg_add
                              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.neg_mul (k i) (Nat.rawCast 1)
                                (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.neg_mul (∑ x, H x j * k x) (Nat.rawCast 1)
                                  (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.neg_one_mul
                                    (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.IsInt.to_raw_eq
                                      (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isInt_mul (Eq.refl HMul.hMul)
                                        (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.IsInt.of_raw ℝ (Int.negOfNat 1))
                                        (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.IsNat.to_isInt
                                          (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.IsNat.of_raw ℝ 1))
                                        (Eq.refl (Int.negOfNat 1)))))))
                              Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.neg_zero)
                            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_pf_add_lt
                              (lambda ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * (H i j ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1))
                              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_pf_zero_add
                                (k i ^ Nat.rawCast 1 *
                                    ((∑ x, H x j * k x) ^ Nat.rawCast 1 *
                                      (Int.negOfNat 1).rawCast) +
                                  0)))))
                        (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_congr (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.atom_pf (k i))
                          (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.atom_pf (v j))
                          (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_mul
                            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_add
                              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_pf_left (k i) (Nat.rawCast 1)
                                (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_pf_right (v j) (Nat.rawCast 1)
                                  (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.one_mul (Nat.rawCast 1))))
                              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_zero (k i ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1))
                              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_pf_add_zero
                                (k i ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * (v j ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1) + 0)))
                            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.zero_mul (v j ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1 + 0))
                            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_pf_add_zero
                              (k i ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * (v j ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1) + 0))))
                        (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_pf_add_lt
                          (lambda ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * (H i j ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1))
                          (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_pf_add_gt
                            (k i ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * (v j ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1))
                            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_pf_add_zero
                              (k i ^ Nat.rawCast 1 *
                                  ((∑ x, H x j * k x) ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * (Int.negOfNat 1).rawCast) +
                                0)))))))))))))
theorems.name: M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateDirect_eq_expanded
theorems.doc: Direct and expanded E88/NDM delta updates are algebraically identical.

theorems.source_text:
/-- A fixed-right raw-write M2RNN candidate implements E88/NDM's direct delta
update when its value path is the retrieval error and its fixed transition is
`lambda I`. -/
theorem m2rnnCandidate_with_delta_value_eq_e88_direct
    (lambda : Real) (H : MatState K V) (k : Vec K) (v : Vec V) :
    m2rnnCandidate (lambda • (1 : Matrix (Fin V) (Fin V) Real))
        H k (e88Delta H k v) =
      e88DeltaUpdateDirect lambda H k v := by
  ext i j
  simp [m2rnnCandidate, e88DeltaUpdateDirect, e88Delta, queryReadout,
    outerKV, matrixTanh, matrixMap, Matrix.mulVec,
    dotProduct]
theorems.signature:
∀ {K V : ℕ} (lambda : ℝ) (H : M2RNNComparison.MatState K V) (k : M2RNNComparison.Vec K)
  (v : M2RNNComparison.Vec V),
  M2RNNComparison.m2rnnCandidate (lambda • 1) H k (M2RNNComparison.e88Delta H k v) =
    M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateDirect lambda H k v
theorems.proof_body:
fun {K V} lambda H k v =>
  Matrix.ext fun i j =>
    of_eq_true
      (Eq.trans
        (congr
          (congrArg (fun x => Eq (Matrix.of x i j))
            (funext fun i =>
              funext fun j =>
                congrArg (fun x => Real.tanh (x i j))
                  (congr
                    (congrArg HAdd.hAdd
                      (Eq.trans (Matrix.mul_smul H lambda 1)
                        (congrArg (HSMul.hSMul lambda) (Matrix.mul_one H))))
                    (congrArg (⇑Matrix.of)
                      (funext fun i =>
                        funext fun j =>
                          congrArg (fun x => k i * (v j - x))
                            (Finset.sum_congr (Eq.refl Finset.univ) fun x a =>
                              Eq.refl (H x j * k x)))))))
          (congrArg (fun x => Matrix.of x i j)
            (funext fun i =>
              funext fun j =>
                congrArg (fun x => Real.tanh ((lambda • H + Matrix.of x) i j))
                  (funext fun i =>
                    funext fun j =>
                      congrArg (fun x => k i * (v j - x))
                        (Finset.sum_congr (Eq.refl Finset.univ) fun x a =>
                          Eq.refl (H x j * k x))))))
        (eq_self (Real.tanh (lambda * H i j + k i * (v j - ∑ x, H x j * k x)))))
theorems.name: M2RNNComparison.m2rnnCandidate_with_delta_value_eq_e88_direct
theorems.doc:
A fixed-right raw-write M2RNN candidate implements E88/NDM's direct delta
update when its value path is the retrieval error and its fixed transition is
`lambda I`.

theorems.source_text:
/-- Positive embedding theorem: M2RNN can match one E88/NDM delta step if it is
given the extra read-then-delta resource that computes `v - Hᵀk` before the raw
write.

This is the formal complement to the one-step separation theorem: fixed
raw-write M2RNN does not natively contain the delta correction, but an augmented
read-then-delta M2RNN candidate embeds the E88/NDM update exactly. -/
theorem m2rnn_read_then_delta_embeds_e88_delta_update
    (lambda : Real) (H : MatState K V) (k : Vec K) (v : Vec V) :
    m2rnnCandidate (lambda • (1 : Matrix (Fin V) (Fin V) Real))
        H k (e88Delta H k v) =
      e88DeltaUpdateExpanded lambda H k v := by
  rw [m2rnnCandidate_with_delta_value_eq_e88_direct,
    e88DeltaUpdateDirect_eq_expanded]
theorems.signature:
∀ {K V : ℕ} (lambda : ℝ) (H : M2RNNComparison.MatState K V) (k : M2RNNComparison.Vec K)
  (v : M2RNNComparison.Vec V),
  M2RNNComparison.m2rnnCandidate (lambda • 1) H k (M2RNNComparison.e88Delta H k v) =
    M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded lambda H k v
theorems.proof_body:
fun {K V} lambda H k v =>
  Eq.mpr
    (id
      (congrArg (fun _a => _a = M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded lambda H k v)
        (M2RNNComparison.m2rnnCandidate_with_delta_value_eq_e88_direct lambda H k v)))
    (Eq.mpr
      (id
        (congrArg (fun _a => _a = M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded lambda H k v)
          (M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateDirect_eq_expanded lambda H k v)))
      (Eq.refl (M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded lambda H k v)))
theorems.name: M2RNNComparison.m2rnn_read_then_delta_embeds_e88_delta_update

Positive embedding theorem: M2RNN can match one E88/NDM delta step if it is

This is the formal complement to the one-step separation theorem: fixed

theorems.source_text:
theorem lowerLeftState_row0 (j : Fin 2) :
    lowerLeftState 0 j = 0 := by
  fin_cases j <;> simp [lowerLeftState, Matrix.of_apply]
theorems.signature: ∀ (j : Fin 2), RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState 0 j = 0
theorems.proof_body:
fun j =>
  List.Mem.casesOn (motive := fun a x =>
    List.finRange 2 = a →
      Fintype.complete j ≍ x → RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState 0 j = 0)
    (Fintype.complete j)
    (fun as h =>
      List.cons.noConfusion
        (Fintype.complete j ≍ List.Mem.head as → RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState 0 j = 0)
        ((fun i => i) ⟨0, Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_refl 2)⟩)
        ((fun x1 x2 => (fun i => i) x1 :: x2) ⟨1, Nat.le_refl 2⟩ []) j as h fun head_eq =>
        Eq.ndrec (motive := fun j =>
          ∀ (this : j ∈ Fintype.elems),
            (fun x1 x2 => (fun i => i) x1 :: x2) ⟨1, Nat.le_refl 2⟩ [] = as →
              this ≍ List.Mem.head as → RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState 0 j = 0)
          (fun this tail_eq =>
            Eq.ndrec (motive := fun as =>
              this ≍ List.Mem.head as →
                RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState 0
                    ((fun i => i) ⟨0, Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_refl 2)⟩) =
                  0)
              (fun h =>
                of_eq_true
                  (Eq.trans
                    (congrArg (fun x => x = 0)
                      (ite_cond_eq_false (if 0 = 0 then 1 else 0) 0 zero_ne_one._simp_1))
                    (eq_self 0)))
              tail_eq)
          head_eq (Fintype.complete j))
    (fun b {as} a h =>
      List.cons.noConfusion
        (Fintype.complete j ≍ List.Mem.tail b a → RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState 0 j = 0)
        ((fun i => i) ⟨0, Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_refl 2)⟩)
        ((fun x1 x2 => (fun i => i) x1 :: x2) ⟨1, Nat.le_refl 2⟩ []) b as h fun head_eq =>
        Eq.ndrec (motive := fun b =>
          (fun x1 x2 => (fun i => i) x1 :: x2) ⟨1, Nat.le_refl 2⟩ [] = as →
            Fintype.complete j ≍ List.Mem.tail b a →
              RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState 0 j = 0)
          (fun tail_eq =>
            Eq.ndrec (motive := fun {as} =>
              ∀ (a : List.Mem j as),
                Fintype.complete j ≍
                    List.Mem.tail ((fun i => i) ⟨0, Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_refl 2)⟩) a →
                  RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState 0 j = 0)
              (fun a h =>
                List.Mem.casesOn (motive := fun a_1 x =>
                  (fun x1 x2 => (fun i => i) x1 :: x2) ⟨1, Nat.le_refl 2⟩ [] = a_1 →
                    a ≍ x → RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState 0 j = 0)
                  a
                  (fun as h =>
                    List.cons.noConfusion
                      (a ≍ List.Mem.head as → RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState 0 j = 0)
                      ((fun i => i) ⟨1, Nat.le_refl 2⟩) [] j as h fun head_eq =>
                      Eq.ndrec (motive := fun j =>
                        ∀
                          (a :
                            List.Mem j
                              ((fun x1 x2 => (fun i => i) x1 :: x2) ⟨1, Nat.le_refl 2⟩ [])),
                          [] = as →
                            a ≍ List.Mem.head as →
                              RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState 0 j = 0)
                        (fun a tail_eq =>
                          Eq.ndrec (motive := fun as =>
                            a ≍ List.Mem.head as →
                              RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState 0
                                  ((fun i => i) ⟨1, Nat.le_refl 2⟩) =
                                0)
                            (fun h =>
                              of_eq_true
                                (Eq.trans
                                  (congrArg (fun x => x = 0)
                                    (ite_cond_eq_false (if 1 = 0 then 1 else 0) 0
                                      zero_ne_one._simp_1))
                                  (eq_self 0)))
                            tail_eq)
                        head_eq a)
                  (fun b {as} a_1 h =>
                    List.cons.noConfusion
                      (a ≍ List.Mem.tail b a_1 → RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState 0 j = 0)
                      ((fun i => i) ⟨1, Nat.le_refl 2⟩) [] b as h fun head_eq =>
                      Eq.ndrec (motive := fun b =>
                        [] = as →
                          a ≍ List.Mem.tail b a_1 →
                            RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState 0 j = 0)
                        (fun tail_eq =>
                          Eq.ndrec (motive := fun {as} =>
                            ∀ (a_2 : List.Mem j as),
                              a ≍ List.Mem.tail ((fun i => i) ⟨1, Nat.le_refl 2⟩) a_2 →
                                RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState 0 j = 0)
                            (fun a_2 h =>
                              List.Mem.casesOn (motive := fun a x =>
                                [] = a →
                                  a_2 ≍ x → RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState 0 j = 0)
                                a_2 (fun as h => False.elim (noConfusion_of_Nat List.ctorIdx h))
                                (fun b {as} a h => False.elim (noConfusion_of_Nat List.ctorIdx h))
                                (Eq.refl []) (HEq.refl a_2))
                            tail_eq a_1)
                        head_eq)
                  (Eq.refl ((fun x1 x2 => (fun i => i) x1 :: x2) ⟨1, Nat.le_refl 2⟩ []))
                  (HEq.refl a))
              tail_eq a)
          head_eq)
    (Eq.refl (List.finRange 2)) (HEq.refl (Fintype.complete j))
theorems.name: MultiStepSeparation.lowerLeftState_row0

theorems.source_text:
/-- Row 0 of the M2RNN candidate `tanh(H · W + k · vᵀ)` is zero when
row 0 of `H` is zero and either `v = 0` or `k(0) = 0`.

In our witness we use `v = 0`, so the outer product `k · vᵀ` vanishes; the
right-multiplication `H · W` keeps row 0 zero by row locality. -/
theorem m2rnnCandidate_row0_zero_at_zero_row
    (W : TwoMat) (k : TwoVec) (H : TwoMat)
    (hrow : ∀ j, H 0 j = 0) (j : Fin 2) :
    M2RNNComparison.m2rnnCandidate W H k (0 : TwoVec) 0 j = 0 := by
  simp [M2RNNComparison.m2rnnCandidate, M2RNNComparison.matrixTanh,
    M2RNNComparison.matrixMap, M2RNNComparison.outerKV,
    Matrix.mul_apply, hrow]
theorems.signature:
∀ (W : RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoMat) (k : RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoVec)
  (H : RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoMat),
  (∀ (j : Fin 2), H 0 j = 0) → ∀ (j : Fin 2), M2RNNComparison.m2rnnCandidate W H k 0 0 j = 0
theorems.proof_body:
fun W k H hrow j =>
  of_eq_true
    (Eq.trans
      (congrArg (fun x => x = 0)
        (Eq.trans
          (congrArg (fun x => Matrix.of x 0 j)
            (funext fun i =>
              funext fun j =>
                congrArg Real.tanh
                  (Eq.trans
                    (congrArg (fun x => (H * W + Matrix.of x) i j)
                      (funext fun i => funext fun j => mul_zero (k i)))
                    (Eq.trans
                      (congrArg (fun x => x + 0) (Fin.sum_univ_two fun i_1 => H i i_1 * W i_1 j))
                      (add_zero (H i 0 * W 0 j + H i 1 * W 1 j))))))
          (Eq.trans
            (congrArg Real.tanh
              (Eq.trans
                (congr
                  (congrArg HAdd.hAdd
                    (Eq.trans (congrArg (fun x => x * W 0 j) ((fun j => hrow j) 0))
                      (zero_mul (W 0 j))))
                  (Eq.trans (congrArg (fun x => x * W 1 j) ((fun j => hrow j) 1))
                    (zero_mul (W 1 j))))
                (add_zero 0)))
            Real.tanh_zero)))
      (eq_self 0))
theorems.name: MultiStepSeparation.m2rnnCandidate_row0_zero_at_zero_row
theorems.doc:
Row 0 of the M2RNN candidate `tanh(H · W + k · vᵀ)` is zero when
row 0 of `H` is zero and either `v = 0` or `k(0) = 0`.

In our witness we use `v = 0`, so the outer product `k · vᵀ` vanishes; the

theorems.source_text:
/-- Row 0 of `m2rnnRowForgetUpdate2` is zero when row 0 of `H` is zero
and `v = 0`. -/
theorem m2rnnRowForgetUpdate2_row0_zero
    (W : TwoMat) (r : TwoVec) (k : TwoVec) (H : TwoMat)
    (hrow : ∀ j, H 0 j = 0) (j : Fin 2) :
    m2rnnRowForgetUpdate2 W r H k (0 : TwoVec) 0 j = 0 := by
  have hcand : M2RNNComparison.m2rnnCandidate W H k (0 : TwoVec) 0 j = 0 :=
    m2rnnCandidate_row0_zero_at_zero_row W k H hrow j
  simp [m2rnnRowForgetUpdate2, rowForgetCarry2, hrow, hcand]
theorems.signature:
∀ (W : RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoMat) (r k : RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoVec)
  (H : RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoMat),
  (∀ (j : Fin 2), H 0 j = 0) →
    ∀ (j : Fin 2), RecurrentResourceFormalism.m2rnnRowForgetUpdate2 W r H k 0 0 j = 0
theorems.proof_body:
fun W r k H hrow j =>
  have hcand := MultiStepSeparation.m2rnnCandidate_row0_zero_at_zero_row W k H hrow j;
  of_eq_true
    (Eq.trans
      (congrArg (fun x => x = 0)
        (Eq.trans
          (congr
            (congrArg HAdd.hAdd
              (Eq.trans (congrArg (HMul.hMul (r 0)) ((fun j => hrow j) j)) (mul_zero (r 0))))
            (Eq.trans (congrArg (HMul.hMul (1 - r 0)) hcand) (mul_zero (1 - r 0))))
          (add_zero 0)))
      (eq_self 0))

theorems.doc:

theorem m2rnnColumnForgetUpdate2_row0_zero

theorems.signature:
∀ (W : RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoMat) (c k : RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoVec)
  (H : RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoMat),
  (∀ (j : Fin 2), H 0 j = 0) →

theorems.proof_body:
fun W c k H hrow j =>
  have hcand := MultiStepSeparation.m2rnnCandidate_row0_zero_at_zero_row W k H hrow j;
  of_eq_true
    (Eq.trans
      (congrArg (fun x => x = 0)
        (Eq.trans
          (congr
            (congrArg HAdd.hAdd
              (Eq.trans (congrArg (HMul.hMul (c j)) ((fun j => hrow j) j)) (mul_zero (c j))))
            (Eq.trans (congrArg (HMul.hMul (1 - c j)) hcand) (mul_zero (1 - c j))))
          (add_zero 0)))

theorem m2rnnCellForgetUpdate2_row0_zero
    (W : TwoMat) (g : TwoMat) (k : TwoVec) (H : TwoMat)
    (hrow : ∀ j, H 0 j = 0) (j : Fin 2) :
    m2rnnCellForgetUpdate2 W g H k (0 : TwoVec) 0 j = 0 := by

theorems.signature:
∀ (W g : RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoMat) (k : RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoVec)
  (H : RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoMat),
  (∀ (j : Fin 2), H 0 j = 0) →
    ∀ (j : Fin 2), RecurrentResourceFormalism.m2rnnCellForgetUpdate2 W g H k 0 0 j = 0
theorems.proof_body:
fun W g k H hrow j =>
  have hcand := MultiStepSeparation.m2rnnCandidate_row0_zero_at_zero_row W k H hrow j;
  of_eq_true
    (Eq.trans
      (congrArg (fun x => x = 0)
        (Eq.trans
          (congr
            (congrArg HAdd.hAdd
              (Eq.trans (congrArg (HMul.hMul (g 0 j)) ((fun j => hrow j) j)) (mul_zero (g 0 j))))
            (Eq.trans (congrArg (HMul.hMul (1 - g 0 j)) hcand) (mul_zero (1 - g 0 j))))
          (add_zero 0)))
      (eq_self 0))
theorems.name: MultiStepSeparation.m2rnnCellForgetUpdate2_row0_zero
theorems.doc:
Row 0 of `m2rnnCellForgetUpdate2` is zero when row 0 of `H` is zero
and `v = 0`.

theorems.source_text:
/-- Generic zero-row preservation: for any fixed-right raw-write external-forget
resource, applying the update with `v = 0` to a state whose row 0 is zero
yields a state whose row 0 is also zero. -/
theorem FixedRightRawExternalForget2_preserves_zero_row
    (resource : FixedRightRawExternalForget2)
    (k : TwoVec) (H : TwoMat)
    (hrow : ∀ j, H 0 j = 0) (j : Fin 2) :
    resource.update H k (0 : TwoVec) 0 j = 0 := by
  cases resource with
  | row W r => exact m2rnnRowForgetUpdate2_row0_zero W r k H hrow j
  | column W c => exact m2rnnColumnForgetUpdate2_row0_zero W c k H hrow j
  | cell W g => exact m2rnnCellForgetUpdate2_row0_zero W g k H hrow j
theorems.signature:
∀ (resource : RecurrentResourceFormalism.FixedRightRawExternalForget2)
  (k : RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoVec) (H : RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoMat),
  (∀ (j : Fin 2), H 0 j = 0) → ∀ (j : Fin 2), resource.update H k 0 0 j = 0
theorems.proof_body:
fun resource k H hrow j =>
  RecurrentResourceFormalism.FixedRightRawExternalForget2.casesOn (motive := fun t =>
    resource = t → resource.update H k 0 0 j = 0) resource
    (fun W r h => Eq.symm h ▸ MultiStepSeparation.m2rnnRowForgetUpdate2_row0_zero W r k H hrow j)
    (fun W c h => Eq.symm h ▸ MultiStepSeparation.m2rnnColumnForgetUpdate2_row0_zero W c k H hrow j)
    (fun W g h => Eq.symm h ▸ MultiStepSeparation.m2rnnCellForgetUpdate2_row0_zero W g k H hrow j)
    (Eq.refl resource)
theorems.name: MultiStepSeparation.FixedRightRawExternalForget2_preserves_zero_row
theorems.doc:
Generic zero-row preservation: for any fixed-right raw-write external-forget
resource, applying the update with `v = 0` to a state whose row 0 is zero
yields a state whose row 0 is also zero.

theorems.source_text:
/-- After two M2RNN-style steps with inputs `(k₁, 0)` and `(0, 0)` starting from
`lowerLeftState`, the entry `(0, 0)` is zero. -/
theorem twoStep_m2rnn_entry_zero
    (resource : FixedRightRawExternalForget2) (k1 : TwoVec) :
    twoStep resource.update lowerLeftState k1 (0 : TwoVec) (0 : TwoVec) (0 : TwoVec) 0 0
      = 0 := by
  have hrow0 : ∀ j, lowerLeftState 0 j = 0 := lowerLeftState_row0
  have hrow1 :
      ∀ j, resource.update lowerLeftState k1 (0 : TwoVec) 0 j = 0 := by
    intro j
    exact FixedRightRawExternalForget2_preserves_zero_row resource k1 lowerLeftState hrow0 j
  -- After step 2 (with k = 0, v = 0), row 0 of update is also zero.
  exact FixedRightRawExternalForget2_preserves_zero_row resource
    (0 : TwoVec) (resource.update lowerLeftState k1 (0 : TwoVec)) hrow1 0
theorems.signature:
∀ (resource : RecurrentResourceFormalism.FixedRightRawExternalForget2)
  (k1 : RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoVec),
  MultiStepSeparation.twoStep resource.update RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState k1 0 0 0 0
      0 =
    0
theorems.proof_body:
fun resource k1 =>
  have hrow0 := MultiStepSeparation.lowerLeftState_row0;
  have hrow1 := fun j =>
    MultiStepSeparation.FixedRightRawExternalForget2_preserves_zero_row resource k1
      RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState hrow0 j;
  MultiStepSeparation.FixedRightRawExternalForget2_preserves_zero_row resource 0
    (resource.update RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState k1 0) hrow1 0

theorems.doc:
After two M2RNN-style steps with inputs `(k₁, 0)` and `(0, 0)` starting from

theorems.source_text:
/-- After one NDM step on `(lowerLeftState, mixedKey, 0)`, the state at entry
`(0, 0)` is `tanh(-1)`. -/
theorem emender_step1_entry_at_witness :
    M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1 lowerLeftState mixedKey (0 : TwoVec) 0 0
      = Real.tanh (-1) := by
  simp [M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded, M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaTransition,
    M2RNNComparison.matrixTanh, M2RNNComparison.matrixMap,
    M2RNNComparison.outerKV, lowerLeftState, mixedKey, Matrix.mul_apply]
theorems.signature:
M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1 RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState
    RecurrentResourceFormalism.mixedKey 0 0 0 =
  Real.tanh (-1)
theorems.proof_body:
of_eq_true
  (Eq.trans
    (congr
      (congrArg Eq
        (Eq.trans
          (congrArg (fun x => Matrix.of x 0 0)
            (funext fun i =>
              funext fun j =>
                congrArg Real.tanh
                  (Eq.trans
                    (congrArg (fun x => x i j)
                      (congr
                        (congrArg
                          (fun x =>
                            HAdd.hAdd
                              (x *
                                Matrix.of fun i j => if i = 1 then if j = 0 then 1 else 0 else 0))
                          (congr (congrArg HSub.hSub (one_smul ℝ 1))
                            (congrArg (⇑Matrix.of) (funext fun i => funext fun j => mul_one 1))))
                        (congrArg (⇑Matrix.of) (funext fun i => funext fun j => mul_zero 1))))
                    (Eq.trans
                      (congrArg (fun x => x + 0)
                        (Eq.trans
                          (Eq.trans
                            (Finset.sum_congr (Eq.refl Finset.univ) fun x a =>
                              Eq.trans (mul_ite (x = 1) (1 i x - 1) (if j = 0 then 1 else 0) 0)
                                (ite_congr (Eq.refl (x = 1))
                                  (fun a =>
                                    Eq.trans (mul_ite (j = 0) (1 i x - 1) 1 0)
                                      (ite_congr (Eq.refl (j = 0)) (fun a => mul_one (1 i x - 1))
                                        fun a => mul_zero (1 i x - 1)))
                                  fun a => mul_zero (1 i x - 1)))
                            (Finset.sum_ite_eq' Finset.univ 1 fun x =>
                              if j = 0 then 1 i x - 1 else 0))
                          (ite_cond_eq_true (if j = 0 then 1 i 1 - 1 else 0) 0
                            (Finset.mem_univ._simp_1 1))))
                      (add_zero (if j = 0 then 1 i 1 - 1 else 0))))))
          (Eq.trans
            (congrArg Real.tanh
              (Eq.trans (ite_cond_eq_true (1 0 1 - 1) 0 (eq_self 0))
                (Eq.trans
                  (congrArg (fun x => x - 1)
                    (Matrix.one_apply_ne
                      (of_eq_true (Eq.trans (congrArg Not zero_ne_one._simp_1) not_false_eq_true))))
                  (zero_sub 1))))
            (Real.tanh_neg 1))))
      (Real.tanh_neg 1))
    (eq_self (-Real.tanh 1)))
theorems.name: MultiStepSeparation.emender_step1_entry_at_witness
theorems.doc:
After one NDM step on `(lowerLeftState, mixedKey, 0)`, the state at entry
`(0, 0)` is `tanh(-1)`.

theorems.source_text:
/-- An NDM step with `k = 0` and `v = 0` reduces to elementwise `tanh`. -/
theorem emender_zero_input_step (H : TwoMat) (i j : Fin 2) :
    M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1 H (0 : TwoVec) (0 : TwoVec) i j
      = Real.tanh (H i j) := by
  simp [M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded, M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaTransition,
    M2RNNComparison.matrixTanh, M2RNNComparison.matrixMap,
    M2RNNComparison.outerKV, Matrix.mul_apply,
    Matrix.one_apply, Finset.sum_ite_eq]
theorems.signature:
∀ (H : RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoMat) (i j : Fin 2),
  M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1 H 0 0 i j = Real.tanh (H i j)
theorems.proof_body:
fun H i j =>
  of_eq_true
    (Eq.trans
      (congrArg (fun x => Matrix.of x i j = Real.tanh (H i j))
        (funext fun i =>
          funext fun j =>
            congrArg Real.tanh
              (Eq.trans
                (congrArg (fun x => x i j)
                  (congr
                    (congrArg (fun x => HAdd.hAdd (x * H))
                      (congr (congrArg HSub.hSub (one_smul ℝ 1))
                        (congrArg (⇑Matrix.of) (funext fun i => funext fun j => mul_zero 0))))
                    (congrArg (⇑Matrix.of) (funext fun i => funext fun j => mul_zero 0))))
                (Eq.trans
                  (congrArg (fun x => x + 0)
                    (Eq.trans
                      (Eq.trans
                        (Finset.sum_congr (Eq.refl Finset.univ) fun x a =>
                          Eq.trans
                            (Eq.trans
                              (congrArg (fun x_1 => x_1 * H x j)
                                (sub_zero (if i = x then 1 else 0)))
                              (ite_mul (i = x) 1 0 (H x j)))
                            (ite_congr (Eq.refl (i = x)) (fun a => one_mul (H x j)) fun a =>
                              zero_mul (H x j)))
                        (Finset.sum_ite_eq Finset.univ i fun x => H x j))
                      (ite_cond_eq_true (H i j) 0 (Finset.mem_univ._simp_1 i))))
                  (add_zero (H i j))))))
      (eq_self (Real.tanh (H i j))))
theorems.name: MultiStepSeparation.emender_zero_input_step
theorems.doc: An NDM step with `k = 0` and `v = 0` reduces to elementwise `tanh`.

theorems.source_text:
/-- After two NDM steps on `(lowerLeftState, mixedKey, 0)` then `(0, 0)`, the
entry `(0, 0)` is `tanh(tanh(-1))`, which is strictly nonzero. -/
theorem emender_twoStep_entry_at_witness :
    twoStep (M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1)
        lowerLeftState mixedKey (0 : TwoVec) (0 : TwoVec) (0 : TwoVec) 0 0
      = Real.tanh (Real.tanh (-1)) := by
  unfold twoStep
  rw [emender_zero_input_step]
  rw [emender_step1_entry_at_witness]
theorems.signature:
MultiStepSeparation.twoStep (M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1)
    RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState RecurrentResourceFormalism.mixedKey 0 0 0 0 0 =
  Real.tanh (Real.tanh (-1))
theorems.proof_body:
id
  (Eq.mpr
    (id
      (congrArg (fun _a => _a = Real.tanh (Real.tanh (-1)))
        (MultiStepSeparation.emender_zero_input_step
          (M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1 RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState
            RecurrentResourceFormalism.mixedKey 0)
          0 0)))
    (Eq.mpr
      (id
        (congrArg (fun _a => Real.tanh _a = Real.tanh (Real.tanh (-1)))
          MultiStepSeparation.emender_step1_entry_at_witness))
      (Eq.refl (Real.tanh (Real.tanh (-1))))))
theorems.name: MultiStepSeparation.emender_twoStep_entry_at_witness
theorems.doc:
After two NDM steps on `(lowerLeftState, mixedKey, 0)` then `(0, 0)`, the
entry `(0, 0)` is `tanh(tanh(-1))`, which is strictly nonzero.

theorems.source_text:
/-- `tanh(tanh(-1))` is nonzero. -/
theorem tanh_tanh_neg_one_ne_zero : Real.tanh (Real.tanh (-1)) ≠ 0 := by
  intro h
  have hinner : Real.tanh (-1) = 0 := by
    have hzero : Real.tanh (Real.tanh (-1)) = Real.tanh 0 := by
      simpa [Real.tanh_zero] using h
    exact Activation.tanh_injective hzero
  exact tanh_neg_one_ne_zero hinner
theorems.signature: Real.tanh (Real.tanh (-1)) ≠ 0
theorems.proof_body:
fun h =>
  have hinner :=
    have hzero :=
      Eq.mpr
        (id
          (Eq.trans
            (congr
              (congrArg Eq
                (Eq.trans (congrArg Real.tanh (Real.tanh_neg 1)) (Real.tanh_neg (Real.tanh 1))))
              Real.tanh_zero)
            inv_eq_one._simp_4))
        (Eq.mp
          (Eq.trans
            (congrArg (fun x => x = 0)
              (Eq.trans (congrArg Real.tanh (Real.tanh_neg 1)) (Real.tanh_neg (Real.tanh 1))))
            inv_eq_one._simp_4)
          h);
    Activation.tanh_injective hzero;
  RecurrentResourceFormalism.tanh_neg_one_ne_zero hinner
theorems.name: MultiStepSeparation.tanh_tanh_neg_one_ne_zero
theorems.doc: `tanh(tanh(-1))` is nonzero.

theorems.source_text:
/-- **Two-step resource separation (Milestone 1).**

NDM and every fixed-right raw-write M2RNN-style resource produce different
states after the two-token input sequence `[(mixedKey, 0), (0, 0)]` applied to
the witness state `lowerLeftState`.

This is the natural composition of the one-step separation. The first step is
the existing one-step witness; the second step injects a "free" elementwise
`tanh` for NDM while M2RNN's row-locality keeps the first row dead. The
distinguishing entry is `(0, 0)`: NDM produces `tanh(tanh(-1)) ≠ 0`, every
fixed-right raw-write resource produces exactly `0`.

This rules out the possibility that the one-step gap is washed out by
composition. The resource separation persists through (at least) two recurrent
steps. -/
theorem emender_m2rnn_two_step_separation :
    twoStep (M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1)
        lowerLeftState mixedKey (0 : TwoVec) (0 : TwoVec) (0 : TwoVec)
      = Matrix.of (fun i j =>
          if i = 0 ∧ j = 0 then Real.tanh (Real.tanh (-1))
          else twoStep (M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1)
                  lowerLeftState mixedKey (0 : TwoVec) (0 : TwoVec) (0 : TwoVec) i j)
    ∧ ∀ resource : FixedRightRawExternalForget2,
        twoStep resource.update
            lowerLeftState mixedKey (0 : TwoVec) (0 : TwoVec) (0 : TwoVec)
          ≠ twoStep (M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1)
              lowerLeftState mixedKey (0 : TwoVec) (0 : TwoVec) (0 : TwoVec) := by
  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
  · ext i j
    by_cases h : i = 0 ∧ j = 0
    · rcases h with ⟨hi, hj⟩
      subst hi; subst hj
      simp [emender_twoStep_entry_at_witness]
    · simp [h]
  · intro resource hEq
    have hR : twoStep resource.update lowerLeftState mixedKey (0 : TwoVec)
        (0 : TwoVec) (0 : TwoVec) 0 0 = 0 :=
      twoStep_m2rnn_entry_zero resource mixedKey
    have hN : twoStep (M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1)
        lowerLeftState mixedKey (0 : TwoVec) (0 : TwoVec) (0 : TwoVec) 0 0
        = Real.tanh (Real.tanh (-1)) :=
      emender_twoStep_entry_at_witness
    have hEqEntry :
        twoStep resource.update lowerLeftState mixedKey (0 : TwoVec)
            (0 : TwoVec) (0 : TwoVec) 0 0 =
          twoStep (M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1)
            lowerLeftState mixedKey (0 : TwoVec) (0 : TwoVec) (0 : TwoVec) 0 0 := by
      exact congrFun (congrFun hEq 0) 0
    rw [hR, hN] at hEqEntry
    exact tanh_tanh_neg_one_ne_zero hEqEntry.symm
theorems.signature:
(MultiStepSeparation.twoStep (M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1)
      RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState RecurrentResourceFormalism.mixedKey 0 0 0 =
    Matrix.of fun i j =>
      if i = 0 ∧ j = 0 then Real.tanh (Real.tanh (-1))
      else
        MultiStepSeparation.twoStep (M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1)
          RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState RecurrentResourceFormalism.mixedKey 0 0 0 i j) ∧
  ∀ (resource : RecurrentResourceFormalism.FixedRightRawExternalForget2),
    MultiStepSeparation.twoStep resource.update RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState
        RecurrentResourceFormalism.mixedKey 0 0 0 ≠
      MultiStepSeparation.twoStep (M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1)
        RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState RecurrentResourceFormalism.mixedKey 0 0 0
theorems.proof_body:
⟨Matrix.ext fun i j =>
    if h : i = 0 ∧ j = 0 then
      And.casesOn h fun hi hj =>
        Eq.symm hi ▸
          Eq.symm hj ▸
            of_eq_true
              (Eq.trans
                (congr
                  (congrArg Eq
                    (Eq.trans MultiStepSeparation.emender_twoStep_entry_at_witness
                      (Eq.trans (congrArg Real.tanh (Real.tanh_neg 1))
                        (Real.tanh_neg (Real.tanh 1)))))
                  (Eq.trans
                    (congrArg (fun x => Matrix.of x 0 0)
                      (funext fun i =>
                        funext fun j =>
                          ite_congr (Eq.refl (i = 0 ∧ j = 0))
                            (fun a =>
                              Eq.trans (congrArg Real.tanh (Real.tanh_neg 1))
                                (Real.tanh_neg (Real.tanh 1)))
                            fun a =>
                            Eq.refl
                              (MultiStepSeparation.twoStep
                                (M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1)
                                RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState
                                RecurrentResourceFormalism.mixedKey 0 0 0 i j)))
                    (ite_cond_eq_true (-Real.tanh (Real.tanh 1))
                      (MultiStepSeparation.twoStep (M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1)
                        RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState
                        RecurrentResourceFormalism.mixedKey 0 0 0 0 0)
                      (Eq.trans (congr (congrArg And (eq_self 0)) (eq_self 0)) (and_self True)))))
                (eq_self (-Real.tanh (Real.tanh 1))))
    else
      of_eq_true
        (Eq.trans
          (congrArg
            (Eq
              (MultiStepSeparation.twoStep (M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1)
                RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState RecurrentResourceFormalism.mixedKey 0 0 0
                i j))
            (Eq.trans
              (congrArg (fun x => Matrix.of x i j)
                (funext fun i =>
                  funext fun j =>
                    ite_congr (Eq.refl (i = 0 ∧ j = 0))
                      (fun a =>
                        Eq.trans (congrArg Real.tanh (Real.tanh_neg 1))
                          (Real.tanh_neg (Real.tanh 1)))
                      fun a =>
                      Eq.refl
                        (MultiStepSeparation.twoStep (M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1)
                          RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState
                          RecurrentResourceFormalism.mixedKey 0 0 0 i j)))
              (ite_cond_eq_false (-Real.tanh (Real.tanh 1))
                (MultiStepSeparation.twoStep (M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1)
                  RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState RecurrentResourceFormalism.mixedKey 0 0
                  0 i j)
                (eq_false h))))
          (eq_self
            (MultiStepSeparation.twoStep (M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1)
              RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState RecurrentResourceFormalism.mixedKey 0 0 0 i
              j))),
  fun resource hEq =>
  have hR :=
    MultiStepSeparation.twoStep_m2rnn_entry_zero resource RecurrentResourceFormalism.mixedKey;
  have hN := MultiStepSeparation.emender_twoStep_entry_at_witness;
  have hEqEntry := congrFun (congrFun hEq 0) 0;
  MultiStepSeparation.tanh_tanh_neg_one_ne_zero
    (Eq.symm
      (Eq.mp (congrArg (fun _a => 0 = _a) hN)
        (Eq.mp
          (congrArg
            (fun _a =>
              _a =
                MultiStepSeparation.twoStep (M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1)
                  RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState RecurrentResourceFormalism.mixedKey 0 0
                  0 0 0)
            hR)
          hEqEntry)))⟩
theorems.name: MultiStepSeparation.emender_m2rnn_two_step_separation
theorems.doc:
**Two-step resource separation (Milestone 1).**

NDM and every fixed-right raw-write M2RNN-style resource produce different
states after the two-token input sequence `[(mixedKey, 0), (0, 0)]` applied to
the witness state `lowerLeftState`.

This is the natural composition of the one-step separation. The first step is
the existing one-step witness; the second step injects a "free" elementwise
`tanh` for NDM while M2RNN's row-locality keeps the first row dead. The
distinguishing entry is `(0, 0)`: NDM produces `tanh(tanh(-1)) ≠ 0`, every
fixed-right raw-write resource produces exactly `0`.

This rules out the possibility that the one-step gap is washed out by
composition. The resource separation persists through (at least) two recurrent
steps.

theorems.source_text:
/-- **Compact form of two-step separation.**

For every fixed-right raw-write M2RNN-style resource, there is a two-token
input sequence on which the composed two-step output differs from NDM's
composed two-step output.

The witnessing sequence is `[(mixedKey, 0), (0, 0)]`, applied to the witness
state `lowerLeftState`. -/
theorem emender_m2rnn_two_step_separation_exists :
    ∀ resource : FixedRightRawExternalForget2,
      ∃ (H0 : TwoMat) (k1 v1 k2 v2 : TwoVec),
        twoStep resource.update H0 k1 v1 k2 v2 ≠
          twoStep (M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1) H0 k1 v1 k2 v2 := by
  intro resource
  refine ⟨lowerLeftState, mixedKey, (0 : TwoVec), (0 : TwoVec), (0 : TwoVec), ?_⟩
  exact (emender_m2rnn_two_step_separation.2 resource)
theorems.signature:
∀ (resource : RecurrentResourceFormalism.FixedRightRawExternalForget2),
  ∃ H0 k1 v1 k2 v2,
    MultiStepSeparation.twoStep resource.update H0 k1 v1 k2 v2 ≠
      MultiStepSeparation.twoStep (M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1) H0 k1 v1 k2 v2
theorems.proof_body:
fun resource =>
  Exists.intro RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState
    (Exists.intro RecurrentResourceFormalism.mixedKey
      (Exists.intro 0
        (Exists.intro 0
          (Exists.intro 0 (MultiStepSeparation.emender_m2rnn_two_step_separation.right resource)))))
theorems.name: MultiStepSeparation.emender_m2rnn_two_step_separation_exists
theorems.doc:
**Compact form of two-step separation.**

For every fixed-right raw-write M2RNN-style resource, there is a two-token
input sequence on which the composed two-step output differs from NDM's
composed two-step output.

The witnessing sequence is `[(mixedKey, 0), (0, 0)]`, applied to the witness
state `lowerLeftState`.

theorems.source_text:
/-- `n`-fold composition of `Real.tanh`. -/
noncomputable def tanhIter : ℕ → ℝ → ℝ
  | 0, x => x
  | n + 1, x => Real.tanh (tanhIter n x)
theorems.signature: ℕ → ℝ → ℝ
theorems.proof_body:
fun x x_1 =>
  Nat.brecOn (motive := fun x => ℝ → ℝ) x
    (fun x f x_2 =>
      (match (motive := (x : ℕ) → ℝ → Nat.below (motive := fun x => ℝ → ℝ) x → ℝ) x, x_2 with
        | 0, x => fun x_3 => x
        | n.succ, x => fun x_3 => Real.tanh (x_3.1 x))
        f)
    x_1
theorems.name: MultiStepSeparation.tanhIter
theorems.doc: `n`-fold composition of `Real.tanh`.

theorems.source_text:
/-- `tanhIter n x` is zero iff `x` is zero (for any `n`). -/
theorem tanhIter_ne_zero_of_ne_zero (n : ℕ) (x : ℝ) (hx : x ≠ 0) :
    tanhIter n x ≠ 0 := by
  induction n with
  | zero => simpa [tanhIter]
  | succ n ih =>
    intro h
    have hzero : Real.tanh (tanhIter n x) = Real.tanh 0 := by
      simpa [tanhIter, Real.tanh_zero] using h
    have hcomp : tanhIter n x = 0 := Activation.tanh_injective hzero
    exact ih hcomp
theorems.signature: ∀ (n : ℕ) (x : ℝ), x ≠ 0 → MultiStepSeparation.tanhIter n x ≠ 0
theorems.proof_body:
fun n x hx =>
  Nat.recAux (id hx)
    (fun n ih h =>
      have hzero :=
        Eq.mpr (id (congrArg (Eq (Real.tanh (MultiStepSeparation.tanhIter n x))) Real.tanh_zero)) h;
      have hcomp := Activation.tanh_injective hzero;
      ih hcomp)
    n

theorems.source_text:
/-- `tanhIter` commutes with prefix application of `tanh`: applying `tanhIter n`
to `tanh a` is the same as applying `tanh` to `tanhIter n a`. -/
theorem tanhIter_tanh_comm (n : ℕ) (a : ℝ) :
    tanhIter n (Real.tanh a) = Real.tanh (tanhIter n a) := by
  induction n with
  | zero => simp [tanhIter]
  | succ n ih => simp [tanhIter, ih]
theorems.signature:
∀ (n : ℕ) (a : ℝ),
  MultiStepSeparation.tanhIter n (Real.tanh a) = Real.tanh (MultiStepSeparation.tanhIter n a)
theorems.proof_body:
fun n a =>
  Nat.recAux (of_eq_true (eq_self (Real.tanh a)))
    (fun n ih =>
      of_eq_true
        (Eq.trans
          (congrArg
            (fun x => Real.tanh x = Real.tanh (Real.tanh (MultiStepSeparation.tanhIter n a))) ih)
          (eq_self (Real.tanh (Real.tanh (MultiStepSeparation.tanhIter n a))))))
    n
theorems.name: MultiStepSeparation.tanhIter_tanh_comm
theorems.doc:
`tanhIter` commutes with prefix application of `tanh`: applying `tanhIter n`
to `tanh a` is the same as applying `tanh` to `tanhIter n a`.

theorems.source_text:
/-- Apply `update` `n` times with inputs `(k₁, v₁)`, `(k₂, v₂)`, …. -/
def iterUpdate (update : OneStepUpdate) (H : TwoMat) :
    List (TwoVec × TwoVec) → TwoMat
  | [] => H
  | (k, v) :: rest => iterUpdate update (update H k v) rest
theorems.signature:
MultiStepSeparation.OneStepUpdate →
  RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoMat →
    List (RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoVec × RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoVec) →
      RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoMat
theorems.proof_body:
fun update H x =>
  List.brecOn (motive := fun x =>
    RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoMat → RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoMat) x
    (fun x f H =>
      (match (motive :=
          (x : List (RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoVec × RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoVec)) →
            List.below (motive := fun x =>
                RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoMat → RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoMat) x →
              RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoMat)
          x with
        | [] => fun x => H
        | (k, v) :: rest => fun x => x.1 (update H k v))
        f)
    H
theorems.name: MultiStepSeparation.iterUpdate
theorems.doc: Apply `update` `n` times with inputs `(k₁, v₁)`, `(k₂, v₂)`, ….

theorems.source_text:
/-- The list of `n` "zero" steps `(0, 0)`. -/
def zeroSteps : ℕ → List (TwoVec × TwoVec)
  | 0 => []
  | n + 1 => ((0 : TwoVec), (0 : TwoVec)) :: zeroSteps n
theorems.signature: ℕ → List (RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoVec × RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoVec)
theorems.proof_body:
fun x =>
  Nat.brecOn x fun x f =>
    (match (motive :=
        (x : ℕ) →
          Nat.below x →
            List (RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoVec × RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoVec))
        x with
      | 0 => fun x => []
      | n.succ => fun x => (0, 0) :: x.1)
      f
theorems.name: MultiStepSeparation.zeroSteps
theorems.doc: The list of `n` "zero" steps `(0, 0)`.

theorems.source_text:
/-- The k-step witness input: one witness step followed by `(k - 1)` zero
steps. Length is exactly `k` for every `k ≥ 1`. -/
def kStepWitnessInputs (k : ℕ) : List (TwoVec × TwoVec) :=
  (mixedKey, (0 : TwoVec)) :: zeroSteps (k - 1)
theorems.signature: ℕ → List (RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoVec × RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoVec)
theorems.proof_body: fun k => (RecurrentResourceFormalism.mixedKey, 0) :: MultiStepSeparation.zeroSteps (k - 1)
theorems.name: MultiStepSeparation.kStepWitnessInputs
theorems.doc:
The k-step witness input: one witness step followed by `(k - 1)` zero
steps. Length is exactly `k` for every `k ≥ 1`.

theorems.source_text:
/-- Iterating any M2RNN-style resource on a list of zero-key/zero-value steps
preserves the zero-row-0 property. -/
theorem iterUpdate_zeroSteps_preserves_row0
    (resource : FixedRightRawExternalForget2)
    (H : TwoMat) (hrow : ∀ j, H 0 j = 0) (n : ℕ) (j : Fin 2) :
    iterUpdate resource.update H (zeroSteps n) 0 j = 0 := by
  induction n generalizing H with
  | zero => simpa [iterUpdate, zeroSteps] using hrow j
  | succ n ih =>
    simp only [zeroSteps, iterUpdate]
    apply ih
    intro j'
    exact FixedRightRawExternalForget2_preserves_zero_row resource
      (0 : TwoVec) H hrow j'
theorems.signature:
∀ (resource : RecurrentResourceFormalism.FixedRightRawExternalForget2)
  (H : RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoMat),
  (∀ (j : Fin 2), H 0 j = 0) →
    ∀ (n : ℕ) (j : Fin 2),
      MultiStepSeparation.iterUpdate resource.update H (MultiStepSeparation.zeroSteps n) 0 j = 0
theorems.proof_body:
fun resource H hrow n j =>
  Nat.recAux (motive := fun n =>
    ∀ (H : RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoMat),
      (∀ (j : Fin 2), H 0 j = 0) →
        MultiStepSeparation.iterUpdate resource.update H (MultiStepSeparation.zeroSteps n) 0 j = 0)
    (fun H hrow => id (hrow j))
    (fun n ih H hrow =>
      id
        (ih (resource.update H 0 0) fun j' =>
          MultiStepSeparation.FixedRightRawExternalForget2_preserves_zero_row resource 0 H hrow j'))
    n H hrow
theorems.name: MultiStepSeparation.iterUpdate_zeroSteps_preserves_row0
theorems.doc:
Iterating any M2RNN-style resource on a list of zero-key/zero-value steps
preserves the zero-row-0 property.

theorems.source_text:
/-- Entry `(0, 0)` of the k-step M2RNN trajectory on the witness state is `0`
for every `k ≥ 1`. -/
theorem iterUpdate_m2rnn_kStep_entry_zero
    (resource : FixedRightRawExternalForget2) (k : ℕ) (hk : 1 ≤ k) :
    iterUpdate resource.update lowerLeftState (kStepWitnessInputs k) 0 0 = 0 := by
  unfold kStepWitnessInputs
  simp only [iterUpdate]
  have hrow1 : ∀ j, resource.update lowerLeftState mixedKey (0 : TwoVec) 0 j = 0 := by
    intro j
    exact FixedRightRawExternalForget2_preserves_zero_row resource
      mixedKey lowerLeftState lowerLeftState_row0 j
  exact iterUpdate_zeroSteps_preserves_row0 resource
    (resource.update lowerLeftState mixedKey (0 : TwoVec)) hrow1 (k - 1) 0
theorems.signature:
∀ (resource : RecurrentResourceFormalism.FixedRightRawExternalForget2) (k : ℕ),
  1 ≤ k →
    MultiStepSeparation.iterUpdate resource.update RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState
        (MultiStepSeparation.kStepWitnessInputs k) 0 0 =
      0
theorems.proof_body:
fun resource k hk =>
  id
    (id
      (have hrow1 := fun j =>
        MultiStepSeparation.FixedRightRawExternalForget2_preserves_zero_row resource
          RecurrentResourceFormalism.mixedKey RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState
          MultiStepSeparation.lowerLeftState_row0 j;
      MultiStepSeparation.iterUpdate_zeroSteps_preserves_row0 resource
        (resource.update RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState
          RecurrentResourceFormalism.mixedKey 0)
        hrow1 (k - 1) 0))
theorems.name: MultiStepSeparation.iterUpdate_m2rnn_kStep_entry_zero
theorems.doc:
Entry `(0, 0)` of the k-step M2RNN trajectory on the witness state is `0`
for every `k ≥ 1`.

theorems.source_text:
/-- An NDM step with `(k = 0, v = 0)` applied to a matrix whose row 0 is
`(a, 0)` and rows ≥ 1 are zero produces a matrix whose row 0 is `(tanh a, 0)`
and rows ≥ 1 are zero. -/
theorem emender_zero_step_on_clean_state
    (a : ℝ) (i j : Fin 2) :
    M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1
        (Matrix.of (fun (r c : Fin 2) =>
          if r = 0 ∧ c = 0 then a else 0))
        (0 : TwoVec) (0 : TwoVec) i j
      = (if i = 0 ∧ j = 0 then Real.tanh a else 0) := by
  fin_cases i <;> fin_cases j <;>
    simp [M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded, M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaTransition,
      M2RNNComparison.matrixTanh, M2RNNComparison.matrixMap, M2RNNComparison.outerKV,
      Matrix.mul_apply, Matrix.one_apply,
      Matrix.of_apply, Real.tanh_zero]
theorems.signature:
∀ (a : ℝ) (i j : Fin 2),
  M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1 (Matrix.of fun r c => if r = 0 ∧ c = 0 then a else 0) 0 0
      i j =
    if i = 0 ∧ j = 0 then Real.tanh a else 0
theorems.proof_body:
fun a i j =>
  List.Mem.casesOn (motive := fun a_1 x =>
    List.finRange 2 = a_1 →
      Fintype.complete i ≍ x →
        M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1
            (Matrix.of fun r c => if r = 0 ∧ c = 0 then a else 0) 0 0 i j =
          if i = 0 ∧ j = 0 then Real.tanh a else 0)
    (Fintype.complete i)
    (fun as h =>
      List.cons.noConfusion
        (Fintype.complete i ≍ List.Mem.head as →
          M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1
              (Matrix.of fun r c => if r = 0 ∧ c = 0 then a else 0) 0 0 i j =
            if i = 0 ∧ j = 0 then Real.tanh a else 0)
        ((fun i => i) ⟨0, Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_refl 2)⟩)
        ((fun x1 x2 => (fun i => i) x1 :: x2) ⟨1, Nat.le_refl 2⟩ []) i as h fun head_eq =>
        Eq.ndrec (motive := fun i =>
          ∀ (this : i ∈ Fintype.elems),
            (fun x1 x2 => (fun i => i) x1 :: x2) ⟨1, Nat.le_refl 2⟩ [] = as →
              this ≍ List.Mem.head as →
                M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1
                    (Matrix.of fun r c => if r = 0 ∧ c = 0 then a else 0) 0 0 i j =
                  if i = 0 ∧ j = 0 then Real.tanh a else 0)
          (fun this tail_eq =>
            Eq.ndrec (motive := fun as =>
              this ≍ List.Mem.head as →
                M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1
                    (Matrix.of fun r c => if r = 0 ∧ c = 0 then a else 0) 0 0
                    ((fun i => i) ⟨0, Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_refl 2)⟩) j =
                  if (fun i => i) ⟨0, Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_refl 2)⟩ = 0 ∧ j = 0 then Real.tanh a
                  else 0)
              (fun h =>
                List.Mem.casesOn (motive := fun a_1 x =>
                  List.finRange 2 = a_1 →
                    Fintype.complete j ≍ x →
                      M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1
                          (Matrix.of fun r c => if r = 0 ∧ c = 0 then a else 0) 0 0
                          ((fun i => i) ⟨0, Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_refl 2)⟩) j =
                        if (fun i => i) ⟨0, Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_refl 2)⟩ = 0 ∧ j = 0 then

                        else 0)

                      (Fintype.complete j ≍ List.Mem.head as →

                      fun head_eq =>
                      Eq.ndrec (motive := fun j =>
                        ∀ (this : j ∈ Fintype.elems),
                          (fun x1 x2 => (fun i => i) x1 :: x2) ⟨1, Nat.le_refl 2⟩ [] = as →
                            this ≍ List.Mem.head as →
                              M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1
                                  (Matrix.of fun r c => if r = 0 ∧ c = 0 then a else 0) 0 0
                                  ((fun i => i) ⟨0, Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_refl 2)⟩) j =
                                if (fun i => i) ⟨0, Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_refl 2)⟩ = 0 ∧ j = 0 then
                                  Real.tanh a
                                else 0)
                        (fun this tail_eq =>
                          Eq.ndrec (motive := fun as =>
                            this ≍ List.Mem.head as →
                              M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1
                                  (Matrix.of fun r c => if r = 0 ∧ c = 0 then a else 0) 0 0
                                  ((fun i => i) ⟨0, Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_refl 2)⟩)
                                  ((fun i => i) ⟨0, Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_refl 2)⟩) =
                                if
                                    (fun i => i) ⟨0, Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_refl 2)⟩ = 0 ∧
                                      (fun i => i) ⟨0, Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_refl 2)⟩ = 0 then
                                  Real.tanh a
                                else 0)
                            (fun h =>
                              of_eq_true
                                (Eq.trans
                                  (congr
                                    (congrArg Eq
                                      (Eq.trans
                                        (congrArg (fun x => Matrix.of x 0 0)
                                          (funext fun i =>
                                            funext fun j =>
                                              congrArg Real.tanh
                                                (Eq.trans
                                                  (congrArg (fun x => x i j)

                                                                (Eq.trans

                                                                  (true_and (j = 0)))

                                        (congrArg Real.tanh
                                          (Eq.trans

                                    (ite_cond_eq_true (Real.tanh a) 0

                        head_eq (Fintype.complete j))
                  (fun b {as} a_1 h =>
                    List.cons.noConfusion
                      (Fintype.complete j ≍ List.Mem.tail b a_1 →
                        M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1

                                                                          fun x a_4 =>

                                                                                  x_1 *

theorems.name: MultiStepSeparation.emender_zero_step_on_clean_state
theorems.doc:
An NDM step with `(k = 0, v = 0)` applied to a matrix whose row 0 is
`(a, 0)` and rows ≥ 1 are zero produces a matrix whose row 0 is `(tanh a, 0)`
and rows ≥ 1 are zero.

theorems.source_text:
/-- After one NDM step on the witness, the state at position `(i, j)` equals
`tanh(-1)` when `(i, j) = (0, 0)` and `0` otherwise. -/
theorem emender_step1_clean_form (i j : Fin 2) :
    M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1 lowerLeftState mixedKey (0 : TwoVec) i j
      = (if i = 0 ∧ j = 0 then Real.tanh (-1) else 0) := by
  fin_cases i <;> fin_cases j <;>
    simp [M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded, M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaTransition,
      M2RNNComparison.matrixTanh, M2RNNComparison.matrixMap,
      M2RNNComparison.outerKV, lowerLeftState, mixedKey, Matrix.mul_apply,
      Matrix.one_apply]
theorems.signature:
∀ (i j : Fin 2),
  M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1 RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState
      RecurrentResourceFormalism.mixedKey 0 i j =
    if i = 0 ∧ j = 0 then Real.tanh (-1) else 0

                                RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState
                                RecurrentResourceFormalism.mixedKey 0 i j =
                              if i = 0 ∧ j = 0 then Real.tanh (-1) else 0)
                        (fun tail_eq =>
                          Eq.ndrec (motive := fun {as} =>
                            ∀ (a_2 : List.Mem i as),
                              a ≍ List.Mem.tail ((fun i => i) ⟨1, Nat.le_refl 2⟩) a_2 →
                                M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1
                                    RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState
                                    RecurrentResourceFormalism.mixedKey 0 i j =
                                  if i = 0 ∧ j = 0 then Real.tanh (-1) else 0)
                            (fun a_2 h =>
                              List.Mem.casesOn (motive := fun a x =>
                                [] = a →
                                  a_2 ≍ x →
                                    M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1
                                        RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState
                                        RecurrentResourceFormalism.mixedKey 0 i j =
                                      if i = 0 ∧ j = 0 then Real.tanh (-1) else 0)
                                a_2 (fun as h => False.elim (noConfusion_of_Nat List.ctorIdx h))
                                (fun b {as} a h => False.elim (noConfusion_of_Nat List.ctorIdx h))
                                (Eq.refl []) (HEq.refl a_2))
                            tail_eq a_1)
                        head_eq)
                  (Eq.refl ((fun x1 x2 => (fun i => i) x1 :: x2) ⟨1, Nat.le_refl 2⟩ []))
                  (HEq.refl a))
              tail_eq a)
          head_eq)
    (Eq.refl (List.finRange 2)) (HEq.refl (Fintype.complete i))
theorems.name: MultiStepSeparation.emender_step1_clean_form
theorems.doc:
After one NDM step on the witness, the state at position `(i, j)` equals
`tanh(-1)` when `(i, j) = (0, 0)` and `0` otherwise.

theorems.source_text:
/-- After one NDM step on the witness, the full state matrix equals the
"clean" form: `(0, 0)` entry is `tanh(-1)`, all others zero. -/
theorem emender_step1_clean_matrix :
    M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1 lowerLeftState mixedKey (0 : TwoVec)
      = Matrix.of (fun (r c : Fin 2) =>
          if r = 0 ∧ c = 0 then Real.tanh (-1) else 0) := by
  ext i j
  rw [emender_step1_clean_form]
  by_cases h : i = 0 ∧ j = 0 <;> simp [h, Matrix.of_apply]
theorems.signature:
M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1 RecurrentResourceFormalism.lowerLeftState
    RecurrentResourceFormalism.mixedKey 0 =
  Matrix.of fun r c => if r = 0 ∧ c = 0 then Real.tanh (-1) else 0
theorems.proof_body:
Matrix.ext fun i j =>
  Eq.mpr
    (id
      (congrArg
        (fun _a => _a = Matrix.of (fun r c => if r = 0 ∧ c = 0 then Real.tanh (-1) else 0) i j)
        (MultiStepSeparation.emender_step1_clean_form i j)))
    (if h : i = 0 ∧ j = 0 then
      of_eq_true
        (Eq.trans
          (congr
            (congrArg Eq
              (Eq.trans
                (ite_cond_eq_true (Real.tanh (-1)) 0
                  (Eq.trans
                    (congr (congrArg And (Eq.trans (congrArg (fun x => x = 0) h.1) (eq_self 0)))
                      (Eq.trans (congrArg (fun x => x = 0) h.2) (eq_self 0)))
                    (and_self True)))
                (Real.tanh_neg 1)))
            (Eq.trans
              (congr
                (congr
                  (congrArg (⇑Matrix.of)
                    (funext fun r =>
                      funext fun c =>
                        ite_congr (Eq.refl (r = 0 ∧ c = 0)) (fun a => Real.tanh_neg 1) fun a =>
                          Eq.refl 0))
                  h.1)
                h.2)
              (ite_cond_eq_true (-Real.tanh 1) 0
                (Eq.trans (congr (congrArg And (eq_self 0)) (eq_self 0)) (and_self True)))))
          (eq_self (-Real.tanh 1)))
    else
      of_eq_true
        (Eq.trans
          (congr (congrArg Eq (ite_cond_eq_false (Real.tanh (-1)) 0 (eq_false h)))
            (Eq.trans
              (congrArg (fun x => Matrix.of x i j)
                (funext fun r =>
                  funext fun c =>
                    ite_congr (Eq.refl (r = 0 ∧ c = 0)) (fun a => Real.tanh_neg 1) fun a =>
                      Eq.refl 0))
              (ite_cond_eq_false (-Real.tanh 1) 0 (eq_false h))))
          (eq_self 0)))
theorems.name: MultiStepSeparation.emender_step1_clean_matrix

After one NDM step on the witness, the full state matrix equals the
