theorems.name: OnlineMemory.stateIndependentAdditiveWrite_cannot_exact_overwrite_two_memories
theorems.doc:
More generally, no state-independent additive write can overwrite two old
memories that disagree at the addressed key in one step.

This is the resource-separation hook: to implement overwrite uniformly, the
write term must either depend on the old state, add extra machinery, or use
additional steps/resources to infer and cancel the old content.

theorems.source_text:
/-- Unit-key delta write exactly overwrites the addressed readout. -/
theorem linearDeltaWrite_exact_overwrite
    (S : Memory K V) (k : KeyVec K) (v : ValueVec V)
    (hk : keyDot k k = 1) :
    read (linearDeltaWrite S k v) k = v := by
  rw [read_linearDeltaWrite]
  ext j
  simp [correction, hk]
theorems.signature:
∀ {K V : ℕ} (S : OnlineMemory.Memory K V) (k : OnlineMemory.KeyVec K) (v : OnlineMemory.ValueVec V),
  OnlineMemory.keyDot k k = 1 → OnlineMemory.read (OnlineMemory.linearDeltaWrite S k v) k = v
theorems.proof_body:
fun {K V} S k v hk =>
  Eq.mpr (id (congrArg (fun _a => _a = v) (OnlineMemory.read_linearDeltaWrite S k k v)))
    (funext fun j =>
      of_eq_true
        (Eq.trans
          (congrArg (fun x => x = v j)
            (Eq.trans
              (congrArg (fun x => (OnlineMemory.read S k + x) j)
                (funext fun j =>
                  Eq.trans (congrArg (fun x => x * (v j - OnlineMemory.read S k j)) hk)
                    (one_mul (v j - OnlineMemory.read S k j))))
              (add_sub_cancel (OnlineMemory.read S k j) (v j))))
          (eq_self (v j))))
theorems.name: OnlineMemory.linearDeltaWrite_exact_overwrite
theorems.doc: Unit-key delta write exactly overwrites the addressed readout.

theorems.source_text:
/-- Orthogonal queries are preserved by a delta write. -/
theorem linearDeltaWrite_preserves_orthogonal_query
    (S : Memory K V) (k q : KeyVec K) (v : ValueVec V)
    (horth : keyDot k q = 0) :
    read (linearDeltaWrite S k v) q = read S q := by
  rw [read_linearDeltaWrite]
  ext j
  simp [horth]
theorems.signature:
∀ {K V : ℕ} (S : OnlineMemory.Memory K V) (k q : OnlineMemory.KeyVec K)
  (v : OnlineMemory.ValueVec V),
  OnlineMemory.keyDot k q = 0 →
    OnlineMemory.read (OnlineMemory.linearDeltaWrite S k v) q = OnlineMemory.read S q
theorems.proof_body:
fun {K V} S k q v horth =>
  Eq.mpr
    (id
      (congrArg (fun _a => _a = OnlineMemory.read S q)
        (OnlineMemory.read_linearDeltaWrite S k q v)))
    (funext fun j =>
      of_eq_true
        (Eq.trans
          (congrArg (fun x => x = OnlineMemory.read S q j)
            (Eq.trans
              (congrArg (fun x => (OnlineMemory.read S q + x) j)
                (funext fun j =>
                  Eq.trans (congrArg (fun x => x * OnlineMemory.correction S k v j) horth)
                    (zero_mul (OnlineMemory.correction S k v j))))
              (add_zero (OnlineMemory.read S q j))))
          (eq_self (OnlineMemory.read S q j))))
theorems.name: OnlineMemory.linearDeltaWrite_preserves_orthogonal_query
theorems.doc: Orthogonal queries are preserved by a delta write.

theorems.source_text:
/-- In the idealized no-decay, unit-write setting, GDN and E88/NDM share the
same delta-correcting pre-nonlinearity. -/
theorem gdn_and_emender_share_ideal_delta_write
    (S : Memory K V) (k : KeyVec K) (v : ValueVec V) :
    gdnIdealWrite S k v = emenderPreTanhWrite S k v := by
  rfl
theorems.signature:
∀ {K V : ℕ} (S : OnlineMemory.Memory K V) (k : OnlineMemory.KeyVec K) (v : OnlineMemory.ValueVec V),
  OnlineMemory.gdnIdealWrite S k v = OnlineMemory.emenderPreTanhWrite S k v
theorems.proof_body: fun {K V} S k v => Eq.refl (OnlineMemory.gdnIdealWrite S k v)
theorems.name: OnlineMemory.gdn_and_emender_share_ideal_delta_write
theorems.doc:
In the idealized no-decay, unit-write setting, GDN and E88/NDM share the
same delta-correcting pre-nonlinearity.

theorems.source_text:
/-- The ideal overwrite theorem applies to both GDN's delta core and E88/NDM's
pre-tanh delta core. -/
theorem shared_delta_core_exact_overwrite
    (S : Memory K V) (k : KeyVec K) (v : ValueVec V)
    (hk : keyDot k k = 1) :
    read (gdnIdealWrite S k v) k = v ∧
    read (emenderPreTanhWrite S k v) k = v := by
  constructor <;> exact linearDeltaWrite_exact_overwrite S k v hk
theorems.signature:
∀ {K V : ℕ} (S : OnlineMemory.Memory K V) (k : OnlineMemory.KeyVec K) (v : OnlineMemory.ValueVec V),
  OnlineMemory.keyDot k k = 1 →
    OnlineMemory.read (OnlineMemory.gdnIdealWrite S k v) k = v ∧
      OnlineMemory.read (OnlineMemory.emenderPreTanhWrite S k v) k = v
theorems.proof_body:
fun {K V} S k v hk =>
  ⟨OnlineMemory.linearDeltaWrite_exact_overwrite S k v hk,
    OnlineMemory.linearDeltaWrite_exact_overwrite S k v hk⟩
theorems.name: OnlineMemory.shared_delta_core_exact_overwrite
theorems.doc:
The ideal overwrite theorem applies to both GDN's delta core and E88/NDM's
pre-tanh delta core.

theorems.source_text:
/-- A finite family of keys is orthonormal when self-overlap is one and
cross-overlap is zero. -/
def OrthonormalKeys {N : Nat} (keys : Fin N → KeyVec K) : Prop :=
  ∀ i j, keyDot (keys i) (keys j) = if i = j then 1 else 0
theorems.signature: {K N : ℕ} → (Fin N → OnlineMemory.KeyVec K) → Prop
theorems.proof_body: fun {K N} keys => ∀ (i j : Fin N), OnlineMemory.keyDot (keys i) (keys j) = if i = j then 1 else 0
theorems.name: OnlineMemory.OrthonormalKeys
theorems.doc:
A finite family of keys is orthonormal when self-overlap is one and
cross-overlap is zero.

theorems.source_text:
/-- The explicit associative-memory table for a finite family of key/value
pairs. -/
def memoryTable {N : Nat}
    (keys : Fin N → KeyVec K) (values : Fin N → ValueVec V) : Memory K V :=
  Finset.univ.sum fun i => M2RNNComparison.outerKV (keys i) (values i)
theorems.signature:
{K V N : ℕ} →
  (Fin N → OnlineMemory.KeyVec K) → (Fin N → OnlineMemory.ValueVec V) → OnlineMemory.Memory K V
theorems.proof_body: fun {K V N} keys values => ∑ i, M2RNNComparison.outerKV (keys i) (values i)
theorems.name: OnlineMemory.memoryTable
theorems.doc:
The explicit associative-memory table for a finite family of key/value
pairs.

theorems.source_text:
/-- Orthonormal keys support exact finite-table retrieval. This is the capacity
upper bound shared by ideal GDN-style delta memory and E88/NDM's pre-tanh delta
core: `N` orthogonal keys can store `N` independent value vectors in a matrix
state. -/
theorem memoryTable_retrieves_orthonormal {N : Nat}
    (keys : Fin N → KeyVec K) (values : Fin N → ValueVec V)
    (horth : OrthonormalKeys keys) :
    ∀ j, read (memoryTable keys values) (keys j) = values j := by
  intro j
  ext dim
  simp [memoryTable, read, M2RNNComparison.queryReadout,
    M2RNNComparison.outerKV, Matrix.sum_apply, Matrix.of_apply,
    Matrix.mulVec, dotProduct]
  calc
    Finset.univ.sum
        (fun x : Fin K => (Finset.univ.sum fun i : Fin N =>
          keys i x * values i dim) * keys j x)
        =
      Finset.univ.sum
        (fun x : Fin K => Finset.univ.sum fun i : Fin N =>
          values i dim * keys i x * keys j x) := by
          apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
          intro x _
          rw [Finset.sum_mul]
          apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
          intro i _
          ring
    _ =
      Finset.univ.sum
        (fun i : Fin N => Finset.univ.sum fun x : Fin K =>
          values i dim * keys i x * keys j x) := by
          rw [Finset.sum_comm]
    _ =
      Finset.univ.sum
        (fun i : Fin N => values i dim *
          Finset.univ.sum (fun x : Fin K => keys i x * keys j x)) := by
          apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
          intro i _
          rw [Finset.mul_sum]
          apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
          intro x _
          ring
    _ =
      Finset.univ.sum
        (fun i : Fin N => values i dim * keyDot (keys i) (keys j)) := by
          rfl
    _ =
      Finset.univ.sum
        (fun i : Fin N => values i dim * (if i = j then 1 else 0)) := by
          apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
          intro i _
          rw [horth i j]
    _ = values j dim := by
          rw [Fintype.sum_eq_single j]
          · simp
          · intro i hi
            simp [hi]
theorems.signature:
∀ {K V N : ℕ} (keys : Fin N → OnlineMemory.KeyVec K) (values : Fin N → OnlineMemory.ValueVec V),
  OnlineMemory.OrthonormalKeys keys →
    ∀ (j : Fin N), OnlineMemory.read (OnlineMemory.memoryTable keys values) (keys j) = values j
theorems.proof_body:
fun {K V N} keys values horth j =>
  funext fun dim =>
    Eq.mpr
      (id
        (congrArg (fun x => x = values j dim)
          (Finset.sum_congr (Eq.refl Finset.univ) fun x a =>
            congrArg (fun x_1 => x_1 * keys j x)
              (Eq.trans
                (congrArg (fun x_1 => x_1.transpose dim x)
                  (Finset.sum_congr (Eq.refl Finset.univ) fun x a =>
                    Eq.refl (Matrix.of fun i j => keys x i * values x j)))
                (Eq.trans
                  (Matrix.sum_apply x dim Finset.univ fun c =>
                    Matrix.of fun i j => keys c i * values c j)
                  (Finset.sum_congr (Eq.refl Finset.univ) fun x_1 a =>
                    Eq.refl (keys x_1 x * values x_1 dim)))))))
      (Trans.trans
        (Trans.trans
          (Trans.trans
            (Trans.trans
              (Trans.trans
                (Finset.sum_congr rfl fun x a =>
                  Eq.mpr
                    (id
                      (congrArg (fun _a => _a = ∑ i, values i dim * keys i x * keys j x)
                        (Finset.sum_mul Finset.univ (fun i => keys i x * values i dim) (keys j x))))
                    (Finset.sum_congr rfl fun i a =>
                      Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.of_eq
                        (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_congr
                          (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_congr (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.atom_pf (keys i x))
                            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.atom_pf (values i dim))
                            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_mul
                              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_add
                                (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_pf_left (keys i x) (Nat.rawCast 1)
                                  (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_pf_right (values i dim) (Nat.rawCast 1)
                                    (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.one_mul (Nat.rawCast 1))))
                                (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_zero
                                  (keys i x ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1))
                                (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_pf_add_zero
                                  (keys i x ^ Nat.rawCast 1 *
                                      (values i dim ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1) +
                                    0)))
                              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.zero_mul
                                (values i dim ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1 + 0))
                              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_pf_add_zero
                                (keys i x ^ Nat.rawCast 1 *
                                    (values i dim ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1) +
                                  0))))
                          (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.atom_pf (keys j x))
                          (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_mul
                            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_add
                              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_pf_left (keys i x) (Nat.rawCast 1)
                                (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_pf_left (values i dim) (Nat.rawCast 1)
                                  (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_pf_right (keys j x) (Nat.rawCast 1)
                                    (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.one_mul (Nat.rawCast 1)))))
                              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_zero
                                (keys i x ^ Nat.rawCast 1 *
                                  (values i dim ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1)))
                              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_pf_add_zero
                                (keys i x ^ Nat.rawCast 1 *
                                    (values i dim ^ Nat.rawCast 1 *
                                      (keys j x ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1)) +
                                  0)))
                            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.zero_mul
                              (keys j x ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1 + 0))
                            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_pf_add_zero
                              (keys i x ^ Nat.rawCast 1 *
                                  (values i dim ^ Nat.rawCast 1 *
                                    (keys j x ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1)) +
                                0))))
                        (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_congr
                          (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_congr
                            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.atom_pf (values i dim))
                            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.atom_pf (keys i x))
                            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_mul
                              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_add
                                (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_pf_right (keys i x) (Nat.rawCast 1)
                                  (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_pf_left (values i dim) (Nat.rawCast 1)
                                    (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.one_mul (Nat.rawCast 1))))
                                (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_zero
                                  (values i dim ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1))
                                (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_pf_add_zero
                                  (keys i x ^ Nat.rawCast 1 *
                                      (values i dim ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1) +
                                    0)))
                              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.zero_mul
                                (keys i x ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1 + 0))
                              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_pf_add_zero
                                (keys i x ^ Nat.rawCast 1 *
                                    (values i dim ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1) +
                                  0))))
                          (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.atom_pf (keys j x))
                          (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_mul
                            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_add
                              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_pf_left (keys i x) (Nat.rawCast 1)
                                (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_pf_left (values i dim) (Nat.rawCast 1)
                                  (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_pf_right (keys j x) (Nat.rawCast 1)
                                    (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.one_mul (Nat.rawCast 1)))))
                              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_zero
                                (keys i x ^ Nat.rawCast 1 *
                                  (values i dim ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1)))
                              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_pf_add_zero
                                (keys i x ^ Nat.rawCast 1 *
                                    (values i dim ^ Nat.rawCast 1 *
                                      (keys j x ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1)) +
                                  0)))
                            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.zero_mul
                              (keys j x ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1 + 0))
                            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_pf_add_zero
                              (keys i x ^ Nat.rawCast 1 *
                                  (values i dim ^ Nat.rawCast 1 *
                                    (keys j x ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1)) +
                                0))))))
                (Eq.mpr
                  (id
                    (congrArg (fun _a => _a = ∑ i, ∑ x, values i dim * keys i x * keys j x)
                      Finset.sum_comm))
                  (Eq.refl (∑ y, ∑ x, values y dim * keys y x * keys j x))))
              (Finset.sum_congr rfl fun i a =>
                Eq.mpr
                  (id
                    (congrArg (fun _a => ∑ x, values i dim * keys i x * keys j x = _a)
                      (Finset.mul_sum Finset.univ (fun x => keys i x * keys j x) (values i dim))))
                  (Finset.sum_congr rfl fun x a =>
                    Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.of_eq
                      (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_congr
                        (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_congr (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.atom_pf (values i dim))
                          (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.atom_pf (keys i x))
                          (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_mul
                            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_add
                              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_pf_left (values i dim) (Nat.rawCast 1)
                                (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_pf_right (keys i x) (Nat.rawCast 1)
                                  (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.one_mul (Nat.rawCast 1))))
                              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_zero
                                (values i dim ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1))
                              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_pf_add_zero
                                (values i dim ^ Nat.rawCast 1 *
                                    (keys i x ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1) +
                                  0)))
                            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.zero_mul
                              (keys i x ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1 + 0))
                            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_pf_add_zero
                              (values i dim ^ Nat.rawCast 1 *
                                  (keys i x ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1) +
                                0))))
                        (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.atom_pf (keys j x))
                        (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_mul
                          (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_add
                            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_pf_left (values i dim) (Nat.rawCast 1)
                              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_pf_left (keys i x) (Nat.rawCast 1)
                                (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_pf_right (keys j x) (Nat.rawCast 1)
                                  (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.one_mul (Nat.rawCast 1)))))
                            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_zero
                              (values i dim ^ Nat.rawCast 1 *
                                (keys i x ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1)))
                            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_pf_add_zero
                              (values i dim ^ Nat.rawCast 1 *
                                  (keys i x ^ Nat.rawCast 1 *
                                    (keys j x ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1)) +
                                0)))
                          (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.zero_mul
                            (keys j x ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1 + 0))
                          (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_pf_add_zero
                            (values i dim ^ Nat.rawCast 1 *
                                (keys i x ^ Nat.rawCast 1 *
                                  (keys j x ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1)) +
                              0))))
                      (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_congr (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.atom_pf (values i dim))
                        (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_congr (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.atom_pf (keys i x))
                          (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.atom_pf (keys j x))
                          (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_mul
                            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_add
                              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_pf_left (keys i x) (Nat.rawCast 1)
                                (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_pf_right (keys j x) (Nat.rawCast 1)
                                  (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.one_mul (Nat.rawCast 1))))
                              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_zero
                                (keys i x ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1))
                              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_pf_add_zero
                                (keys i x ^ Nat.rawCast 1 *
                                    (keys j x ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1) +
                                  0)))
                            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.zero_mul
                              (keys j x ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1 + 0))
                            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_pf_add_zero
                              (keys i x ^ Nat.rawCast 1 *
                                  (keys j x ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1) +
                                0))))
                        (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_mul
                          (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_add
                            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_pf_left (values i dim) (Nat.rawCast 1)
                              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_pf_right (keys i x) (Nat.rawCast 1)
                                (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_pf_right (keys j x) (Nat.rawCast 1)
                                  (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.one_mul (Nat.rawCast 1)))))
                            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_zero
                              (values i dim ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1))
                            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_pf_add_zero
                              (values i dim ^ Nat.rawCast 1 *
                                  (keys i x ^ Nat.rawCast 1 *
                                    (keys j x ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1)) +
                                0)))
                          (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.zero_mul
                            (keys i x ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * (keys j x ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1) +
                              0))
                          (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_pf_add_zero
                            (values i dim ^ Nat.rawCast 1 *
                                (keys i x ^ Nat.rawCast 1 *
                                  (keys j x ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1)) +
                              0)))))))
            (Eq.refl (∑ i, values i dim * ∑ x, keys i x * keys j x)))
          (Finset.sum_congr rfl fun i a =>
            Eq.mpr
              (id
                (congrArg (fun _a => values i dim * _a = values i dim * if i = j then 1 else 0)
                  (horth i j)))
              (Eq.refl (values i dim * if i = j then 1 else 0))))
        (Eq.mpr
          (id
            (congrArg (fun _a => _a = values j dim)
              (Fintype.sum_eq_single j fun i hi =>
                of_eq_true
                  (Eq.trans
                    (congrArg (fun x => x = 0)
                      (Eq.trans
                        (congrArg (HMul.hMul (values i dim)) (ite_cond_eq_false 1 0 (eq_false hi)))
                        (mul_zero (values i dim))))
                    (eq_self 0)))))
          (of_eq_true
            (Eq.trans
              (congrArg (fun x => x = values j dim)
                (Eq.trans (congrArg (HMul.hMul (values j dim)) (ite_cond_eq_true 1 0 (eq_self j)))
                  (mul_one (values j dim))))
              (eq_self (values j dim))))))
theorems.name: OnlineMemory.memoryTable_retrieves_orthonormal
theorems.doc:
Orthonormal keys support exact finite-table retrieval. This is the capacity
upper bound shared by ideal GDN-style delta memory and E88/NDM's pre-tanh delta
core: `N` orthogonal keys can store `N` independent value vectors in a matrix
state.

theorems.source_text:
/-- Online extension step: if `S` already stores an orthogonal family of keys,
a delta write to a new orthogonal unit key stores the new value and preserves
all existing readouts. This is the induction step behind online associative
memory. -/
theorem linearDeltaWrite_extends_orthogonal_readouts {N : Nat}
    (S : Memory K V) (keys : Fin N → KeyVec K) (values : Fin N → ValueVec V)
    (newKey : KeyVec K) (newValue : ValueVec V)
    (hunit : keyDot newKey newKey = 1)
    (horth : ∀ i, keyDot newKey (keys i) = 0)
    (hstored : ∀ i, read S (keys i) = values i) :
    read (linearDeltaWrite S newKey newValue) newKey = newValue ∧
    ∀ i, read (linearDeltaWrite S newKey newValue) (keys i) = values i := by
  constructor
  · exact linearDeltaWrite_exact_overwrite S newKey newValue hunit
  · intro i
    rw [linearDeltaWrite_preserves_orthogonal_query S newKey (keys i) newValue (horth i)]
    exact hstored i
theorems.signature:
∀ {K V N : ℕ} (S : OnlineMemory.Memory K V) (keys : Fin N → OnlineMemory.KeyVec K)
  (values : Fin N → OnlineMemory.ValueVec V) (newKey : OnlineMemory.KeyVec K)
  (newValue : OnlineMemory.ValueVec V),
  OnlineMemory.keyDot newKey newKey = 1 →
    (∀ (i : Fin N), OnlineMemory.keyDot newKey (keys i) = 0) →
      (∀ (i : Fin N), OnlineMemory.read S (keys i) = values i) →
        OnlineMemory.read (OnlineMemory.linearDeltaWrite S newKey newValue) newKey = newValue ∧
          ∀ (i : Fin N),
            OnlineMemory.read (OnlineMemory.linearDeltaWrite S newKey newValue) (keys i) = values i
theorems.proof_body:
fun {K V N} S keys values newKey newValue hunit horth hstored =>
  ⟨OnlineMemory.linearDeltaWrite_exact_overwrite S newKey newValue hunit, fun i =>
    Eq.mpr
      (id
        (congrArg (fun _a => _a = values i)
          (OnlineMemory.linearDeltaWrite_preserves_orthogonal_query S newKey (keys i) newValue
            (horth i))))
      (hstored i)⟩
theorems.name: OnlineMemory.linearDeltaWrite_extends_orthogonal_readouts
theorems.doc:
Online extension step: if `S` already stores an orthogonal family of keys,
a delta write to a new orthogonal unit key stores the new value and preserves
all existing readouts. This is the induction step behind online associative
memory.

theorems.source_text:
/-- Online overwrite step for an orthonormal table: rewriting one key replaces
only that key and preserves every other key. -/
theorem linearDeltaWrite_overwrites_one_preserves_others {N : Nat}
    (keys : Fin N → KeyVec K) (values : Fin N → ValueVec V)
    (horth : OrthonormalKeys keys) (target : Fin N) (newValue : ValueVec V) :
    read (linearDeltaWrite (memoryTable keys values) (keys target) newValue)
        (keys target) = newValue ∧
    ∀ i, i ≠ target →
      read (linearDeltaWrite (memoryTable keys values) (keys target) newValue)
        (keys i) = values i := by
  have hunit : keyDot (keys target) (keys target) = 1 := by
    have h := horth target target
    simpa using h
  constructor
  · exact linearDeltaWrite_exact_overwrite (memoryTable keys values) (keys target) newValue hunit
  · intro i hi
    have horth_i : keyDot (keys target) (keys i) = 0 := by
      have h := horth target i
      simp [hi.symm] at h
      exact h
    rw [linearDeltaWrite_preserves_orthogonal_query
      (memoryTable keys values) (keys target) (keys i) newValue horth_i]
    exact memoryTable_retrieves_orthonormal keys values horth i
theorems.signature:
∀ {K V N : ℕ} (keys : Fin N → OnlineMemory.KeyVec K) (values : Fin N → OnlineMemory.ValueVec V),
  OnlineMemory.OrthonormalKeys keys →
    ∀ (target : Fin N) (newValue : OnlineMemory.ValueVec V),
      OnlineMemory.read
            (OnlineMemory.linearDeltaWrite (OnlineMemory.memoryTable keys values) (keys target)
              newValue)
            (keys target) =
          newValue ∧
        ∀ (i : Fin N),
          i ≠ target →
            OnlineMemory.read
                (OnlineMemory.linearDeltaWrite (OnlineMemory.memoryTable keys values) (keys target)
                  newValue)
                (keys i) =
              values i
theorems.proof_body:
fun {K V N} keys values horth target newValue =>
  have hunit :=
    have h := horth target target;
    Eq.mp
      (congrArg (Eq (OnlineMemory.keyDot (keys target) (keys target)))
        (ite_cond_eq_true 1 0 (eq_self target)))
      h;
  ⟨OnlineMemory.linearDeltaWrite_exact_overwrite (OnlineMemory.memoryTable keys values)
      (keys target) newValue hunit,
    fun i hi =>
    have horth_i :=
      have h := horth target i;
      Eq.mp
        (congrArg (Eq (OnlineMemory.keyDot (keys target) (keys i)))
          (ite_cond_eq_false 1 0 (eq_false (Ne.symm hi))))
        h;
    Eq.mpr
      (id
        (congrArg (fun _a => _a = values i)
          (OnlineMemory.linearDeltaWrite_preserves_orthogonal_query
            (OnlineMemory.memoryTable keys values) (keys target) (keys i) newValue horth_i)))
      (OnlineMemory.memoryTable_retrieves_orthonormal keys values horth i)⟩
theorems.name: OnlineMemory.linearDeltaWrite_overwrites_one_preserves_others

Online overwrite step for an orthonormal table: rewriting one key replaces
only that key and preserves every other key.

theorems.source_text:
/-- Uniform one-step overwrite: for every old memory, every unit key, and every
desired value, one write makes that key read as the desired value. -/
def UniformOneStepOverwrite
    (write : Memory K V → KeyVec K → ValueVec V → Memory K V) : Prop :=
  ∀ (S : Memory K V) (k : KeyVec K) (v : ValueVec V),
    keyDot k k = 1 → read (write S k v) k = v
theorems.signature:
{K V : ℕ} →
  (OnlineMemory.Memory K V →
      OnlineMemory.KeyVec K → OnlineMemory.ValueVec V → OnlineMemory.Memory K V) →
    Prop
theorems.proof_body:
fun {K V} write =>
  ∀ (S : OnlineMemory.Memory K V) (k : OnlineMemory.KeyVec K) (v : OnlineMemory.ValueVec V),
    OnlineMemory.keyDot k k = 1 → OnlineMemory.read (write S k v) k = v

theorems.source_text:
/-- Uniform orthogonal preservation: a write at key `k` leaves any orthogonal
query `q` unchanged. -/
def UniformOrthogonalPreservation
    (write : Memory K V → KeyVec K → ValueVec V → Memory K V) : Prop :=
  ∀ (S : Memory K V) (k q : KeyVec K) (v : ValueVec V),
    keyDot k q = 0 → read (write S k v) q = read S q
theorems.signature:
{K V : ℕ} →
  (OnlineMemory.Memory K V →
      OnlineMemory.KeyVec K → OnlineMemory.ValueVec V → OnlineMemory.Memory K V) →
    Prop
theorems.proof_body:
fun {K V} write =>
  ∀ (S : OnlineMemory.Memory K V) (k q : OnlineMemory.KeyVec K) (v : OnlineMemory.ValueVec V),
    OnlineMemory.keyDot k q = 0 → OnlineMemory.read (write S k v) q = OnlineMemory.read S q

Uniform orthogonal preservation: a write at key `k` leaves any orthogonal
query `q` unchanged.

theorems.source_text:
/-- A model family has one-step exact overwrite semantics for orthogonal keys if
its write operation satisfies exact overwrite and non-target preservation.

This is intentionally an operational spec, not a full architecture definition.
It lets later modules compare which update families implement the spec directly
and which need extra heads, dimensions, layers, or recurrent steps. -/
structure OneStepOverwriteSpec (K V : Nat) where
  write : Memory K V → KeyVec K → ValueVec V → Memory K V
  exactOverwrite :
    ∀ (S : Memory K V) (k : KeyVec K) (v : ValueVec V),
      keyDot k k = 1 → read (write S k v) k = v
  preservesOrthogonal :
    ∀ (S : Memory K V) (k q : KeyVec K) (v : ValueVec V),
      keyDot k q = 0 → read (write S k v) q = read S q
theorems.signature: ℕ → ℕ → Type

theorems.source_text:
/-- A model family has one-step exact overwrite semantics for orthogonal keys if
its write operation satisfies exact overwrite and non-target preservation.

This is intentionally an operational spec, not a full architecture definition.
It lets later modules compare which update families implement the spec directly
and which need extra heads, dimensions, layers, or recurrent steps. -/
structure OneStepOverwriteSpec (K V : Nat) where
  write : Memory K V → KeyVec K → ValueVec V → Memory K V
  exactOverwrite :
    ∀ (S : Memory K V) (k : KeyVec K) (v : ValueVec V),
      keyDot k k = 1 → read (write S k v) k = v
  preservesOrthogonal :
    ∀ (S : Memory K V) (k q : KeyVec K) (v : ValueVec V),
      keyDot k q = 0 → read (write S k v) q = read S q
theorems.signature:
{K V : ℕ} →
  {P : Sort u} →
    {x1 x2 : OnlineMemory.OneStepOverwriteSpec K V} →
      x1 = x2 → OnlineMemory.OneStepOverwriteSpec.noConfusionType P x1 x2
theorems.proof_body:
fun {K V} {P} {x1 x2} h12 =>
  Eq.ndrec (motive := fun {x2} =>
    x1 = x2 → OnlineMemory.OneStepOverwriteSpec.noConfusionType P x1 x2)
    (fun h11 =>
      OnlineMemory.OneStepOverwriteSpec.casesOn x1 fun write exactOverwrite preservesOrthogonal k =>
        k ⋯)
    h12 h12
theorems.name: OnlineMemory.OneStepOverwriteSpec.noConfusion

theorems.source_text:
/-- The ideal delta rule directly satisfies one-step overwrite semantics. -/
def linearDeltaOneStepOverwriteSpec (K V : Nat) : OneStepOverwriteSpec K V where
  write := linearDeltaWrite
  exactOverwrite := by
    intro S k v hk
    exact linearDeltaWrite_exact_overwrite S k v hk
  preservesOrthogonal := by
    intro S k q v horth
    exact linearDeltaWrite_preserves_orthogonal_query S k q v horth
theorems.signature: (K V : ℕ) → OnlineMemory.OneStepOverwriteSpec K V
theorems.proof_body: fun K V => { write := OnlineMemory.linearDeltaWrite, exactOverwrite := ⋯, preservesOrthogonal := ⋯ }
theorems.name: OnlineMemory.linearDeltaOneStepOverwriteSpec
theorems.doc: The ideal delta rule directly satisfies one-step overwrite semantics.

theorems.source_text:

theorems.source_text:
/-- A structure-level spec entails the two uniform operational obligations for
its write function. -/
theorem oneStepOverwriteSpec_has_uniform_obligations
    (spec : OneStepOverwriteSpec K V) :
    UniformOneStepOverwrite spec.write ∧
    UniformOrthogonalPreservation spec.write := by
  constructor
  · intro S k v hk
    exact spec.exactOverwrite S k v hk
  · intro S k q v horth
    exact spec.preservesOrthogonal S k q v horth
theorems.signature:
∀ {K V : ℕ} (spec : OnlineMemory.OneStepOverwriteSpec K V),
  OnlineMemory.UniformOneStepOverwrite spec.write ∧
    OnlineMemory.UniformOrthogonalPreservation spec.write
theorems.proof_body:
fun {K V} spec =>
  ⟨fun S k v hk => spec.exactOverwrite S k v hk, fun S k q v horth =>
    spec.preservesOrthogonal S k q v horth⟩
theorems.name: OnlineMemory.oneStepOverwriteSpec_has_uniform_obligations
theorems.doc:
A structure-level spec entails the two uniform operational obligations for
its write function.

theorems.source_text:
/-- The two uniform obligations are exactly the data needed to package a write
function as a one-step overwrite spec. -/
theorem exists_oneStepOverwriteSpec_iff_uniform_obligations
    (write : Memory K V → KeyVec K → ValueVec V → Memory K V) :
    (∃ spec : OneStepOverwriteSpec K V, spec.write = write) ↔
    UniformOneStepOverwrite write ∧
    UniformOrthogonalPreservation write := by
  constructor
  · rintro ⟨spec, hwrite⟩
    rw [← hwrite]
    exact oneStepOverwriteSpec_has_uniform_obligations spec
  · intro h
    refine ⟨{
      write := write
      exactOverwrite := ?_
      preservesOrthogonal := ?_
    }, rfl⟩
    · exact h.1
    · exact h.2
theorems.signature:
∀ {K V : ℕ}
  (write :
    OnlineMemory.Memory K V →
      OnlineMemory.KeyVec K → OnlineMemory.ValueVec V → OnlineMemory.Memory K V),
  (∃ spec, spec.write = write) ↔
    OnlineMemory.UniformOneStepOverwrite write ∧ OnlineMemory.UniformOrthogonalPreservation write
theorems.proof_body:
fun {K V} write =>
  {
    mp := fun a =>
      Exists.casesOn a fun spec hwrite =>
        Eq.mpr
          (id
            (congrArg
              (fun _a =>
                OnlineMemory.UniformOneStepOverwrite _a ∧
                  OnlineMemory.UniformOrthogonalPreservation _a)
              (Eq.symm hwrite)))
          (OnlineMemory.oneStepOverwriteSpec_has_uniform_obligations spec),
    mpr := fun h =>
      Exists.intro { write := write, exactOverwrite := h.left, preservesOrthogonal := h.right }
        rfl }
theorems.name: OnlineMemory.exists_oneStepOverwriteSpec_iff_uniform_obligations
theorems.doc:
The two uniform obligations are exactly the data needed to package a write
function as a one-step overwrite spec.

theorems.source_text:
/-- A state-independent additive write cannot satisfy the same uniform overwrite
target when there exist two old memories with different content at the addressed
key.

This is the spec-level separation: delta memory satisfies the uniform one-step
overwrite obligation; raw/state-independent additive writes cannot satisfy it
uniformly unless the old addressed content is somehow already fixed or canceled
by extra machinery. -/
theorem stateIndependentAdditiveWrite_not_uniformOneStepOverwrite
    (term : KeyVec K → ValueVec V → Memory K V)
    (S₁ S₂ : Memory K V) (k : KeyVec K) (v : ValueVec V) (j : Fin V)
    (hunit : keyDot k k = 1)
    (hread : read S₁ k j ≠ read S₂ k j) :
    ¬ UniformOneStepOverwrite (K := K) (V := V)
      (stateIndependentAdditiveWrite term) := by
  intro hspec
  exact stateIndependentAdditiveWrite_cannot_exact_overwrite_two_memories
    term S₁ S₂ k v j hread ⟨hspec S₁ k v hunit, hspec S₂ k v hunit⟩
theorems.signature:
∀ {K V : ℕ} (term : OnlineMemory.KeyVec K → OnlineMemory.ValueVec V → OnlineMemory.Memory K V)
  (S₁ S₂ : OnlineMemory.Memory K V) (k : OnlineMemory.KeyVec K) (v : OnlineMemory.ValueVec V)
  (j : Fin V),
  OnlineMemory.keyDot k k = 1 →
    OnlineMemory.read S₁ k j ≠ OnlineMemory.read S₂ k j →
      ¬OnlineMemory.UniformOneStepOverwrite (OnlineMemory.stateIndependentAdditiveWrite term)
theorems.proof_body:
fun {K V} term S₁ S₂ k v j hunit hread hspec =>
  OnlineMemory.stateIndependentAdditiveWrite_cannot_exact_overwrite_two_memories term S₁ S₂ k v j
    hread ⟨hspec S₁ k v hunit, hspec S₂ k v hunit⟩
theorems.name: OnlineMemory.stateIndependentAdditiveWrite_not_uniformOneStepOverwrite
theorems.doc:
A state-independent additive write cannot satisfy the same uniform overwrite
target when there exist two old memories with different content at the addressed
key.

This is the spec-level separation: delta memory satisfies the uniform one-step
overwrite obligation; raw/state-independent additive writes cannot satisfy it
uniformly unless the old addressed content is somehow already fixed or canceled
by extra machinery.

theorems.source_text:
/-- The raw outer-product association primitive used by M2RNN-style matrix
state updates cannot be a uniform one-step overwrite memory when two old
memories disagree at the addressed key. -/
theorem rawOuterWrite_not_uniformOneStepOverwrite
    (S₁ S₂ : Memory K V) (k : KeyVec K) (v : ValueVec V) (j : Fin V)
    (hunit : keyDot k k = 1)
    (hread : read S₁ k j ≠ read S₂ k j) :
    ¬ UniformOneStepOverwrite (K := K) (V := V) rawOuterWrite := by
  intro hspec
  let term : KeyVec K → ValueVec V → Memory K V :=
    fun k v => M2RNNComparison.outerKV k v
  have h₁ :
      read (stateIndependentAdditiveWrite term S₁ k v) k = v := by
    simpa [term, stateIndependentAdditiveWrite, rawOuterWrite]
      using hspec S₁ k v hunit
  have h₂ :
      read (stateIndependentAdditiveWrite term S₂ k v) k = v := by
    simpa [term, stateIndependentAdditiveWrite, rawOuterWrite]
      using hspec S₂ k v hunit
  exact stateIndependentAdditiveWrite_cannot_exact_overwrite_two_memories
    term S₁ S₂ k v j hread ⟨h₁, h₂⟩
theorems.signature:
∀ {K V : ℕ} (S₁ S₂ : OnlineMemory.Memory K V) (k : OnlineMemory.KeyVec K)
  (v : OnlineMemory.ValueVec V) (j : Fin V),
  OnlineMemory.keyDot k k = 1 →
    OnlineMemory.read S₁ k j ≠ OnlineMemory.read S₂ k j →
      ¬OnlineMemory.UniformOneStepOverwrite OnlineMemory.rawOuterWrite
theorems.name: OnlineMemory.rawOuterWrite_not_uniformOneStepOverwrite
theorems.doc:
The raw outer-product association primitive used by M2RNN-style matrix
state updates cannot be a uniform one-step overwrite memory when two old
memories disagree at the addressed key.

theorems.source_text:
/-- Memory-semantics separation for the direct comparison with M2RNN:
GDN's ideal delta core and E88/NDM's pre-tanh delta core satisfy uniform
one-step overwrite, while M2RNN's raw association primitive cannot satisfy that
same target without extra state-dependent correction or other machinery. -/
theorem delta_core_separates_gdn_emender_from_m2rnn_raw_write
    (S₁ S₂ : Memory K V) (k : KeyVec K) (v : ValueVec V) (j : Fin V)
    (hunit : keyDot k k = 1)
    (hread : read S₁ k j ≠ read S₂ k j) :
    UniformOneStepOverwrite (K := K) (V := V) gdnIdealWrite ∧
    UniformOneStepOverwrite (K := K) (V := V) emenderPreTanhWrite ∧
    ¬ UniformOneStepOverwrite (K := K) (V := V) rawOuterWrite := by
  constructor
  · exact gdnIdealWrite_uniformOneStepOverwrite
  constructor
  · exact emenderPreTanhWrite_uniformOneStepOverwrite
  · exact rawOuterWrite_not_uniformOneStepOverwrite S₁ S₂ k v j hunit hread
theorems.signature:
∀ {K V : ℕ} (S₁ S₂ : OnlineMemory.Memory K V) (k : OnlineMemory.KeyVec K)
  (v : OnlineMemory.ValueVec V) (j : Fin V),
  OnlineMemory.keyDot k k = 1 →
    OnlineMemory.read S₁ k j ≠ OnlineMemory.read S₂ k j →
      OnlineMemory.UniformOneStepOverwrite OnlineMemory.gdnIdealWrite ∧
        OnlineMemory.UniformOneStepOverwrite OnlineMemory.emenderPreTanhWrite ∧
          ¬OnlineMemory.UniformOneStepOverwrite OnlineMemory.rawOuterWrite
theorems.proof_body:
fun {K V} S₁ S₂ k v j hunit hread =>
  ⟨OnlineMemory.gdnIdealWrite_uniformOneStepOverwrite,
    ⟨OnlineMemory.emenderPreTanhWrite_uniformOneStepOverwrite,
      OnlineMemory.rawOuterWrite_not_uniformOneStepOverwrite S₁ S₂ k v j hunit hread⟩⟩
theorems.name: OnlineMemory.delta_core_separates_gdn_emender_from_m2rnn_raw_write
theorems.doc:
Memory-semantics separation for the direct comparison with M2RNN:
GDN's ideal delta core and E88/NDM's pre-tanh delta core satisfy uniform
one-step overwrite, while M2RNN's raw association primitive cannot satisfy that
same target without extra state-dependent correction or other machinery.

theorems.signature: (x x_1 : RecurrentResourceFormalism.ArchitectureSignature) → Decidable (x = x_1)
theorems.proof_body:
fun x x_1 =>
  match x, x_1 with
  |
  { name := a, stateGeometry := a_1, temporalNonlinearity := a_2, transitionSide := a_3,
      transitionControl := a_4, writeRule := a_5, carryPlacement := a_6, memorySemantics := a_7,
      implementationMode := a_8, totalLayers := a_9, nonlinearTemporalLayers := a_10,
      headsPerLayer := a_11, stateScalarsPerHead := a_12, pureRecurrentStack := a_13,
      scanCompatible := a_14 },
    { name := b, stateGeometry := b_1, temporalNonlinearity := b_2, transitionSide := b_3,
      transitionControl := b_4, writeRule := b_5, carryPlacement := b_6, memorySemantics := b_7,
      implementationMode := b_8, totalLayers := b_9, nonlinearTemporalLayers := b_10,
      headsPerLayer := b_11, stateScalarsPerHead := b_12, pureRecurrentStack := b_13,
      scanCompatible := b_14 } =>
    if h : a = b then
      h ▸
        if h : a_1 = b_1 then
          h ▸
            if h : a_2 = b_2 then
              h ▸
                if h : a_3 = b_3 then
                  h ▸
                    if h : a_4 = b_4 then
                      h ▸
                        if h : a_5 = b_5 then
                          h ▸
                            if h : a_6 = b_6 then
                              h ▸
                                if h : a_7 = b_7 then
                                  h ▸
                                    if h : a_8 = b_8 then
                                      h ▸
                                        if h : a_9 = b_9 then
                                          h ▸
                                            if h : a_10 = b_10 then
                                              h ▸
                                                if h : a_11 = b_11 then
                                                  h ▸
                                                    if h : a_12 = b_12 then
                                                      h ▸
                                                        if h : a_13 = b_13 then
                                                          h ▸
                                                            if h : a_14 = b_14 then h ▸ isTrue ⋯
                                                            else isFalse ⋯
                                                        else isFalse ⋯
                                                    else isFalse ⋯
                                                else isFalse ⋯
                                            else isFalse ⋯
                                        else isFalse ⋯
                                    else isFalse ⋯
                                else isFalse ⋯
                            else isFalse ⋯
                        else isFalse ⋯
                    else isFalse ⋯
                else isFalse ⋯
            else isFalse ⋯
        else isFalse ⋯
    else isFalse ⋯

/-- Pure recurrent stack with temporal nonlinearity in every layer. -/
def isPureNonlinearRecurrentStack (a : ArchitectureSignature) : Bool :=
  a.pureRecurrentStack &&
  allLayersTemporallyNonlinear a &&
  hasTemporalNonlinearity a.temporalNonlinearity
theorems.signature: RecurrentResourceFormalism.ArchitectureSignature → Bool

fun a =>
  a.pureRecurrentStack && RecurrentResourceFormalism.allLayersTemporallyNonlinear a &&
    RecurrentResourceFormalism.hasTemporalNonlinearity a.temporalNonlinearity
theorems.name: RecurrentResourceFormalism.isPureNonlinearRecurrentStack
theorems.doc: Pure recurrent stack with temporal nonlinearity in every layer.

theorems.source_text:
/-- NDM: Nonlinear Delta Memory.

This is the paper-facing model family: a pure nonlinear recurrent stack with
matrix state, input-dependent delta correction, and many independent recurrent
programs per token. E88 is the current production implementation lineage of
this family.
-/
def emender (layers heads nState : Nat) : ArchitectureSignature where
  name := "E88/NDM"
  stateGeometry := .matrix
  temporalNonlinearity := .fullState
  transitionSide := .left
  transitionControl := .inputDependent
  writeRule := .deltaCorrecting
  carryPlacement := .insideNonlinearity
  memorySemantics := .errorCorrectingAssociativeTable
  implementationMode := .sequentialManyProgram
  totalLayers := layers
  nonlinearTemporalLayers := layers
  headsPerLayer := heads
  stateScalarsPerHead := nState * nState
  pureRecurrentStack := true
  scanCompatible := false
theorems.signature: ℕ → ℕ → ℕ → RecurrentResourceFormalism.ArchitectureSignature
theorems.proof_body:
fun layers heads nState =>
  { name := "E88/NDM", stateGeometry := RecurrentResourceFormalism.StateGeometry.matrix,
    temporalNonlinearity := RecurrentResourceFormalism.TemporalNonlinearity.fullState,
    transitionSide := RecurrentResourceFormalism.TransitionSide.left,
    transitionControl := RecurrentResourceFormalism.TransitionControl.inputDependent,
    writeRule := RecurrentResourceFormalism.WriteRule.deltaCorrecting,
    carryPlacement := RecurrentResourceFormalism.CarryPlacement.insideNonlinearity,
    memorySemantics := RecurrentResourceFormalism.MemorySemantics.errorCorrectingAssociativeTable,
    implementationMode := RecurrentResourceFormalism.ImplementationMode.sequentialManyProgram,
    totalLayers := layers, nonlinearTemporalLayers := layers, headsPerLayer := heads,
    stateScalarsPerHead := nState * nState, pureRecurrentStack := true, scanCompatible := false }
theorems.name: RecurrentResourceFormalism.emender
theorems.doc:
NDM: Nonlinear Delta Memory.

This is the paper-facing model family: a pure nonlinear recurrent stack with
matrix state, input-dependent delta correction, and many independent recurrent
programs per token. E88 is the current production implementation lineage of
this family.

theorems.signature: ℕ → ℕ → ℕ → RecurrentResourceFormalism.ArchitectureSignature
theorems.proof_body: fun layers heads nState => RecurrentResourceFormalism.emender layers heads nState
theorems.name: RecurrentResourceFormalism.e88NDM
theorems.doc: Backward-compatible name for the E88 implementation lineage of NDM.

  totalLayers := layers
  nonlinearTemporalLayers := layers
  headsPerLayer := heads

  pureRecurrentStack := true
  scanCompatible := false

/-- Gated DeltaNet / FLA-GDN: matrix state and delta-style associative memory,
but linear/affine in the recurrent state for fixed inputs. -/
def gatedDeltaNet (layers heads stateScalarsPerHead : Nat) : ArchitectureSignature where
  name := "Gated DeltaNet"
  stateGeometry := .matrix
  temporalNonlinearity := .none
  transitionSide := .left
  transitionControl := .inputDependent
  writeRule := .selectiveEraseWrite
  carryPlacement := .none
  memorySemantics := .rawAssociativeTable
  implementationMode := .parallelScan
  totalLayers := layers
  nonlinearTemporalLayers := 0
  headsPerLayer := heads
  stateScalarsPerHead := stateScalarsPerHead
  pureRecurrentStack := true
  scanCompatible := true

      (m2rnnPure m2Layers m2Heads m2State).writeRule ∧
    e88NDM_1p27B.transitionControl ≠
      (m2rnnPure m2Layers m2Heads m2State).transitionControl := by

  · intro h

  · intro h
    cases h
  · intro h
    cases h

∀ (m2Layers m2Heads m2State : ℕ),
  RecurrentResourceFormalism.e88NDM_1p27B.transitionSide ≠
      (RecurrentResourceFormalism.m2rnnPure m2Layers m2Heads m2State).transitionSide ∧

    Eq.casesOn (motive := fun a t =>
      (RecurrentResourceFormalism.m2rnnPure m2Layers m2Heads m2State).transitionSide = a →
        h ≍ t → False)

      (fun h_1 =>
        False.elim (noConfusion_of_Nat RecurrentResourceFormalism.TransitionSide.ctorIdx h_1))
      (Eq.refl (RecurrentResourceFormalism.m2rnnPure m2Layers m2Heads m2State).transitionSide)
      (HEq.refl h),
    ⟨fun h =>
      Eq.casesOn (motive := fun a t =>
        (RecurrentResourceFormalism.m2rnnPure m2Layers m2Heads m2State).writeRule = a →

        (Eq.refl (RecurrentResourceFormalism.m2rnnPure m2Layers m2Heads m2State).writeRule)
        (HEq.refl h),

        (Eq.refl (RecurrentResourceFormalism.m2rnnPure m2Layers m2Heads m2State).transitionControl)
        (HEq.refl h)⟩⟩

theorems.signature: RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoVec

theorems.source_text:
/-- E88's expanded delta transition is genuinely key-dependent: two different
keys induce two different left-transition matrices.

This is the concrete core of the one-step transition-family separation. M2RNN's
learned right transition `W` is fixed across keys inside a layer; E88's delta
rule induces `lambda I - k k^T`, which changes with the current key. -/
theorem e88_two_keys_induce_distinct_left_transitions :
    M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaTransition (K := 2) 1 key0 ≠
      M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaTransition (K := 2) 1 key1 := by
  intro h
  have h00 := congrArg (fun M : TwoMat => M 0 0) h
  simp [M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaTransition, M2RNNComparison.outerKV, key0, key1] at h00
theorems.signature:
M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaTransition 1 RecurrentResourceFormalism.key0 ≠
  M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaTransition 1 RecurrentResourceFormalism.key1
theorems.proof_body:
fun h =>
  have h00 := congrArg (fun M => M 0 0) h;
  False.elim
    (Eq.mp
      (Eq.trans
        (congr
          (congrArg Eq
            (Eq.trans
              (congrArg (fun x => x 0 0)
                (congr (congrArg HSub.hSub (one_smul ℝ 1))
                  (congrArg (⇑Matrix.of)
                    (funext fun i =>
                      funext fun j =>
                        Eq.trans (mul_ite (j = 0) (if i = 0 then 1 else 0) 1 0)
                          (ite_congr (Eq.refl (j = 0)) (fun a => mul_one (if i = 0 then 1 else 0))
                            fun a => mul_zero (if i = 0 then 1 else 0))))))
              (Eq.trans
                (congr (congrArg HSub.hSub (Matrix.one_apply_eq 0))
                  (Eq.trans (ite_cond_eq_true (if 0 = 0 then 1 else 0) 0 (eq_self 0))
                    (ite_cond_eq_true 1 0 (eq_self 0))))
                (sub_self 1))))
          (Eq.trans
            (congrArg (fun x => x 0 0)
              (congr (congrArg HSub.hSub (one_smul ℝ 1))
                (congrArg (⇑Matrix.of)
                  (funext fun i =>
                    funext fun j =>
                      Eq.trans (mul_ite (j = 1) (if i = 1 then 1 else 0) 1 0)
                        (ite_congr (Eq.refl (j = 1)) (fun a => mul_one (if i = 1 then 1 else 0))
                          fun a => mul_zero (if i = 1 then 1 else 0))))))
            (Eq.trans
              (congr (congrArg HSub.hSub (Matrix.one_apply_eq 0))
                (ite_cond_eq_false (if 0 = 1 then 1 else 0) 0 zero_ne_one._simp_1))
              (sub_zero 1))))
        zero_ne_one._simp_1)
      h00)
theorems.name: RecurrentResourceFormalism.e88_two_keys_induce_distinct_left_transitions
theorems.doc:
E88's expanded delta transition is genuinely key-dependent: two different
keys induce two different left-transition matrices.

This is the concrete core of the one-step transition-family separation. M2RNN's
learned right transition `W` is fixed across keys inside a layer; E88's delta
rule induces `lambda I - k k^T`, which changes with the current key.

theorems.source_text:
/-- No fixed two-dimensional transition matrix can equal E88's key-dependent
transition for both basis keys. -/
theorem no_fixed_transition_matches_e88_two_basis_keys
    (A : TwoMat) :
    ¬ (A = M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaTransition (K := 2) 1 key0 ∧
       A = M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaTransition (K := 2) 1 key1) := by
  intro h
  exact e88_two_keys_induce_distinct_left_transitions (h.1.symm.trans h.2)
theorems.signature:
∀ (A : RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoMat),
  ¬(A = M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaTransition 1 RecurrentResourceFormalism.key0 ∧
      A = M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaTransition 1 RecurrentResourceFormalism.key1)
theorems.proof_body:
fun A h =>
  RecurrentResourceFormalism.e88_two_keys_induce_distinct_left_transitions
    (Eq.trans (Eq.symm h.left) h.right)
theorems.name: RecurrentResourceFormalism.no_fixed_transition_matches_e88_two_basis_keys
theorems.doc:
No fixed two-dimensional transition matrix can equal E88's key-dependent
transition for both basis keys.

theorems.source_text:
/-- M2RNN preactivation in the 2×2 witness setting:
`H W + k vᵀ`. -/
def m2rnnPreactivation2
    (W H : TwoMat) (k v : TwoVec) : TwoMat :=
  H * W + M2RNNComparison.outerKV k v
theorems.signature:
RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoMat →
  RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoMat →
    RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoVec →
      RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoVec → RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoMat
theorems.proof_body: fun W H k v => H * W + M2RNNComparison.outerKV k v
theorems.name: RecurrentResourceFormalism.m2rnnPreactivation2
theorems.doc:
M2RNN preactivation in the 2×2 witness setting:
`H W + k vᵀ`.

theorems.source_text:
/-- E88/NDM expanded delta preactivation in the 2×2 witness setting:
`(I - k kᵀ) H + k vᵀ`. -/
def e88DeltaPreactivation2
    (H : TwoMat) (k v : TwoVec) : TwoMat :=
  M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaTransition (K := 2) 1 k * H +
    M2RNNComparison.outerKV k v
theorems.signature:
RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoMat →
  RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoVec →
    RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoVec → RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoMat
theorems.proof_body: fun H k v => M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaTransition 1 k * H + M2RNNComparison.outerKV k v
theorems.name: RecurrentResourceFormalism.e88DeltaPreactivation2
theorems.doc:
E88/NDM expanded delta preactivation in the 2×2 witness setting:
`(I - k kᵀ) H + k vᵀ`.

theorems.source_text:
/-- No fixed M2RNN right-transition matrix can make the M2RNN preactivation
family match E88's key-dependent delta preactivation for two basis keys.

This is the one-step transition-family version of the separation. M2RNN's
candidate path has a fixed right transition `W`; E88/NDM's expanded delta rule
has an input-dependent left transition `I - k kᵀ`. If the two families matched
for all states and values at both `key0` and `key1`, then testing on `H = I`
and `v = 0` would force the same fixed `W` to equal two distinct E88
transitions. -/
theorem no_fixed_m2rnn_preactivation_matches_e88_two_basis_keys
    (W : TwoMat) :
    ¬ ((∀ H v,
          m2rnnPreactivation2 W H key0 v =
            e88DeltaPreactivation2 H key0 v) ∧
       (∀ H v,
          m2rnnPreactivation2 W H key1 v =
            e88DeltaPreactivation2 H key1 v)) := by
  intro h
  have hw0 :
      W = M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaTransition (K := 2) 1 key0 := by
    simpa [m2rnnPreactivation2, e88DeltaPreactivation2,
      M2RNNComparison.outerKV] using h.1 (1 : TwoMat) (0 : TwoVec)
  have hw1 :
      W = M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaTransition (K := 2) 1 key1 := by
    simpa [m2rnnPreactivation2, e88DeltaPreactivation2,
      M2RNNComparison.outerKV] using h.2 (1 : TwoMat) (0 : TwoVec)
  exact no_fixed_transition_matches_e88_two_basis_keys W ⟨hw0, hw1⟩
theorems.signature:
∀ (W : RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoMat),
  ¬((∀ (H : RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoMat) (v : RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoVec),
        RecurrentResourceFormalism.m2rnnPreactivation2 W H RecurrentResourceFormalism.key0 v =
          RecurrentResourceFormalism.e88DeltaPreactivation2 H RecurrentResourceFormalism.key0 v) ∧
      ∀ (H : RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoMat) (v : RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoVec),
        RecurrentResourceFormalism.m2rnnPreactivation2 W H RecurrentResourceFormalism.key1 v =
          RecurrentResourceFormalism.e88DeltaPreactivation2 H RecurrentResourceFormalism.key1 v)
theorems.proof_body:
fun W h =>
  have hw0 :=
    Eq.mp
      (Eq.trans
        (congr
          (congrArg Eq
            (congr (congrArg HAdd.hAdd (one_mul W))
              (congrArg (⇑Matrix.of)
                (funext fun i => funext fun j => mul_zero (RecurrentResourceFormalism.key0 i)))))
          (congr
            (congrArg HAdd.hAdd
              (mul_one (M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaTransition 1 RecurrentResourceFormalism.key0)))
            (congrArg (⇑Matrix.of)
              (funext fun i => funext fun j => mul_zero (RecurrentResourceFormalism.key0 i)))))
        (mul_left_inj._simp_4 (Matrix.of fun i j => 0)))
      (h.left 1 0);
  have hw1 :=
    Eq.mp
      (Eq.trans
        (congr
          (congrArg Eq
            (congr (congrArg HAdd.hAdd (one_mul W))
              (congrArg (⇑Matrix.of)
                (funext fun i => funext fun j => mul_zero (RecurrentResourceFormalism.key1 i)))))
          (congr
            (congrArg HAdd.hAdd
              (mul_one (M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaTransition 1 RecurrentResourceFormalism.key1)))
            (congrArg (⇑Matrix.of)
              (funext fun i => funext fun j => mul_zero (RecurrentResourceFormalism.key1 i)))))
        (mul_left_inj._simp_4 (Matrix.of fun i j => 0)))
      (h.right 1 0);
  RecurrentResourceFormalism.no_fixed_transition_matches_e88_two_basis_keys W ⟨hw0, hw1⟩
theorems.name: RecurrentResourceFormalism.no_fixed_m2rnn_preactivation_matches_e88_two_basis_keys
theorems.doc:
No fixed M2RNN right-transition matrix can make the M2RNN preactivation
family match E88's key-dependent delta preactivation for two basis keys.

This is the one-step transition-family version of the separation. M2RNN's
candidate path has a fixed right transition `W`; E88/NDM's expanded delta rule
has an input-dependent left transition `I - k kᵀ`. If the two families matched
for all states and values at both `key0` and `key1`, then testing on `H = I`
and `v = 0` would force the same fixed `W` to equal two distinct E88
transitions.

theorems.source_text:
/-- The same fixed-transition separation lifts through the `tanh` candidate
path. Since `tanh` is injective over reals, uniformly matching the M2RNN
candidate `tanh(H W + k vᵀ)` to the E88 candidate
`tanh((I - k kᵀ) H + k vᵀ)` would imply equality of the preactivations, which
the previous theorem rules out for two basis keys.

This still isolates the candidate path. The external M2RNN forget interpolation
is a further resource axis handled by later theorems. -/
theorem no_fixed_m2rnn_candidate_matches_e88_two_basis_keys
    (W : TwoMat) :
    ¬ ((∀ H v,
          M2RNNComparison.m2rnnCandidate W H key0 v =
            M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1 H key0 v) ∧
       (∀ H v,
          M2RNNComparison.m2rnnCandidate W H key1 v =
            M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1 H key1 v)) := by
  intro h
  apply no_fixed_m2rnn_preactivation_matches_e88_two_basis_keys W
  constructor
  · intro H v
    ext i j
    have hij := congrArg (fun M : TwoMat => M i j) (h.1 H v)
    have hpre := Activation.tanh_injective hij
    simpa [m2rnnPreactivation2, e88DeltaPreactivation2,
      M2RNNComparison.m2rnnCandidate, M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded,
      M2RNNComparison.matrixTanh, M2RNNComparison.matrixMap] using hpre
  · intro H v
    ext i j
    have hij := congrArg (fun M : TwoMat => M i j) (h.2 H v)
    have hpre := Activation.tanh_injective hij
    simpa [m2rnnPreactivation2, e88DeltaPreactivation2,
      M2RNNComparison.m2rnnCandidate, M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded,
      M2RNNComparison.matrixTanh, M2RNNComparison.matrixMap] using hpre
theorems.signature:
∀ (W : RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoMat),
  ¬((∀ (H : M2RNNComparison.MatState 2 2) (v : M2RNNComparison.Vec 2),
        M2RNNComparison.m2rnnCandidate W H RecurrentResourceFormalism.key0 v =
          M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1 H RecurrentResourceFormalism.key0 v) ∧
      ∀ (H : M2RNNComparison.MatState 2 2) (v : M2RNNComparison.Vec 2),
        M2RNNComparison.m2rnnCandidate W H RecurrentResourceFormalism.key1 v =
          M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1 H RecurrentResourceFormalism.key1 v)
theorems.proof_body:
fun W h =>
  RecurrentResourceFormalism.no_fixed_m2rnn_preactivation_matches_e88_two_basis_keys W
    ⟨fun H v =>
      Matrix.ext fun i j =>
        have hij := congrArg (fun M => M i j) (h.left H v);
        have hpre := Activation.tanh_injective hij;
        Eq.mpr
          (id
            (mul_left_inj._simp_4 (M2RNNComparison.outerKV RecurrentResourceFormalism.key0 v i j)))
          (Eq.mp
            (mul_left_inj._simp_4 (M2RNNComparison.outerKV RecurrentResourceFormalism.key0 v i j))
            hpre),
      fun H v =>
      Matrix.ext fun i j =>
        have hij := congrArg (fun M => M i j) (h.right H v);
        have hpre := Activation.tanh_injective hij;
        Eq.mpr
          (id
            (mul_left_inj._simp_4 (M2RNNComparison.outerKV RecurrentResourceFormalism.key1 v i j)))
          (Eq.mp
            (mul_left_inj._simp_4 (M2RNNComparison.outerKV RecurrentResourceFormalism.key1 v i j))
            hpre)⟩
theorems.name: RecurrentResourceFormalism.no_fixed_m2rnn_candidate_matches_e88_two_basis_keys
theorems.doc:
The same fixed-transition separation lifts through the `tanh` candidate
path. Since `tanh` is injective over reals, uniformly matching the M2RNN
candidate `tanh(H W + k vᵀ)` to the E88 candidate
`tanh((I - k kᵀ) H + k vᵀ)` would imply equality of the preactivations, which
the previous theorem rules out for two basis keys.

This still isolates the candidate path. The external M2RNN forget interpolation
is a further resource axis handled by later theorems.

theorems.source_text:
/-- The two basis keys still induce distinct E88/NDM updates after the
elementwise `tanh` nonlinearity.

The witness fixes `H = I` and `v = 0`. The preactivations are just the two
key-dependent E88 transition matrices, and elementwise `tanh` is injective. -/
theorem e88_two_keys_induce_distinct_updates_at_identity_zero :
    M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1 (1 : TwoMat) key0 (0 : TwoVec) ≠
      M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1 (1 : TwoMat) key1 (0 : TwoVec) := by
  intro h
  apply e88_two_keys_induce_distinct_left_transitions
  ext i j
  have hij := congrArg (fun M : TwoMat => M i j) h
  have hpre := Activation.tanh_injective hij
  simpa [M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded, M2RNNComparison.matrixTanh,
    M2RNNComparison.matrixMap, M2RNNComparison.outerKV] using hpre
theorems.signature:
M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1 1 RecurrentResourceFormalism.key0 0 ≠
  M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1 1 RecurrentResourceFormalism.key1 0
theorems.proof_body:
fun h =>
  RecurrentResourceFormalism.e88_two_keys_induce_distinct_left_transitions
    (Matrix.ext fun i j =>
      have hij := congrArg (fun M => M i j) h;
      have hpre := Activation.tanh_injective hij;
      Eq.mp
        (congr
          (congrArg Eq
            (Eq.trans
              (congrArg (fun x => x i j)
                (congr
                  (congrArg HAdd.hAdd
                    (mul_one
                      (M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaTransition 1 RecurrentResourceFormalism.key0)))
                  (congrArg (⇑Matrix.of)
                    (funext fun i =>
                      funext fun j => mul_zero (RecurrentResourceFormalism.key0 i)))))
              (add_zero
                (M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaTransition 1 RecurrentResourceFormalism.key0 i j))))
          (Eq.trans
            (congrArg (fun x => x i j)
              (congr
                (congrArg HAdd.hAdd
                  (mul_one (M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaTransition 1 RecurrentResourceFormalism.key1)))
                (congrArg (⇑Matrix.of)
                  (funext fun i => funext fun j => mul_zero (RecurrentResourceFormalism.key1 i)))))
            (add_zero (M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaTransition 1 RecurrentResourceFormalism.key1 i j))))
        hpre)
theorems.name: RecurrentResourceFormalism.e88_two_keys_induce_distinct_updates_at_identity_zero
theorems.doc:
The two basis keys still induce distinct E88/NDM updates after the
elementwise `tanh` nonlinearity.

The witness fixes `H = I` and `v = 0`. The preactivations are just the two
key-dependent E88 transition matrices, and elementwise `tanh` is injective.

theorems.source_text:
/-- Adding M2RNN's external scalar forget carry does not remove the
fixed-transition separation.

For any fixed right-transition matrix `W` and scalar forget gate `f`, the full
M2RNN update

`f H + (1 - f) tanh(H W + k vᵀ)`

cannot uniformly match E88/NDM's expanded delta update for both basis keys. The
witness again sets `H = I` and `v = 0`: M2RNN's full update is identical for
`key0` and `key1`, because the key only appears through the zero raw-write term,
while E88's update remains key-dependent through `(I - k kᵀ) H`. -/
theorem no_fixed_m2rnn_update_matches_e88_two_basis_keys
    (W : TwoMat) (f : Real) :
    ¬ ((∀ H v,
          M2RNNComparison.m2rnnUpdate W f H key0 v =
            M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1 H key0 v) ∧
       (∀ H v,
          M2RNNComparison.m2rnnUpdate W f H key1 v =
            M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1 H key1 v)) := by
  intro h
  have hm2 :
      M2RNNComparison.m2rnnUpdate W f (1 : TwoMat) key0 (0 : TwoVec) =
        M2RNNComparison.m2rnnUpdate W f (1 : TwoMat) key1 (0 : TwoVec) := by
    simp [M2RNNComparison.m2rnnUpdate, M2RNNComparison.m2rnnCandidate,
      M2RNNComparison.outerKV]
  have he88 :
      M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1 (1 : TwoMat) key0 (0 : TwoVec) =
        M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1 (1 : TwoMat) key1 (0 : TwoVec) := by
    calc
      M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1 (1 : TwoMat) key0 (0 : TwoVec)
          = M2RNNComparison.m2rnnUpdate W f (1 : TwoMat) key0 (0 : TwoVec) :=
            (h.1 (1 : TwoMat) (0 : TwoVec)).symm
      _ = M2RNNComparison.m2rnnUpdate W f (1 : TwoMat) key1 (0 : TwoVec) := hm2
      _ = M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1 (1 : TwoMat) key1 (0 : TwoVec) :=
            h.2 (1 : TwoMat) (0 : TwoVec)
  exact e88_two_keys_induce_distinct_updates_at_identity_zero he88
theorems.signature:
∀ (W : RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoMat) (f : ℝ),
  ¬((∀ (H : M2RNNComparison.MatState 2 2) (v : M2RNNComparison.Vec 2),
        M2RNNComparison.m2rnnUpdate W f H RecurrentResourceFormalism.key0 v =
          M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1 H RecurrentResourceFormalism.key0 v) ∧
      ∀ (H : M2RNNComparison.MatState 2 2) (v : M2RNNComparison.Vec 2),
        M2RNNComparison.m2rnnUpdate W f H RecurrentResourceFormalism.key1 v =
          M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1 H RecurrentResourceFormalism.key1 v)
theorems.proof_body:
fun W f h =>
  have hm2 :=
    of_eq_true
      (Eq.trans
        (congr
          (congrArg (fun x => Eq (f • 1 + (1 - f) • M2RNNComparison.matrixTanh x))
            (congr (congrArg HAdd.hAdd (one_mul W))
              (congrArg (⇑Matrix.of)
                (funext fun i => funext fun j => mul_zero (RecurrentResourceFormalism.key0 i)))))
          (congrArg (fun x => f • 1 + (1 - f) • M2RNNComparison.matrixTanh x)
            (congr (congrArg HAdd.hAdd (one_mul W))
              (congrArg (⇑Matrix.of)
                (funext fun i => funext fun j => mul_zero (RecurrentResourceFormalism.key1 i))))))
        (eq_self (f • 1 + (1 - f) • M2RNNComparison.matrixTanh (W + Matrix.of fun i j => 0))));
  have he88 := Trans.trans (Trans.trans (Eq.symm (h.left 1 0)) hm2) (h.right 1 0);
  RecurrentResourceFormalism.e88_two_keys_induce_distinct_updates_at_identity_zero he88
theorems.name: RecurrentResourceFormalism.no_fixed_m2rnn_update_matches_e88_two_basis_keys
theorems.doc:
Adding M2RNN's external scalar forget carry does not remove the
fixed-transition separation.

For any fixed right-transition matrix `W` and scalar forget gate `f`, the full
M2RNN update

`f H + (1 - f) tanh(H W + k vᵀ)`

cannot uniformly match E88/NDM's expanded delta update for both basis keys. The
witness again sets `H = I` and `v = 0`: M2RNN's full update is identical for
`key0` and `key1`, because the key only appears through the zero raw-write term,
while E88's update remains key-dependent through `(I - k kᵀ) H`.

theorems.source_text:
/-- `tanh(1)` is nonzero. -/
theorem tanh_one_ne_zero : Real.tanh (1 : Real) ≠ 0 := by
  intro h
  have hzero : Real.tanh (1 : Real) = Real.tanh 0 := by
    simpa [Real.tanh_zero] using h
  have hone : (1 : Real) = 0 := Activation.tanh_injective hzero
  norm_num at hone
theorems.signature: Real.tanh 1 ≠ 0
theorems.proof_body:
fun h =>
  have hzero := Eq.mpr (id (congrArg (Eq (Real.tanh 1)) Real.tanh_zero)) h;
  have hone := Activation.tanh_injective hzero;
  False.elim
    (Eq.mp
      (eq_false
        (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isNat_eq_false (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isNat_ofNat ℝ Nat.cast_one)
          (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isNat_ofNat ℝ Nat.cast_zero) (Eq.refl false)))
      hone)
theorems.name: RecurrentResourceFormalism.tanh_one_ne_zero
theorems.doc: `tanh(1)` is nonzero.

theorems.source_text:
/-- `tanh(-1)` is nonzero. -/
theorem tanh_neg_one_ne_zero : Real.tanh (-1 : Real) ≠ 0 := by
  intro h
  have hzero : Real.tanh (-1 : Real) = Real.tanh 0 := by
    simpa [Real.tanh_zero] using h
  have hneg : (-1 : Real) = 0 := Activation.tanh_injective hzero
  norm_num at hneg
theorems.signature: Real.tanh (-1) ≠ 0
theorems.proof_body:
fun h =>
  have hzero :=
    Eq.mpr (id (Eq.trans (congr (congrArg Eq (Real.tanh_neg 1)) Real.tanh_zero) inv_eq_one._simp_4))
      (Eq.mp (Eq.trans (congrArg (fun x => x = 0) (Real.tanh_neg 1)) inv_eq_one._simp_4) h);
  have hneg := Activation.tanh_injective hzero;
  False.elim
    (Eq.mp
      (eq_false
        (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isInt_eq_false
          (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isInt_neg (Eq.refl Neg.neg)
            (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.IsNat.to_isInt (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isNat_ofNat ℝ Nat.cast_one))
            (Eq.refl (Int.negOfNat 1)))
          (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.IsNat.to_isInt (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isNat_ofNat ℝ Nat.cast_zero))
          (Eq.refl false)))
      hneg)
theorems.name: RecurrentResourceFormalism.tanh_neg_one_ne_zero
theorems.doc: `tanh(-1)` is nonzero.

theorems.source_text:
/-- Even if the scalar M2RNN forget gate is allowed to depend on the input key,
the fixed-transition/scalar-carry update cannot uniformly match E88/NDM on the
two basis keys.

The key point is that matching writes from an empty state forces the scalar
forget gate to be `0` for each basis key: with `H = 0`, E88 writes
`tanh(k vᵀ)`, while M2RNN writes `(1 - f(k)) tanh(k vᵀ)`. Since `tanh(1) ≠ 0`,
uniform equality forces `f(key0) = f(key1) = 0`, reducing the claim to the
candidate-path separation above. -/
theorem no_fixed_m2rnn_update_with_key_scalar_forget_matches_e88_two_basis_keys
    (W : TwoMat) (f0 f1 : Real) :
    ¬ ((∀ H v,
          M2RNNComparison.m2rnnUpdate W f0 H key0 v =
            M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1 H key0 v) ∧
       (∀ H v,
          M2RNNComparison.m2rnnUpdate W f1 H key1 v =
            M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1 H key1 v)) := by
  intro h
  have hf0 : f0 = 0 := by
    have hentry := congrArg (fun M : TwoMat => M 0 0) (h.1 (0 : TwoMat) key0)
    have hentry' : (1 - f0) * Real.tanh (1 : Real) = Real.tanh (1 : Real) := by
      simpa [M2RNNComparison.m2rnnUpdate, M2RNNComparison.m2rnnCandidate,
        M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded, M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaTransition,
        M2RNNComparison.matrixTanh, M2RNNComparison.matrixMap,
        M2RNNComparison.outerKV, key0] using hentry
    have hmul : f0 * Real.tanh (1 : Real) = 0 := by nlinarith
    exact (mul_eq_zero.mp hmul).resolve_right tanh_one_ne_zero
  have hf1 : f1 = 0 := by
    have hentry := congrArg (fun M : TwoMat => M 1 1) (h.2 (0 : TwoMat) key1)
    have hentry' : (1 - f1) * Real.tanh (1 : Real) = Real.tanh (1 : Real) := by
      simpa [M2RNNComparison.m2rnnUpdate, M2RNNComparison.m2rnnCandidate,
        M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded, M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaTransition,
        M2RNNComparison.matrixTanh, M2RNNComparison.matrixMap,
        M2RNNComparison.outerKV, key1] using hentry
    have hmul : f1 * Real.tanh (1 : Real) = 0 := by nlinarith
    exact (mul_eq_zero.mp hmul).resolve_right tanh_one_ne_zero
  apply no_fixed_m2rnn_candidate_matches_e88_two_basis_keys W
  constructor
  · intro H v
    simpa [hf0, M2RNNComparison.m2rnnUpdate] using h.1 H v
  · intro H v
    simpa [hf1, M2RNNComparison.m2rnnUpdate] using h.2 H v
theorems.signature:
∀ (W : RecurrentResourceFormalism.TwoMat) (f0 f1 : ℝ),
  ¬((∀ (H : M2RNNComparison.MatState 2 2) (v : M2RNNComparison.Vec 2),
        M2RNNComparison.m2rnnUpdate W f0 H RecurrentResourceFormalism.key0 v =
          M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1 H RecurrentResourceFormalism.key0 v) ∧
      ∀ (H : M2RNNComparison.MatState 2 2) (v : M2RNNComparison.Vec 2),
        M2RNNComparison.m2rnnUpdate W f1 H RecurrentResourceFormalism.key1 v =
          M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1 H RecurrentResourceFormalism.key1 v)
theorems.proof_body:
fun W f0 f1 h =>
  have hf0 :=
    have hentry := congrArg (fun M => M 0 0) (h.left 0 RecurrentResourceFormalism.key0);
    have hentry' :=
      Eq.mp
        (congr
          (congrArg Eq
            (Eq.trans
              (congrArg (fun x => x 0 0)
                (congr (congrArg HAdd.hAdd (smul_zero f0))
                  (congrArg (fun x => (1 - f0) • Matrix.of x)
                    (funext fun i =>
                      funext fun j =>
                        congrArg Real.tanh
                          (Eq.trans
                            (congrArg (fun x => x i j)
                              (congr (congrArg HAdd.hAdd (zero_mul W))
                                (congrArg (⇑Matrix.of)
                                  (funext fun i =>
                                    funext fun j =>
                                      Eq.trans (mul_ite (j = 0) (if i = 0 then 1 else 0) 1 0)
                                        (ite_congr (Eq.refl (j = 0))
                                          (fun a => mul_one (if i = 0 then 1 else 0)) fun a =>
                                          mul_zero (if i = 0 then 1 else 0))))))
                            (zero_add (if j = 0 then if i = 0 then 1 else 0 else 0)))))))
              (Eq.trans
                (congrArg (HAdd.hAdd 0)
                  (Eq.trans
                    (congrArg (fun x => ((1 - f0) • x) 0)
                      (funext fun j =>
                        congrArg Real.tanh
                          (ite_congr (Eq.refl (j = 0)) (fun a => ite_cond_eq_true 1 0 (eq_self 0))
                            fun a => Eq.refl 0)))
                    (congrArg (fun x => (1 - f0) • Real.tanh x)
                      (ite_cond_eq_true 1 0 (eq_self 0)))))
                (zero_add ((1 - f0) * Real.tanh 1)))))
          (Eq.trans
            (congrArg (fun x => Matrix.of x 0 0)
              (funext fun i =>
                funext fun j =>
                  congrArg Real.tanh
                    (Eq.trans
                      (congrArg (fun x => x i j)
                        (congr
                          (congrArg HAdd.hAdd
                            (Eq.trans
                              (congrArg (fun x => x * 0)
                                (congr (congrArg HSub.hSub (one_smul ℝ 1))
                                  (congrArg (⇑Matrix.of)
                                    (funext fun i =>
                                      funext fun j =>
                                        Eq.trans (mul_ite (j = 0) (if i = 0 then 1 else 0) 1 0)
                                          (ite_congr (Eq.refl (j = 0))
                                            (fun a => mul_one (if i = 0 then 1 else 0)) fun a =>
                                            mul_zero (if i = 0 then 1 else 0))))))
                              (mul_zero
                                (1 -
                                  Matrix.of fun i j =>
                                    if j = 0 then if i = 0 then 1 else 0 else 0))))
                          (congrArg (⇑Matrix.of)
                            (funext fun i =>
                              funext fun j =>
                                Eq.trans (mul_ite (j = 0) (if i = 0 then 1 else 0) 1 0)
                                  (ite_congr (Eq.refl (j = 0))
                                    (fun a => mul_one (if i = 0 then 1 else 0)) fun a =>
                                    mul_zero (if i = 0 then 1 else 0))))))
                      (zero_add (if j = 0 then if i = 0 then 1 else 0 else 0)))))
            (congrArg Real.tanh
              (Eq.trans (ite_cond_eq_true (if 0 = 0 then 1 else 0) 0 (eq_self 0))
                (ite_cond_eq_true 1 0 (eq_self 0))))))
        hentry;
    have hmul :=
      Mathlib.Tactic.Linarith.eq_of_not_lt_of_not_gt (f0 * Real.tanh 1) 0
        (Not.intro fun a =>
          Mathlib.Tactic.Linarith.lt_irrefl
            (Eq.mp
              (congrArg (fun _a => _a < 0)
                (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.of_eq
                  (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_congr
                    (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.sub_congr
                      (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_congr
                        (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.sub_congr
                          (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.cast_pos
                            (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isNat_ofNat ℝ Nat.cast_one))
                          (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.atom_pf f0)
                          (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.sub_pf
                            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.neg_add
                              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.neg_mul f0 (Nat.rawCast 1)
                                (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.neg_one_mul

            x = M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1 H RecurrentResourceFormalism.key0 v)

            (congr (congrArg HAdd.hAdd (Eq.trans (congrArg (fun x => x • H) hf0) (zero_smul ℝ H)))
              (Eq.trans
                (congrArg
                  (fun x =>
                    x • M2RNNComparison.m2rnnCandidate W H RecurrentResourceFormalism.key0 v)
                  (Eq.trans (congrArg (HSub.hSub 1) hf0) (sub_zero 1)))
                (one_smul ℝ
                  (M2RNNComparison.m2rnnCandidate W H RecurrentResourceFormalism.key0 v))))
            (zero_add (M2RNNComparison.m2rnnCandidate W H RecurrentResourceFormalism.key0 v))))
        (h.left H v),
      fun H v =>
      Eq.mp
        (congrArg
          (fun x =>
            x = M2RNNComparison.e88DeltaUpdateExpanded 1 H RecurrentResourceFormalism.key1 v)
          (Eq.trans
            (congr (congrArg HAdd.hAdd (Eq.trans (congrArg (fun x => x • H) hf1) (zero_smul ℝ H)))
              (Eq.trans
                (congrArg
                  (fun x =>
                    x • M2RNNComparison.m2rnnCandidate W H RecurrentResourceFormalism.key1 v)
                  (Eq.trans (congrArg (HSub.hSub 1) hf1) (sub_zero 1)))
