theorems.source_text:
/-- A hybrid M2RNN stack — one that mixes in attention or scan layers — does not
satisfy `IsMultiProgrammed`, regardless of layer count, head count, or state
size.

This negative witness confirms the predicate is non-vacuous: any signature
with `pureRecurrentStack = false` (indicating the stack contains non-recurrent
layers that can break per-batch independence) fails the multi-programming check.
The specific witness here is the `m2rnnHybrid` family, which mixes M2RNN-style
recurrent layers with attention or GDN layers. -/
theorem not_multiProgrammed_m2rnnHybrid (layers nonlinearLayers heads state : Nat) :
    ¬ IsMultiProgrammed (m2rnnHybrid layers nonlinearLayers heads state) := by
  intro h
  obtain ⟨_, _, h_pure, _⟩ := h
  simp [m2rnnHybrid] at h_pure
theorems.signature:
∀ (layers nonlinearLayers heads state : ℕ),
  ¬RecurrentResourceFormalism.IsMultiProgrammed
      (RecurrentResourceFormalism.m2rnnHybrid layers nonlinearLayers heads state)
theorems.proof_body:
fun layers nonlinearLayers heads state h =>
  And.casesOn h fun left right =>
    And.casesOn right fun left right =>
      And.casesOn right fun h_pure right => False.elim (Eq.mp Bool.false_eq_true h_pure)
theorems.name: RecurrentResourceFormalism.not_multiProgrammed_m2rnnHybrid
theorems.doc:
A hybrid M2RNN stack — one that mixes in attention or scan layers — does not
satisfy `IsMultiProgrammed`, regardless of layer count, head count, or state
size.

This negative witness confirms the predicate is non-vacuous: any signature
with `pureRecurrentStack = false` (indicating the stack contains non-recurrent
layers that can break per-batch independence) fails the multi-programming check.
The specific witness here is the `m2rnnHybrid` family, which mixes M2RNN-style
recurrent layers with attention or GDN layers.

theorems.source_text:
/-- For each `i : Fin 4`, the swap `(i.castSucc, i.succ)` in `Equiv.Perm (Fin 5)`
is exactly the transposition of one of the four adjacent generators. -/
theorem exists_adjacent_generator_for_swap (i : Fin 4) :
    ∃ g : AdjacentGenerator,
      transposition g = Equiv.swap (i.castSucc : Fin 5) (i.succ : Fin 5) := by
  fin_cases i
  · exact ⟨.s01, by rfl⟩
  · exact ⟨.s12, by rfl⟩
  · exact ⟨.s23, by rfl⟩
  · exact ⟨.s34, by rfl⟩
theorems.signature: ∀ (i : Fin 4), ∃ g, S5Tracker.transposition g = Equiv.swap i.castSucc i.succ
theorems.proof_body:
fun i =>
  List.Mem.casesOn (motive := fun a x =>
    List.finRange 4 = a →
      Fintype.complete i ≍ x → ∃ g, S5Tracker.transposition g = Equiv.swap i.castSucc i.succ)
    (Fintype.complete i)
    (fun as h =>
      List.cons.noConfusion
        (Fintype.complete i ≍ List.Mem.head as →
          ∃ g, S5Tracker.transposition g = Equiv.swap i.castSucc i.succ)
        ((fun i => i) ⟨0, Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_refl 4)))⟩)
        ((fun x1 x2 => (fun i => i) x1 :: x2) ⟨1, Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_refl 4))⟩
          ((fun x1 x2 => (fun i => i) x1 :: x2) ⟨2, Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_refl 4)⟩
            ((fun x1 x2 => (fun i => i) x1 :: x2) ⟨3, Nat.le_refl 4⟩ [])))
        i as h fun head_eq =>
        Eq.ndrec (motive := fun i =>
          ∀ (this : i ∈ Fintype.elems),
            (fun x1 x2 => (fun i => i) x1 :: x2) ⟨1, Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_refl 4))⟩
                  ((fun x1 x2 => (fun i => i) x1 :: x2) ⟨2, Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_refl 4)⟩
                    ((fun x1 x2 => (fun i => i) x1 :: x2) ⟨3, Nat.le_refl 4⟩ [])) =
                as →
              this ≍ List.Mem.head as →
                ∃ g, S5Tracker.transposition g = Equiv.swap i.castSucc i.succ)
          (fun this tail_eq =>
            Eq.ndrec (motive := fun as =>
              this ≍ List.Mem.head as →
                ∃ g,
                  S5Tracker.transposition g =
                    Equiv.swap
                      ((fun i => i)
                          ⟨0, Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_refl 4)))⟩).castSucc
                      ((fun i => i)
                          ⟨0, Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_refl 4)))⟩).succ)
              (fun h =>
                Exists.intro S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s01
                  (Eq.refl (S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s01)))
              tail_eq)
          head_eq (Fintype.complete i))
    (fun b {as} a h =>
      List.cons.noConfusion
        (Fintype.complete i ≍ List.Mem.tail b a →
          ∃ g, S5Tracker.transposition g = Equiv.swap i.castSucc i.succ)
        ((fun i => i) ⟨0, Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_refl 4)))⟩)
        ((fun x1 x2 => (fun i => i) x1 :: x2) ⟨1, Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_refl 4))⟩
          ((fun x1 x2 => (fun i => i) x1 :: x2) ⟨2, Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_refl 4)⟩
            ((fun x1 x2 => (fun i => i) x1 :: x2) ⟨3, Nat.le_refl 4⟩ [])))
        b as h fun head_eq =>
        Eq.ndrec (motive := fun b =>
          (fun x1 x2 => (fun i => i) x1 :: x2) ⟨1, Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_refl 4))⟩
                ((fun x1 x2 => (fun i => i) x1 :: x2) ⟨2, Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_refl 4)⟩
                  ((fun x1 x2 => (fun i => i) x1 :: x2) ⟨3, Nat.le_refl 4⟩ [])) =
              as →
            Fintype.complete i ≍ List.Mem.tail b a →
              ∃ g, S5Tracker.transposition g = Equiv.swap i.castSucc i.succ)
          (fun tail_eq =>
            Eq.ndrec (motive := fun {as} =>
              ∀ (a : List.Mem i as),
                Fintype.complete i ≍
                    List.Mem.tail
                      ((fun i => i) ⟨0, Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_refl 4)))⟩)
                      a →
                  ∃ g, S5Tracker.transposition g = Equiv.swap i.castSucc i.succ)
              (fun a h =>
                List.Mem.casesOn (motive := fun a_1 x =>
                  (fun x1 x2 => (fun i => i) x1 :: x2)
                        ⟨1, Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_refl 4))⟩
                        ((fun x1 x2 => (fun i => i) x1 :: x2) ⟨2, Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_refl 4)⟩
                          ((fun x1 x2 => (fun i => i) x1 :: x2) ⟨3, Nat.le_refl 4⟩ [])) =
                      a_1 →
                    a ≍ x → ∃ g, S5Tracker.transposition g = Equiv.swap i.castSucc i.succ)
                  a
                  (fun as h =>
                    List.cons.noConfusion
                      (a ≍ List.Mem.head as →
                        ∃ g, S5Tracker.transposition g = Equiv.swap i.castSucc i.succ)
                      ((fun i => i) ⟨1, Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_refl 4))⟩)
                      ((fun x1 x2 => (fun i => i) x1 :: x2) ⟨2, Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_refl 4)⟩
                        ((fun x1 x2 => (fun i => i) x1 :: x2) ⟨3, Nat.le_refl 4⟩ []))
                      i as h fun head_eq =>
                      Eq.ndrec (motive := fun i =>
                        ∀
                          (a :
                            List.Mem i
                              ((fun x1 x2 => (fun i => i) x1 :: x2)
                                ⟨1, Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_refl 4))⟩
                                ((fun x1 x2 => (fun i => i) x1 :: x2)
                                  ⟨2, Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_refl 4)⟩
                                  ((fun x1 x2 => (fun i => i) x1 :: x2) ⟨3, Nat.le_refl 4⟩ [])))),
                          (fun x1 x2 => (fun i => i) x1 :: x2) ⟨2, Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_refl 4)⟩
                                ((fun x1 x2 => (fun i => i) x1 :: x2) ⟨3, Nat.le_refl 4⟩ []) =
                              as →
                            a ≍ List.Mem.head as →
                              ∃ g, S5Tracker.transposition g = Equiv.swap i.castSucc i.succ)
                        (fun a tail_eq =>
                          Eq.ndrec (motive := fun as =>
                            a ≍ List.Mem.head as →
                              ∃ g,
                                S5Tracker.transposition g =
                                  Equiv.swap
                                    ((fun i => i)
                                        ⟨1, Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_refl 4))⟩).castSucc
                                    ((fun i => i)
                                        ⟨1, Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_refl 4))⟩).succ)
                            (fun h =>
                              Exists.intro S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s12
                                (Eq.refl (S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s12)))
                            tail_eq)
                        head_eq a)
                  (fun b {as} a_1 h =>
                    List.cons.noConfusion
                      (a ≍ List.Mem.tail b a_1 →
                        ∃ g, S5Tracker.transposition g = Equiv.swap i.castSucc i.succ)
                      ((fun i => i) ⟨1, Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_of_lt (Nat.le_refl 4))⟩)

theorems.name: S5Inseparability.exists_adjacent_generator_for_swap
theorems.doc:
For each `i : Fin 4`, the swap `(i.castSucc, i.succ)` in `Equiv.Perm (Fin 5)`
is exactly the transposition of one of the four adjacent generators.

theorems.source_text:
/-- Running a single-token word produces the corresponding adjacent
transposition. -/
@[simp] theorem run_singleton (g : AdjacentGenerator) :
    run [g] = transposition g := by
  simp [run, runFrom, step]
theorems.signature: ∀ (g : S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator), S5Tracker.run [g] = S5Tracker.transposition g
theorems.proof_body:
fun g =>
  of_eq_true
    (Eq.trans
      (congrArg (fun x => List.foldl S5Tracker.step x [] = S5Tracker.transposition g)
        (one_mul (S5Tracker.transposition g)))
      (eq_self (S5Tracker.transposition g)))

Running a single-token word produces the corresponding adjacent

theorems.source_text:
/-- **`S5Tracker.run` is surjective onto S5.** Every element of `Equiv.Perm (Fin 5)`
is realised by some word over the four adjacent generators. -/
theorem run_surjective :
    Function.Surjective (run : List AdjacentGenerator → S5) := by
  intro g
  refine Submonoid.dense_induction
    (motive := fun (x : S5) => ∃ w : List AdjacentGenerator, run w = x)
    (s := Set.range fun i : Fin 4 =>
      Equiv.swap (i.castSucc : Fin 5) (i.succ : Fin 5))
    (closure := Equiv.Perm.mclosure_swap_castSucc_succ 4)
    ?mem ?one ?mul g
  case mem =>
    rintro x ⟨i, rfl⟩
    obtain ⟨gen, hgen⟩ := exists_adjacent_generator_for_swap i
    exact ⟨[gen], by rw [run_singleton, hgen]⟩
  case one =>
    exact ⟨[], rfl⟩
  case mul =>
    rintro x y ⟨wx, hx⟩ ⟨wy, hy⟩
    exact ⟨wx ++ wy, by rw [run_append, hx, hy]⟩
theorems.signature: Function.Surjective S5Tracker.run
theorems.proof_body:
fun g =>
  Submonoid.dense_induction (Set.range fun i => Equiv.swap i.castSucc i.succ)
    (Equiv.Perm.mclosure_swap_castSucc_succ 4)
    (fun x a =>
      Exists.casesOn a fun i h =>
        h ▸
          Exists.casesOn (S5Inseparability.exists_adjacent_generator_for_swap i) fun gen hgen =>
            Exists.intro [gen]
              (Eq.mpr
                (id
                  (congrArg (fun _a => _a = (fun i => Equiv.swap i.castSucc i.succ) i)
                    (S5Inseparability.run_singleton gen)))
                (Eq.mpr
                  (id (congrArg (fun _a => _a = (fun i => Equiv.swap i.castSucc i.succ) i) hgen))
                  (Eq.refl (Equiv.swap i.castSucc i.succ)))))
    (Exists.intro [] rfl)
    (fun x y a =>
      Exists.casesOn (motive := fun x_1 =>
        (fun x => ∃ w, S5Tracker.run w = x) y → (fun x => ∃ w, S5Tracker.run w = x) (x * y)) a
        fun wx hx a =>
        Exists.casesOn a fun wy hy =>
          Exists.intro (wx ++ wy)
            (Eq.mpr (id (congrArg (fun _a => _a = x * y) (S5Tracker.run_append wx wy)))
              (Eq.mpr (id (congrArg (fun _a => _a * S5Tracker.run wy = x * y) hx))
                (Eq.mpr (id (congrArg (fun _a => x * _a = x * y) hy)) (Eq.refl (x * y))))))
    g
theorems.name: S5Inseparability.run_surjective
theorems.doc:
**`S5Tracker.run` is surjective onto S5.** Every element of `Equiv.Perm (Fin 5)`
is realised by some word over the four adjacent generators.

theorems.source_text:
/-- A choice of representative word for each S5 element. Defined
non-constructively via `Classical.choose` from surjectivity. -/
noncomputable def wordOf (g : S5) : List AdjacentGenerator :=
  Classical.choose (run_surjective g)
theorems.signature: S5Witness.S5 → List S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator
theorems.proof_body: fun g => Classical.choose ⋯
theorems.name: S5Inseparability.wordOf
theorems.doc:
A choice of representative word for each S5 element. Defined
non-constructively via `Classical.choose` from surjectivity.

theorems.source_text:
/-- The representative word for `g` runs to `g`. -/
theorem run_wordOf (g : S5) : run (wordOf g) = g :=
  Classical.choose_spec (run_surjective g)
theorems.signature: ∀ (g : S5Witness.S5), S5Tracker.run (S5Inseparability.wordOf g) = g
theorems.proof_body: fun g => Classical.choose_spec (S5Inseparability.run_surjective g)
theorems.name: S5Inseparability.run_wordOf
theorems.doc: The representative word for `g` runs to `g`.

theorems.source_text:
/-- A package of a finite-state online recognizer together with a state
decoder that realizes the S5 tracker on every input word. -/
structure RealizesS5Tracker where
  /-- The underlying finite-state recognizer. -/
  M : FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer AdjacentGenerator
  /-- The state decoder mapping recognizer states to S5 elements. -/
  decode : M.State → S5
  /-- Realization equation: decoded run agrees with the S5 tracker on every
  input word. -/
  realizes : ∀ w : List AdjacentGenerator, decode (M.run w) = run w
theorems.signature: Type (u_1 + 1)
theorems.name: S5Inseparability.RealizesS5Tracker
theorems.doc:
A package of a finite-state online recognizer together with a state
decoder that realizes the S5 tracker on every input word.

theorems.source_text:
/-- The state map `g ↦ M.run (wordOf g)` is injective on `S5`. Two distinct
S5 elements correspond to two distinct recognizer states. -/
theorem realization_state_injective (R : RealizesS5Tracker) :
    Function.Injective (fun g : S5 => R.M.run (wordOf g)) := by
  intro g₁ g₂ hEq
  simp only at hEq
  have h1 : R.decode (R.M.run (wordOf g₁)) = g₁ := by
    rw [R.realizes, run_wordOf]
  have h2 : R.decode (R.M.run (wordOf g₂)) = g₂ := by
    rw [R.realizes, run_wordOf]
  rw [hEq] at h1
  rw [h1] at h2
  exact h2
theorems.signature:
∀ (R : S5Inseparability.RealizesS5Tracker),
  Function.Injective fun g => R.M.run (S5Inseparability.wordOf g)
theorems.proof_body:
fun R ⦃g₁ g₂⦄ hEq =>
  have h1 :=
    Eq.mpr (id (congrArg (fun _a => _a = g₁) (R.realizes (S5Inseparability.wordOf g₁))))
      (Eq.mpr (id (congrArg (fun _a => _a = g₁) (S5Inseparability.run_wordOf g₁))) (Eq.refl g₁));
  have h2 :=
    Eq.mpr (id (congrArg (fun _a => _a = g₂) (R.realizes (S5Inseparability.wordOf g₂))))
      (Eq.mpr (id (congrArg (fun _a => _a = g₂) (S5Inseparability.run_wordOf g₂))) (Eq.refl g₂));
  Eq.mp (congrArg (fun _a => _a = g₂) (Eq.mp (congrArg (fun _a => R.decode _a = g₁) hEq) h1)) h2
theorems.name: S5Inseparability.realization_state_injective
theorems.doc:
The state map `g ↦ M.run (wordOf g)` is injective on `S5`. Two distinct
S5 elements correspond to two distinct recognizer states.

theorems.source_text:
/-- **Capacity lower bound.** Any finite-state recognizer that realizes the
S5 tracker has at least 120 states. -/
theorem capacity_lower_bound_120 (R : RealizesS5Tracker) :
    120 ≤ Fintype.card R.M.State := by
  have h_inj := realization_state_injective R
  have h_card_le : Fintype.card S5 ≤ Fintype.card R.M.State :=
    Fintype.card_le_of_injective _ h_inj
  rw [S5Witness.s5_state_count] at h_card_le
  exact h_card_le
theorems.signature: ∀ (R : S5Inseparability.RealizesS5Tracker), 120 ≤ Fintype.card R.M.State
theorems.name: S5Inseparability.capacity_lower_bound_120
theorems.doc:
**Capacity lower bound.** Any finite-state recognizer that realizes the
S5 tracker has at least 120 states.

theorems.source_text:
/-- **S5 inseparability — existential capacity form.** No finite-state
recognizer with strictly fewer than 120 states realizes the S5 tracker.

This is the cleanest existential reformulation of the
`emender_m2rnn_s5_inseparability` target in `LEAN_FRONTIER.md`. The full
explicit `T(d)` polynomial bound for bounded-precision raw-write RNNs
remains open; see `LEAN_FRONTIER.md` for the obstruction inventory and the
infrastructure that would close it. -/
theorem emender_m2rnn_s5_inseparability_existential
    (R : RealizesS5Tracker) (hcard : Fintype.card R.M.State < 120) :
    False := by
  have h120 := capacity_lower_bound_120 R
  omega
theorems.signature: ∀ (R : S5Inseparability.RealizesS5Tracker), Fintype.card R.M.State < 120 → False
theorems.name: S5Inseparability.emender_m2rnn_s5_inseparability_existential
theorems.doc:
**S5 inseparability — existential capacity form.** No finite-state
recognizer with strictly fewer than 120 states realizes the S5 tracker.

This is the cleanest existential reformulation of the
`emender_m2rnn_s5_inseparability` target in `LEAN_FRONTIER.md`. The full
explicit `T(d)` polynomial bound for bounded-precision raw-write RNNs
remains open; see `LEAN_FRONTIER.md` for the obstruction inventory and the
infrastructure that would close it.

theorems.source_text:
/-- Stronger phrasing: any recognizer with strictly fewer than 120 states
cannot realize the S5 tracker, for any choice of state decoder. -/
theorem small_recognizer_cannot_realize_s5
    (M : FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer AdjacentGenerator)
    (hcard : Fintype.card M.State < 120)
    (decode : M.State → S5) :
    ¬ ∀ w : List AdjacentGenerator, decode (M.run w) = run w := by
  intro h
  exact emender_m2rnn_s5_inseparability_existential ⟨M, decode, h⟩ hcard
theorems.signature:
∀ (M : S5Witness.FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator),
  Fintype.card M.State < 120 →
    ∀ (decode : M.State → S5Witness.S5),
      ¬∀ (w : List S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator), decode (M.run w) = S5Tracker.run w
theorems.proof_body:
fun M hcard decode h =>
  S5Inseparability.emender_m2rnn_s5_inseparability_existential
    { M := M, decode := decode, realizes := h } hcard

theorems.doc:
Stronger phrasing: any recognizer with strictly fewer than 120 states

theorems.source_text:
/-- **Distinguishing-pair form.** Any recognizer with strictly fewer than
120 states admits two input words `w, w'` such that:

* `M.run w = M.run w'` (the recognizer cannot distinguish them), and
* `S5Tracker.run w ≠ S5Tracker.run w'` (the S5 tracker does distinguish them).

The lengths are bounded by `wordOf g₁` and `wordOf g₂` for some collision
pair `g₁ ≠ g₂` in S5, which by the surjectivity proof are finite. -/
theorem exists_distinguishing_word_pair
    (M : FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer AdjacentGenerator)
    (hcard : Fintype.card M.State < 120) :
    ∃ (w w' : List AdjacentGenerator),
      M.run w = M.run w' ∧ run w ≠ run w' := by
  -- The map g ↦ M.run (wordOf g) sends S5 (120 elts) into M.State (< 120),
  -- so it cannot be injective.
  have h_not_inj : ¬ Function.Injective (fun g : S5 => M.run (wordOf g)) := by
    intro h_inj
    have h_card_le : Fintype.card S5 ≤ Fintype.card M.State :=
      Fintype.card_le_of_injective _ h_inj
    rw [S5Witness.s5_state_count] at h_card_le
    omega
  -- Extract a collision.
  obtain ⟨g₁, g₂, h_eq, h_ne⟩ :=
    Function.not_injective_iff.mp h_not_inj
  refine ⟨wordOf g₁, wordOf g₂, h_eq, ?_⟩
  rw [run_wordOf, run_wordOf]
  exact h_ne
theorems.signature:
∀ (M : S5Witness.FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator),
  Fintype.card M.State < 120 → ∃ w w', M.run w = M.run w' ∧ S5Tracker.run w ≠ S5Tracker.run w'
theorems.proof_body:
fun M hcard =>
  have h_not_inj := fun h_inj =>
    have h_card_le :=
      Fintype.card_le_of_injective (fun g => M.run (S5Inseparability.wordOf g)) h_inj;
    S5Inseparability.exists_distinguishing_word_pair._proof_1_1 M hcard
      (Eq.mp (congrArg (fun _a => _a ≤ Fintype.card M.State) S5Witness.s5_state_count) h_card_le);
  Exists.casesOn (Function.not_injective_iff.mp h_not_inj) fun g₁ h =>
    Exists.casesOn h fun g₂ h =>
      And.casesOn h fun h_eq h_ne =>
        Exists.intro (S5Inseparability.wordOf g₁)
          (Exists.intro (S5Inseparability.wordOf g₂)
            ⟨h_eq,
              Eq.mpr
                (id
                  (congrArg (fun _a => _a ≠ S5Tracker.run (S5Inseparability.wordOf g₂))
                    (S5Inseparability.run_wordOf g₁)))
                (Eq.mpr (id (congrArg (fun _a => g₁ ≠ _a) (S5Inseparability.run_wordOf g₂))) h_ne)⟩)
theorems.name: S5Inseparability.exists_distinguishing_word_pair
theorems.doc:
**Distinguishing-pair form.** Any recognizer with strictly fewer than
120 states admits two input words `w, w'` such that:

* `M.run w = M.run w'` (the recognizer cannot distinguish them), and
* `S5Tracker.run w ≠ S5Tracker.run w'` (the S5 tracker does distinguish them).

The lengths are bounded by `wordOf g₁` and `wordOf g₂` for some collision
pair `g₁ ≠ g₂` in S5, which by the surjectivity proof are finite.

theorems.source_text:
/-- **Existential `T(d)` form.** For any recognizer with strictly fewer than
120 states, there exists a finite length `T` and two input words `w, w'`
both of length at most `T`, such that the recognizer cannot tell them apart
but the S5 tracker does. The length `T` depends on the specific recognizer
via the section `wordOf`. -/
theorem exists_T_distinguishing_word_pair
    (M : FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer AdjacentGenerator)
    (hcard : Fintype.card M.State < 120) :
    ∃ (T : ℕ) (w w' : List AdjacentGenerator),
      w.length ≤ T ∧ w'.length ≤ T ∧
      M.run w = M.run w' ∧ run w ≠ run w' := by
  obtain ⟨w, w', hEq, hNe⟩ := exists_distinguishing_word_pair M hcard
  exact ⟨max w.length w'.length, w, w',
    le_max_left _ _, le_max_right _ _, hEq, hNe⟩
theorems.signature:
∀ (M : S5Witness.FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator),
  Fintype.card M.State < 120 →
    ∃ T w w', w.length ≤ T ∧ w'.length ≤ T ∧ M.run w = M.run w' ∧ S5Tracker.run w ≠ S5Tracker.run w'
theorems.proof_body:
fun M hcard =>
  Exists.casesOn (S5Inseparability.exists_distinguishing_word_pair M hcard) fun w h =>
    Exists.casesOn h fun w' h =>
      And.casesOn h fun hEq hNe =>
        Exists.intro (max w.length w'.length)
          (Exists.intro w
            (Exists.intro w'
              ⟨le_max_left w.length w'.length, ⟨le_max_right w.length w'.length, ⟨hEq, hNe⟩⟩⟩))
theorems.name: S5Inseparability.exists_T_distinguishing_word_pair
theorems.doc:
**Existential `T(d)` form.** For any recognizer with strictly fewer than
120 states, there exists a finite length `T` and two input words `w, w'`
both of length at most `T`, such that the recognizer cannot tell them apart
but the S5 tracker does. The length `T` depends on the specific recognizer
via the section `wordOf`.

theorems.source_text:
/-- **Two-sided capacity inseparability.** The 120-state ceiling is tight:

* (Negative) Any recognizer with `< 120` states fails to realize the S5
  tracker.
* (Positive) The S5 tracker recognizer itself has exactly 120 states and
  realizes the S5 tracker (trivially, with `decode = id`).

This is the cleanest paper-facing form of the existential
S5-coset inseparability target with `T = ∞` (universal over all input
words) and capacity bound `N = 120`. The bridge to the explicit-precision
raw-write RNN class (NDM realizes at `d = 12` via
`NDMRealizesS5.emender_realizes_s5_tracker`) remains open;
see `LEAN_FRONTIER.md` for the obstruction inventory. -/
theorem emender_m2rnn_s5_inseparability_two_sided :
    -- Negative side
    (∀ (R : RealizesS5Tracker), 120 ≤ Fintype.card R.M.State) ∧
    -- Positive side: 120 states suffice
    Fintype.card S5Tracker.recognizer.State = 120 := by
  refine ⟨capacity_lower_bound_120, ?_⟩
  exact S5Tracker.recognizer_state_count
theorems.signature:
(∀ (R : S5Inseparability.RealizesS5Tracker), 120 ≤ Fintype.card R.M.State) ∧
  Fintype.card S5Tracker.recognizer.State = 120
theorems.proof_body: ⟨S5Inseparability.capacity_lower_bound_120, S5Tracker.recognizer_state_count⟩
theorems.name: S5Inseparability.emender_m2rnn_s5_inseparability_two_sided

This is the cleanest paper-facing form of the existential

theorems.source_text:
/-- **Concrete realizer witness.** The S5 tracker recognizer itself realizes
the S5 tracker via the identity decoder. This confirms the 120-state ceiling
is tight: there exists a recognizer with exactly 120 states that realizes
the tracker. -/
def trackerRealizer : RealizesS5Tracker where
  M := S5Tracker.recognizer
  decode := id
  realizes w := by
    simpa using (recognizer_run_eq w)
theorems.signature: S5Inseparability.RealizesS5Tracker
theorems.proof_body: { M := S5Tracker.recognizer, decode := id, realizes := S5Inseparability.trackerRealizer._proof_1 }

theorem trackerRealizer_card_eq_120 :
    Fintype.card trackerRealizer.M.State = 120 :=
  S5Tracker.recognizer_state_count

theorems.source_text:
/-- **Final headline form, `emender_m2rnn_s5_inseparability`** — the bound is
tight: 120 states are necessary and sufficient for finite-state realization
of the S5 adjacent-transposition tracker.

* **Necessity:** any realizer has at least 120 states
  (`capacity_lower_bound_120`).
* **Sufficiency:** the explicit `trackerRealizer` has exactly 120 states.

This is the existential capacity form of the v18 target
`emender_m2rnn_s5_inseparability`. The full explicit `T(d)` polynomial
bound for bounded-precision raw-write RNNs remains open; see
`LEAN_FRONTIER.md` for the obstruction inventory. -/
theorem emender_m2rnn_s5_inseparability :
    (∀ (R : RealizesS5Tracker.{0}), 120 ≤ Fintype.card R.M.State) ∧
    (∃ (R : RealizesS5Tracker.{0}), Fintype.card R.M.State = 120) := by
  refine ⟨capacity_lower_bound_120, ⟨trackerRealizer, trackerRealizer_card_eq_120⟩⟩
theorems.signature:
(∀ (R : S5Inseparability.RealizesS5Tracker), 120 ≤ Fintype.card R.M.State) ∧
  ∃ R, Fintype.card R.M.State = 120
theorems.proof_body:
⟨S5Inseparability.capacity_lower_bound_120,
  Exists.intro S5Inseparability.trackerRealizer S5Inseparability.trackerRealizer_card_eq_120⟩
theorems.name: S5Inseparability.emender_m2rnn_s5_inseparability

theorems.source_text:
/-- **THEOREM 1: E88 matrix state has D² degrees of freedom**

Each E88 head state is a D×D matrix. The index set for its entries is
`Fin D × Fin D`, which has cardinality D * D = D².

This quantifies the number of independent real parameters stored per E88 head. -/
theorem e88_capacity_d_squared (d : ℕ) :
    Fintype.card (Fin d × Fin d) = d * d := by
  simp [Fintype.card_prod, Fintype.card_fin]
theorems.signature: ∀ (d : ℕ), Fintype.card (Fin d × Fin d) = d * d
theorems.proof_body:
fun d =>
  of_eq_true
    (Eq.trans
      (congrArg (fun x => x = d * d)
        (Eq.trans (Fintype.card_prod (Fin d) (Fin d))
          (congr (congrArg HMul.hMul (Fintype.card_fin d)) (Fintype.card_fin d))))
      (eq_self (d * d)))
theorems.name: CapacitySeparation.e88_capacity_d_squared
theorems.doc:

This quantifies the number of independent real parameters stored per E88 head.

theorems.source_text:
/-- **THEOREM 2: E1H vector state has D degrees of freedom**

Each E1H head state is a D-dimensional vector. The index set is `Fin D`,
which has cardinality D.

This quantifies the number of independent real parameters stored per E1H head. -/
theorem e1h_capacity_d (d : ℕ) :
    Fintype.card (Fin d) = d := Fintype.card_fin d
theorems.signature: ∀ (d : ℕ), Fintype.card (Fin d) = d
theorems.proof_body: fun d => Fintype.card_fin d
theorems.name: CapacitySeparation.e1h_capacity_d

theorems.source_text:
/-- For D ≥ 2, E88 has strictly more degrees of freedom than E1H:
    D < D² (equivalently, D < D * D when D ≥ 2) -/
theorem e88_dof_strictly_exceeds_e1h (d : ℕ) (hd : d ≥ 2) :
    Fintype.card (Fin d) < Fintype.card (Fin d × Fin d) := by
  simp [Fintype.card_prod, Fintype.card_fin]
  nlinarith
theorems.signature: ∀ d ≥ 2, Fintype.card (Fin d) < Fintype.card (Fin d × Fin d)
theorems.proof_body:
fun d hd =>
  Eq.mpr
    (id
      (Eq.trans
        (congr (congrArg LT.lt (Fintype.card_fin d))
          (Eq.trans (Fintype.card_prod (Fin d) (Fin d))
            (congr (congrArg HMul.hMul (Fintype.card_fin d)) (Fintype.card_fin d))))
        Nat.lt_mul_self_iff._simp_1))
    (lt_of_not_ge fun a =>
      Mathlib.Tactic.Linarith.lt_irrefl
        (Eq.mp
          (congrArg (fun _a => _a < 0)
            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.of_eq
              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_congr
                (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_congr
                  (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.neg_congr
                    (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.cast_pos (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isNat_ofNat ℤ (Eq.refl 1)))
                    (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.neg_add
                      (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.neg_one_mul
                        (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.IsInt.to_raw_eq
                          (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isInt_mul (Eq.refl HMul.hMul)
                            (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.IsInt.of_raw ℤ (Int.negOfNat 1))
                            (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.IsNat.to_isInt
                              (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.IsNat.of_raw ℤ 1))
                            (Eq.refl (Int.negOfNat 1)))))
                      Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.neg_zero))
                  (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.sub_congr
                    (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.cast_pos (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isNat_ofNat ℤ (Eq.refl 2)))
                    (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.atom_pf ↑d)
                    (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.sub_pf
                      (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.neg_add
                        (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.neg_mul (↑d) (Nat.rawCast 1)
                          (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.neg_one_mul
                            (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.IsInt.to_raw_eq
                              (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isInt_mul (Eq.refl HMul.hMul)
                                (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.IsInt.of_raw ℤ (Int.negOfNat 1))
                                (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.IsNat.to_isInt
                                  (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.IsNat.of_raw ℤ 1))
                                (Eq.refl (Int.negOfNat 1))))))
                        Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.neg_zero)
                      (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_pf_add_lt (Nat.rawCast 2)
                        (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_pf_zero_add
                          (↑d ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * (Int.negOfNat 1).rawCast + 0)))))
                  (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_pf_add_overlap
                    (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.IsNat.to_raw_eq
                      (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.IsInt.to_isNat
                        (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isInt_add (Eq.refl HAdd.hAdd)
                          (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.IsInt.of_raw ℤ (Int.negOfNat 1))
                          (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.IsNat.to_isInt
                            (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.IsNat.of_raw ℤ 2))
                          (Eq.refl (Int.ofNat 1)))))
                    (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_pf_zero_add
                      (↑d ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * (Int.negOfNat 1).rawCast + 0))))
                (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.sub_congr (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.atom_pf ↑d)
                  (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.cast_pos (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isNat_ofNat ℤ (Eq.refl 1)))
                  (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.sub_pf
                    (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.neg_add
                      (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.neg_one_mul
                        (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.IsInt.to_raw_eq
                          (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isInt_mul (Eq.refl HMul.hMul)
                            (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.IsInt.of_raw ℤ (Int.negOfNat 1))
                            (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.IsNat.to_isInt
                              (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.IsNat.of_raw ℤ 1))
                            (Eq.refl (Int.negOfNat 1)))))
                      Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.neg_zero)
                    (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_pf_add_gt (Int.negOfNat 1).rawCast
                      (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_pf_add_zero
                        (↑d ^ Nat.rawCast 1 * Nat.rawCast 1 + 0)))))
                (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_pf_add_overlap_zero
                  (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.IsInt.to_isNat
                    (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isInt_add (Eq.refl HAdd.hAdd)
                      (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.IsNat.to_isInt (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.IsNat.of_raw ℤ 1))
                      (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.IsInt.of_raw ℤ (Int.negOfNat 1))
                      (Eq.refl (Int.ofNat 0))))
                  (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_pf_add_overlap_zero
                    (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_overlap_pf_zero (↑d) (Nat.rawCast 1)
                      (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.IsInt.to_isNat
                        (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isInt_add (Eq.refl HAdd.hAdd)
                          (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.IsInt.of_raw ℤ (Int.negOfNat 1))
                          (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.IsNat.to_isInt
                            (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.IsNat.of_raw ℤ 1))
                          (Eq.refl (Int.ofNat 0)))))
                    (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_pf_zero_add 0))))
              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.cast_zero (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isNat_ofNat ℤ (Eq.refl 0)))))
          (Mathlib.Tactic.Linarith.add_lt_of_neg_of_le
            (Mathlib.Tactic.Linarith.add_lt_of_neg_of_le
              (neg_neg_of_pos Mathlib.Tactic.Linarith.zero_lt_one)
              (Mathlib.Tactic.Linarith.sub_nonpos_of_le
                (id (Eq.mp (Mathlib.Tactic.Zify.natCast_le._simp_1 2 d) hd))))
            (Mathlib.Tactic.Linarith.sub_nonpos_of_le
              (id
                (Eq.mp
                  (Eq.trans (Mathlib.Tactic.Zify.natCast_le._simp_1 d 1)
                    (congrArg (LE.le ↑d) Nat.cast_one))
                  a))))))

theorems.source_text:
/-- E88 content-addressable read: compute S · q

E88's state S (a D×D matrix) can be "queried" with a vector q by computing
the matrix-vector product S · q. This is the fundamental retrieval mechanism:
- If S accumulated outer products v_k ⊗ k^T for key-value pairs (k, v_k),
  then S · q selects values weighted by key-query similarity. -/
noncomputable def e88ContentRead {d : ℕ} (S : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) ℝ)
    (q : Fin d → ℝ) : Fin d → ℝ :=
  S.mulVec q
theorems.signature: {d : ℕ} → Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) ℝ → (Fin d → ℝ) → Fin d → ℝ
theorems.proof_body: fun {d} S q => S.mulVec q
theorems.name: CapacitySeparation.e88ContentRead
theorems.doc:
E88 content-addressable read: compute S · q

E88's state S (a D×D matrix) can be "queried" with a vector q by computing
the matrix-vector product S · q. This is the fundamental retrieval mechanism:
- If S accumulated outer products v_k ⊗ k^T for key-value pairs (k, v_k),
  then S · q selects values weighted by key-query similarity.

theorems.source_text:
/-- Helper: inner product of orthogonal basis vectors is 0.
For the standard basis vectors e₀ (= 1 at index 0, 0 elsewhere) and
e₁ (= 1 at index 1, 0 elsewhere), their inner product is 0. -/
private theorem basis_inner_product_zero (d : ℕ) :
    ∑ j : Fin d, (if j.val = 0 then (1:ℝ) else 0) * (if j.val = 1 then 1 else 0) = 0 := by
  apply Finset.sum_eq_zero
  intro j _
  split_ifs with h0 h1
  · -- j.val = 0 and j.val = 1: impossible
    exact absurd (h0.symm.trans h1) (by decide)
  · ring
  · ring
  · ring
theorems.signature: ∀ (d : ℕ), (∑ j, (if ↑j = 0 then 1 else 0) * if ↑j = 1 then 1 else 0) = 0
theorems.proof_body:
fun d =>
  Finset.sum_eq_zero fun j a =>
    if h0 : ↑j = 0 then
      Eq.mpr (id (congrArg (fun x => (x * if ↑j = 1 then 1 else 0) = 0) (if_pos h0)))
        (if h1 : ↑j = 1 then
          Eq.mpr (id (congrArg (fun x => 1 * x = 0) (if_pos h1)))
            (absurd (Eq.trans (Eq.symm h0) h1) (of_decide_eq_true (id (Eq.refl true))))
        else
          Eq.mpr (id (congrArg (fun x => 1 * x = 0) (if_neg h1)))
            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.of_eq
              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_congr
                (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.cast_pos (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isNat_ofNat ℝ Nat.cast_one))
                (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.cast_zero (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isNat_ofNat ℝ Nat.cast_zero))
                (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_mul (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_zero (Nat.rawCast 1))
                  (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.zero_mul 0) (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_pf_zero_add 0)))
              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.cast_zero (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isNat_ofNat ℝ Nat.cast_zero))))
    else
      Eq.mpr (id (congrArg (fun x => (x * if ↑j = 1 then 1 else 0) = 0) (if_neg h0)))
        (if h : ↑j = 1 then
          Eq.mpr (id (congrArg (fun x => 0 * x = 0) (if_pos h)))
            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.of_eq
              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_congr
                (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.cast_zero (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isNat_ofNat ℝ Nat.cast_zero))
                (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.cast_pos (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isNat_ofNat ℝ Nat.cast_one))
                (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.zero_mul (Nat.rawCast 1 + 0)))
              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.cast_zero (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isNat_ofNat ℝ Nat.cast_zero)))
        else
          Eq.mpr (id (congrArg (fun x => 0 * x = 0) (if_neg h)))
            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.of_eq
              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_congr
                (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.cast_zero (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isNat_ofNat ℝ Nat.cast_zero))
                (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.cast_zero (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isNat_ofNat ℝ Nat.cast_zero))
                (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.zero_mul 0))
              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.cast_zero (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isNat_ofNat ℝ Nat.cast_zero))))
theorems.name: _private.ElmanProofs.Expressivity.E88ExceedsE1HCapacity.0.CapacitySeparation.basis_inner_product_zero
theorems.doc:
Helper: inner product of orthogonal basis vectors is 0.
For the standard basis vectors e₀ (= 1 at index 0, 0 elsewhere) and
e₁ (= 1 at index 1, 0 elsewhere), their inner product is 0.

theorems.source_text:
/-- Helper: inner product of e₀ with itself is 1. -/
private theorem basis_self_inner_product (d : ℕ) (hd0 : 0 < d) :
    ∑ j : Fin d, (if j.val = 0 then (1:ℝ) else 0) * (if j.val = 0 then 1 else 0) = 1 := by
  -- Simplify (if p then 1 else 0) * (if p then 1 else 0) = if p then 1 else 0
  have h_sq : ∀ j : Fin d, (if j.val = 0 then (1:ℝ) else 0) * (if j.val = 0 then 1 else 0) =
                             if j.val = 0 then 1 else 0 := fun j => by split_ifs <;> ring
  simp_rw [h_sq]
  -- Convert j.val = 0 condition to j = ⟨0, hd0⟩ for Fin d
  have h_conv : ∀ j : Fin d, (if j.val = 0 then (1:ℝ) else 0) = if j = ⟨0, hd0⟩ then 1 else 0 :=
    fun j => by simp [Fin.ext_iff]
  simp_rw [h_conv]
  -- Apply sum_ite_eq: ∑ j in univ, if j = a then f j else 0 = if a ∈ univ then f a else 0
  simp
theorems.signature: ∀ (d : ℕ), 0 < d → (∑ j, (if ↑j = 0 then 1 else 0) * if ↑j = 0 then 1 else 0) = 1
theorems.proof_body:
fun d hd0 =>
  have h_sq := fun j =>
    if h : ↑j = 0 then
      Eq.mpr
        (id (congr (congrArg Eq (congr (congrArg HMul.hMul (if_pos h)) (if_pos h))) (if_pos h)))
        (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.of_eq
          (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_congr
            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.cast_pos (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isNat_ofNat ℝ Nat.cast_one))
            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.cast_pos (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isNat_ofNat ℝ Nat.cast_one))
            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_mul
              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_add (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.one_mul (Nat.rawCast 1))
                (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_zero (Nat.rawCast 1))
                (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_pf_add_zero (Nat.rawCast 1 + 0)))
              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.zero_mul (Nat.rawCast 1 + 0))
              (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.add_pf_add_zero (Nat.rawCast 1 + 0))))
          (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.cast_pos (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isNat_ofNat ℝ Nat.cast_one)))
    else
      Eq.mpr
        (id (congr (congrArg Eq (congr (congrArg HMul.hMul (if_neg h)) (if_neg h))) (if_neg h)))
        (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.of_eq
          (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.mul_congr
            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.cast_zero (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isNat_ofNat ℝ Nat.cast_zero))
            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.cast_zero (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isNat_ofNat ℝ Nat.cast_zero))
            (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.zero_mul 0))
          (Mathlib.Tactic.Ring.cast_zero (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isNat_ofNat ℝ Nat.cast_zero)));
  Eq.mpr
    (id
      (congrArg (fun x => x = 1)
        (Finset.sum_congr (Eq.refl Finset.univ) fun x a => (fun j => h_sq j) x)))
    (have h_conv := fun j =>
      of_eq_true
        (Eq.trans
          (congrArg (Eq (if ↑j = 0 then 1 else 0))
            (ite_congr CapacitySeparation.basis_self_inner_product._simp_1_1✝ (fun a => Eq.refl 1)
              fun a => Eq.refl 0))
          (eq_self (if ↑j = 0 then 1 else 0)));
    Eq.mpr
      (id
        (congrArg (fun x => x = 1)
          (Finset.sum_congr (Eq.refl Finset.univ) fun x a => (fun j => h_conv j) x)))
      (of_eq_true
        (Eq.trans
          (congrArg (fun x => x = 1)
            (Eq.trans (Finset.sum_ite_eq' Finset.univ ⟨0, hd0⟩ fun x => 1)
              (ite_cond_eq_true 1 0 (Finset.mem_univ._simp_1 ⟨0, hd0⟩))))
          (eq_self 1))))

theorems.source_text:
/-- **THEOREM 3: E88 supports S·q content-based retrieval that E1H cannot**

We formally demonstrate E88's content-addressable retrieval by constructing:
- A state S = e₀ ⊗ e₀ᵀ (outer product of the first standard basis vector)
- Two queries e₀ ≠ e₁ (orthogonal basis vectors)
- S · e₀ = e₀ (nonzero: retrieves the stored association)
- S · e₁ = 0   (zero: orthogonal query finds nothing)

So different queries retrieve different information from the same state.

**Why E1H cannot do this**: E1H's state is a D-vector h, not a matrix.
Any "read" operation on h produces the same information (or a fixed linear
projection thereof) regardless of any "query" vector — there is no matrix
to multiply against, so no key-based addressing is possible.

This difference explains why E88 can implement associative memory patterns
(key-value retrieval) that E1H fundamentally cannot. -/
theorem e88_addressable_e1h_not (d : ℕ) (hd : d ≥ 2) :
    ∃ (S : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) ℝ) (q₁ q₂ : Fin d → ℝ),
      q₁ ≠ q₂ ∧ e88ContentRead S q₁ ≠ e88ContentRead S q₂ := by
  have hd0 : 0 < d := by omega
  have hd1 : 1 < d := by omega
  -- Standard basis vectors: e₀ = 1 at index 0, e₁ = 1 at index 1
  let e₀ : Fin d → ℝ := fun i => if i.val = 0 then 1 else 0
  let e₁ : Fin d → ℝ := fun i => if i.val = 1 then 1 else 0
  -- E88 state: S = e₀ ⊗ e₀ᵀ (rank-1 outer product storing e₀ → e₀ association)
  let S : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) ℝ := Matrix.of (fun i j => e₀ i * e₀ j)
  use S, e₀, e₁
  constructor
  · -- e₀ ≠ e₁: they differ at index 0 (e₀(0) = 1, e₁(0) = 0)
    intro h
    have h00 := congr_fun h ⟨0, hd0⟩
    simp only [e₀, e₁] at h00
    norm_num at h00
  · -- e88ContentRead S e₀ ≠ e88ContentRead S e₁
    -- We show they differ at coordinate 0:
    --   (S · e₀)[0] = (e₀ ⊗ e₀ᵀ · e₀)[0] = e₀[0] · ‖e₀‖² = 1 · 1 = 1
    --   (S · e₁)[0] = (e₀ ⊗ e₀ᵀ · e₁)[0] = e₀[0] · (e₀ · e₁) = 1 · 0 = 0
    intro h_reads_eq
    -- Compute (S · e₀) at index 0 = 1
    have h_read0 : e88ContentRead S e₀ ⟨0, hd0⟩ = 1 := by
      simp only [e88ContentRead, Matrix.mulVec, dotProduct, S, Matrix.of_apply, e₀]
      -- Sum is: ∑ j, (if 0 = 0 then 1 else 0) * (if j.val = 0 then 1 else 0) *
      --                                          (if j.val = 0 then 1 else 0)
      -- = 1 * ∑ j, (if j.val = 0 then 1)²  = 1 * 1 = 1
      simp only [ite_true, one_mul]
      exact basis_self_inner_product d hd0
    -- Compute (S · e₁) at index 0 = 0
    have h_read1 : e88ContentRead S e₁ ⟨0, hd0⟩ = 0 := by
      simp only [e88ContentRead, Matrix.mulVec, dotProduct, S, Matrix.of_apply, e₀]
      -- Sum is: ∑ j, (if 0 = 0 then 1) * (if j.val = 0 then 1) * (if j.val = 1 then 1)
      -- = 1 * ∑ j, (if j.val = 0 then 1) * (if j.val = 1 then 1) = 1 * 0 = 0
      simp only [ite_true, one_mul]
      exact basis_inner_product_zero d
    -- Contradiction: from h_reads_eq, 1 = 0
    have := congr_fun h_reads_eq ⟨0, hd0⟩
    rw [h_read0, h_read1] at this
    norm_num at this
theorems.signature:
∀ d ≥ 2,
  ∃ S q₁ q₂,
    q₁ ≠ q₂ ∧ CapacitySeparation.e88ContentRead S q₁ ≠ CapacitySeparation.e88ContentRead S q₂
theorems.proof_body:
fun d hd =>
  have hd0 :=
    Decidable.byContradiction fun a => CapacitySeparation.e88_addressable_e1h_not._proof_1_1 d hd a;
  have hd1 :=
    Decidable.byContradiction fun a => CapacitySeparation.e88_addressable_e1h_not._proof_1_2 d hd a;
  let e₀ := fun i => if ↑i = 0 then 1 else 0;
  let e₁ := fun i => if ↑i = 1 then 1 else 0;
  let S := Matrix.of fun i j => e₀ i * e₀ j;
  Exists.intro S
    (Exists.intro e₀
      (Exists.intro e₁
        ⟨fun h =>
          have h00 := congr_fun h ⟨0, hd0⟩;
          False.elim
            (Eq.mp
              (Eq.trans
                (Eq.trans
                  (congr
                    (congrArg Eq
                      (ite_congr (eq_true True.intro) (fun a => Eq.refl 1) fun a => Eq.refl 0))
                    (ite_congr
                      (eq_false
                        (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isNat_eq_false
                          (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isNat_ofNat ℕ (Eq.refl 0))
                          (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isNat_ofNat ℕ (Eq.refl 1)) (Eq.refl false)))
                      (fun a => Eq.refl 1) fun a => Eq.refl 0))
                  one_ne_zero._simp_1)
                (eq_false not_false))
              (Eq.mp
                (congrArg (fun x => x = if 0 = 1 then 1 else 0)
                  (ite_congr (eq_self 0) (fun a => Eq.refl 1) fun a => Eq.refl 0))
                h00)),
          fun h_reads_eq =>
          have h_read0 :=
            Eq.mpr
              (id
                (congrArg (fun x => x = 1)
                  (Finset.sum_congr (Eq.refl Finset.univ) fun x a =>
                    congrArg (fun x_1 => (x_1 * if ↑x = 0 then 1 else 0) * if ↑x = 0 then 1 else 0)
                      (ite_congr (eq_self 0) (fun a => Eq.refl 1) fun a => Eq.refl 0))))
              (Eq.mpr
                (id
                  (congrArg (fun x => x = 1)
                    (Finset.sum_congr (Eq.refl Finset.univ) fun x a =>
                      congrArg (fun x_1 => x_1 * if ↑x = 0 then 1 else 0)
                        (one_mul (if ↑x = 0 then 1 else 0)))))
                (CapacitySeparation.basis_self_inner_product✝ d hd0));
          have h_read1 :=
            Eq.mpr
              (id
                (congrArg (fun x => x = 0)
                  (Finset.sum_congr (Eq.refl Finset.univ) fun x a =>
                    congrArg (fun x_1 => (x_1 * if ↑x = 0 then 1 else 0) * e₁ x)
                      (ite_congr (eq_self 0) (fun a => Eq.refl 1) fun a => Eq.refl 0))))
              (Eq.mpr
                (id
                  (congrArg (fun x => x = 0)
                    (Finset.sum_congr (Eq.refl Finset.univ) fun x a =>
                      congrArg (fun x_1 => x_1 * e₁ x) (one_mul (if ↑x = 0 then 1 else 0)))))
                (CapacitySeparation.basis_inner_product_zero✝ d));
          have this := congr_fun h_reads_eq ⟨0, hd0⟩;
          False.elim
            (Eq.mp
              (eq_false
                (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isNat_eq_false
                  (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isNat_ofNat ℝ Nat.cast_one)
                  (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isNat_ofNat ℝ Nat.cast_zero) (Eq.refl false)))
              (Eq.mp (congrArg (fun _a => 1 = _a) h_read1)
                (Eq.mp
                  (congrArg (fun _a => _a = CapacitySeparation.e88ContentRead S e₁ ⟨0, hd0⟩)
                    h_read0)
                  this)))⟩))
theorems.name: CapacitySeparation.e88_addressable_e1h_not
theorems.doc:
**THEOREM 3: E88 supports S·q content-based retrieval that E1H cannot**

We formally demonstrate E88's content-addressable retrieval by constructing:
- A state S = e₀ ⊗ e₀ᵀ (outer product of the first standard basis vector)
- Two queries e₀ ≠ e₁ (orthogonal basis vectors)
- S · e₀ = e₀ (nonzero: retrieves the stored association)
- S · e₁ = 0   (zero: orthogonal query finds nothing)

So different queries retrieve different information from the same state.

**Why E1H cannot do this**: E1H's state is a D-vector h, not a matrix.
Any "read" operation on h produces the same information (or a fixed linear
projection thereof) regardless of any "query" vector — there is no matrix
to multiply against, so no key-based addressing is possible.

This difference explains why E88 can implement associative memory patterns

theorems.source_text:
/-- **THEOREM 4: Capacity Separation**

For D ≥ 2, E88 state scalars per head (D²) strictly exceeds E1H state
scalars per head (D). This is the quantitative foundation explaining E88's
empirical superiority over E1H.

The extra D² - D = D(D-1) scalars per head enable:
- Richer associative memories (multiple key-value pairs per head)
- Content-addressable retrieval (S·q) not available to E1H
- More complex temporal patterns preserved across the sequence -/
theorem capacity_separation (d : ℕ) (hd : d ≥ 2) :
    E1H.e1hStateScalarsPerHead d < E1H.e88StateScalarsPerHead d :=
  E1H.e88_state_exceeds_e1h_state d hd
theorems.signature: ∀ d ≥ 2, E1H.e1hStateScalarsPerHead d < E1H.e88StateScalarsPerHead d
theorems.proof_body: fun d hd => E1H.e88_state_exceeds_e1h_state d hd
theorems.name: CapacitySeparation.capacity_separation

/-- For D ≥ 2, E88 total state capacity (H × D²) strictly exceeds E1H (H × D) -/
theorem total_capacity_separation (numHeads d : ℕ) (hH : numHeads ≥ 1) (hd : d ≥ 2) :
    E1H.e1hTotalState numHeads d < E1H.e88TotalState numHeads d :=
  E1H.e88_total_state_exceeds_e1h numHeads d hH hd

  E1H.e88_vs_e1h_capacity_ratio d

/-- For D = 2: E88 has 4 scalar entries per head, E1H has 2. -/
theorem separation_example_d2 :
    E1H.e88StateScalarsPerHead 2 = 4 ∧
    E1H.e1hStateScalarsPerHead 2 = 2 ∧
    E1H.e1hStateScalarsPerHead 2 < E1H.e88StateScalarsPerHead 2 := by
  simp [E1H.e88StateScalarsPerHead, E1H.e1hStateScalarsPerHead]
theorems.signature:
E1H.e88StateScalarsPerHead 2 = 4 ∧
  E1H.e1hStateScalarsPerHead 2 = 2 ∧ E1H.e1hStateScalarsPerHead 2 < E1H.e88StateScalarsPerHead 2
theorems.proof_body:
of_eq_true
  (Eq.trans
    (congr (congrArg And (eq_self 4))
      (Eq.trans (congr (congrArg And (eq_self 2)) (eq_true_of_decide (Eq.refl true)))
        (and_self True)))
    (and_self True))

theorems.source_text:
/-- The 4 standard basis matrices of ℝ^{2×2} are all distinct.

These are 4 distinct E88 states (when D=2) that any mapping to E1H's
2-dimensional state space must collapse (pigeonhole: 4 states → 2 bins
means at least 2 states are indistinguishable by E1H). -/
theorem four_distinct_e88_states_d2 :
    ∃ (S₁ S₂ S₃ S₄ : Matrix (Fin 2) (Fin 2) ℝ),
      S₁ ≠ S₂ ∧ S₁ ≠ S₃ ∧ S₁ ≠ S₄ ∧ S₂ ≠ S₃ ∧ S₂ ≠ S₄ ∧ S₃ ≠ S₄ := by
  -- The four standard basis matrices for ℝ^{2×2}: eᵢⱼ = 1 at (i,j), 0 elsewhere
  refine ⟨Matrix.of (fun i j : Fin 2 => if i.val = 0 ∧ j.val = 0 then (1:ℝ) else 0),
          Matrix.of (fun i j : Fin 2 => if i.val = 0 ∧ j.val = 1 then (1:ℝ) else 0),
          Matrix.of (fun i j : Fin 2 => if i.val = 1 ∧ j.val = 0 then (1:ℝ) else 0),
          Matrix.of (fun i j : Fin 2 => if i.val = 1 ∧ j.val = 1 then (1:ℝ) else 0),
          ?_, ?_, ?_, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
  -- For each pair, extract the distinguishing entry, then derive 1 = 0
  · intro h  -- e₀₀ ≠ e₀₁: differ at (0,0) where e₀₀=1 but e₀₁=0
    have := congr_fun (congr_fun h ⟨0, by norm_num⟩) ⟨0, by norm_num⟩
    simp [Matrix.of_apply] at this
  · intro h  -- e₀₀ ≠ e₁₀: differ at (0,0) where e₀₀=1 but e₁₀=0
    have := congr_fun (congr_fun h ⟨0, by norm_num⟩) ⟨0, by norm_num⟩
    simp [Matrix.of_apply] at this
  · intro h  -- e₀₀ ≠ e₁₁: differ at (0,0) where e₀₀=1 but e₁₁=0
    have := congr_fun (congr_fun h ⟨0, by norm_num⟩) ⟨0, by norm_num⟩
    simp [Matrix.of_apply] at this
  · intro h  -- e₀₁ ≠ e₁₀: differ at (0,1) where e₀₁=1 but e₁₀=0
    have := congr_fun (congr_fun h ⟨0, by norm_num⟩) ⟨1, by norm_num⟩
    simp [Matrix.of_apply] at this
  · intro h  -- e₀₁ ≠ e₁₁: differ at (0,1) where e₀₁=1 but e₁₁=0
    have := congr_fun (congr_fun h ⟨0, by norm_num⟩) ⟨1, by norm_num⟩
    simp [Matrix.of_apply] at this
  · intro h  -- e₁₀ ≠ e₁₁: differ at (1,0) where e₁₀=1 but e₁₁=0
    have := congr_fun (congr_fun h ⟨1, by norm_num⟩) ⟨0, by norm_num⟩
    simp [Matrix.of_apply] at this
theorems.signature: ∃ S₁ S₂ S₃ S₄, S₁ ≠ S₂ ∧ S₁ ≠ S₃ ∧ S₁ ≠ S₄ ∧ S₂ ≠ S₃ ∧ S₂ ≠ S₄ ∧ S₃ ≠ S₄
theorems.proof_body:
Exists.intro (Matrix.of fun i j => if ↑i = 0 ∧ ↑j = 0 then 1 else 0)
  (Exists.intro (Matrix.of fun i j => if ↑i = 0 ∧ ↑j = 1 then 1 else 0)
    (Exists.intro (Matrix.of fun i j => if ↑i = 1 ∧ ↑j = 0 then 1 else 0)
      (Exists.intro (Matrix.of fun i j => if ↑i = 1 ∧ ↑j = 1 then 1 else 0)
        ⟨fun h =>
          have this :=
            congr_fun
              (congr_fun h
                ⟨0,
                  Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isNat_lt_true
                    (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isNat_ofNat ℕ (Eq.refl 0))
                    (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isNat_ofNat ℕ (Eq.refl 2)) (Eq.refl false)⟩)
              ⟨0,
                Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isNat_lt_true (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isNat_ofNat ℕ (Eq.refl 0))
                  (Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isNat_ofNat ℕ (Eq.refl 2)) (Eq.refl false)⟩;
          False.elim
            (Eq.mp
              (Eq.trans
                (congr
                  (congrArg Eq
                    (Eq.trans
                      (congrArg (fun x => Matrix.of x 0 0)
                        (funext fun i =>
                          funext fun j =>
                            ite_congr
                              (congr (congrArg And Fin.val_eq_zero_iff._simp_1)
                                Fin.val_eq_zero_iff._simp_1)
                              (fun a => Eq.refl 1) fun a => Eq.refl 0))
                      (ite_cond_eq_true 1 0
                        (Eq.trans (congr (congrArg And (eq_self 0)) (eq_self 0)) (and_self True)))))
                  (Eq.trans
                    (congrArg (fun x => Matrix.of x 0 0)
                      (funext fun i =>
                        funext fun j =>
                          ite_congr (congrArg (fun x => x ∧ ↑j = 1) Fin.val_eq_zero_iff._simp_1)
                            (fun a => Eq.refl 1) fun a => Eq.refl 0))
                    (ite_cond_eq_false 1 0
                      (Eq.trans (congr (congrArg And (eq_self 0)) zero_ne_one._simp_1)
                        (and_false True)))))
                one_ne_zero._simp_1)
              this),
          ⟨fun h =>
            have this :=
              congr_fun
                (congr_fun h
                  ⟨0,
                    Mathlib.Meta.NormNum.isNat_lt_true

theorems.name: CapacitySeparation.four_distinct_e88_states_d2
theorems.doc:
The 4 standard basis matrices of ℝ^{2×2} are all distinct.

These are 4 distinct E88 states (when D=2) that any mapping to E1H's
2-dimensional state space must collapse (pigeonhole: 4 states → 2 bins
means at least 2 states are indistinguishable by E1H).

theorems.source_text:
/-- **MAIN SUMMARY: All capacity separation results**

E88 strictly exceeds E1H in state capacity for D ≥ 2:
1. E88 has D² scalar entries per head (vs D for E1H)
2. E88 supports content-addressable retrieval S·q (E1H cannot)
3. E88 state separates from E1H: D < D² for D ≥ 2

This is the state capacity half of why E88 empirically outperforms E1H
(1.19 vs 1.10 perplexity at 32K tokens). -/
theorem e88_exceeds_e1h_capacity_summary (d : ℕ) (hd : d ≥ 2) :
    -- (1) E88 has D² scalar degrees of freedom per head
    Fintype.card (Fin d × Fin d) = d * d ∧
    -- (2) E1H has D scalar degrees of freedom per head
    Fintype.card (Fin d) = d ∧
    -- (3) E88 has strictly more DOF: D < D²
    Fintype.card (Fin d) < Fintype.card (Fin d × Fin d) ∧
    -- (4) Content-addressable: E88 can distinguish different queries; E1H cannot
    (∃ (S : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) ℝ) (q₁ q₂ : Fin d → ℝ),
      q₁ ≠ q₂ ∧ e88ContentRead S q₁ ≠ e88ContentRead S q₂) ∧
    -- (5) Scalar count separation: D < D² (proven via E1HDefinition)
    E1H.e1hStateScalarsPerHead d < E1H.e88StateScalarsPerHead d := by
  exact ⟨e88_capacity_d_squared d,
         e1h_capacity_d d,
         e88_dof_strictly_exceeds_e1h d hd,
         e88_addressable_e1h_not d hd,
         capacity_separation d hd⟩
theorems.signature:
∀ d ≥ 2,
  Fintype.card (Fin d × Fin d) = d * d ∧
    Fintype.card (Fin d) = d ∧
      Fintype.card (Fin d) < Fintype.card (Fin d × Fin d) ∧
        (∃ S q₁ q₂,
            q₁ ≠ q₂ ∧
              CapacitySeparation.e88ContentRead S q₁ ≠ CapacitySeparation.e88ContentRead S q₂) ∧
          E1H.e1hStateScalarsPerHead d < E1H.e88StateScalarsPerHead d
theorems.proof_body:
fun d hd =>
  ⟨CapacitySeparation.e88_capacity_d_squared d,
    ⟨CapacitySeparation.e1h_capacity_d d,
      ⟨CapacitySeparation.e88_dof_strictly_exceeds_e1h d hd,
        ⟨CapacitySeparation.e88_addressable_e1h_not d hd,
          CapacitySeparation.capacity_separation d hd⟩⟩⟩⟩

Twelve gives ample headroom for four orthonormal generator keys placed at
distinct positions, with slack for state-tile widening. -/
abbrev D : ℕ := 12

theorems.source_text:
/-- Assign each adjacent generator to a distinct index in `Fin D`. The four
generators occupy positions `0, 1, 2, 3`; the remaining `Fin D` slots are
unused. -/
def genIndex : AdjacentGenerator → Fin D
  | .s01 => ⟨0, by decide⟩
  | .s12 => ⟨1, by decide⟩
  | .s23 => ⟨2, by decide⟩
  | .s34 => ⟨3, by decide⟩
theorems.signature: S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator → Fin NDMRealizesS5.D
theorems.proof_body:
fun x =>
  match x with
  | S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s01 => ⟨0, NDMRealizesS5.genIndex._proof_1⟩
  | S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s12 => ⟨1, NDMRealizesS5.genIndex._proof_2⟩
  | S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23 => ⟨2, NDMRealizesS5.genIndex._proof_3⟩
  | S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34 => ⟨3, NDMRealizesS5.genIndex._proof_4⟩
theorems.name: NDMRealizesS5.genIndex
theorems.doc:
Assign each adjacent generator to a distinct index in `Fin D`. The four
generators occupy positions `0, 1, 2, 3`; the remaining `Fin D` slots are
unused.

theorems.source_text:
/-- `genIndex` is injective: distinct adjacent generators land at distinct
positions of `Fin D`. -/
theorem genIndex_injective : Function.Injective genIndex := by
  intro a b h
  cases a <;> cases b <;> first | rfl | (simp [genIndex] at h)
theorems.signature: Function.Injective NDMRealizesS5.genIndex
theorems.proof_body:
fun ⦃a b⦄ h =>
  S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.casesOn (motive := fun t => a = t → a = b) a
    (fun h_1 =>
      Eq.ndrec (motive := fun ⦃a⦄ => NDMRealizesS5.genIndex a = NDMRealizesS5.genIndex b → a = b)
        (fun h =>
          S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.casesOn (motive := fun t =>
            b = t → S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s01 = b) b
            (fun h_2 =>
              Eq.ndrec (motive := fun ⦃b⦄ =>
                NDMRealizesS5.genIndex S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s01 = NDMRealizesS5.genIndex b →
                  S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s01 = b)
                (fun h => Eq.refl S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s01) (Eq.symm h_2) h)
            (fun h_2 =>
              Eq.ndrec (motive := fun ⦃b⦄ =>
                NDMRealizesS5.genIndex S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s01 = NDMRealizesS5.genIndex b →
                  S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s01 = b)
                (fun h => False.elim (Eq.mp zero_ne_one._simp_1 h)) (Eq.symm h_2) h)
            (fun h_2 =>
              Eq.ndrec (motive := fun ⦃b⦄ =>
                NDMRealizesS5.genIndex S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s01 = NDMRealizesS5.genIndex b →
                  S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s01 = b)
                (fun h =>
                  False.elim
                    (Eq.mp (Eq.trans Fin.zero_eq_mk._simp_1 (OfNat.ofNat_ne_zero._simp_1 2)) h))
                (Eq.symm h_2) h)
            (fun h_2 =>
              Eq.ndrec (motive := fun ⦃b⦄ =>
                NDMRealizesS5.genIndex S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s01 = NDMRealizesS5.genIndex b →
                  S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s01 = b)
                (fun h =>
                  False.elim
                    (Eq.mp (Eq.trans Fin.zero_eq_mk._simp_1 (OfNat.ofNat_ne_zero._simp_1 3)) h))
                (Eq.symm h_2) h)
            (Eq.refl b))
        (Eq.symm h_1) h)
    (fun h_1 =>
      Eq.ndrec (motive := fun ⦃a⦄ => NDMRealizesS5.genIndex a = NDMRealizesS5.genIndex b → a = b)
        (fun h =>
          S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.casesOn (motive := fun t =>
            b = t → S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s12 = b) b
            (fun h_2 =>
              Eq.ndrec (motive := fun ⦃b⦄ =>
                NDMRealizesS5.genIndex S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s12 = NDMRealizesS5.genIndex b →
                  S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s12 = b)
                (fun h => False.elim (Eq.mp one_ne_zero._simp_1 h)) (Eq.symm h_2) h)
            (fun h_2 =>
              Eq.ndrec (motive := fun ⦃b⦄ =>
                NDMRealizesS5.genIndex S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s12 = NDMRealizesS5.genIndex b →
                  S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s12 = b)
                (fun h => Eq.refl S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s12) (Eq.symm h_2) h)
            (fun h_2 =>
              Eq.ndrec (motive := fun ⦃b⦄ =>
                NDMRealizesS5.genIndex S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s12 = NDMRealizesS5.genIndex b →
                  S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s12 = b)
                (fun h =>
                  False.elim
                    (Eq.mp (Eq.trans Fin.one_eq_mk._simp_1 (OfNat.ofNat_ne_one._simp_1 2)) h))
                (Eq.symm h_2) h)
            (fun h_2 =>
              Eq.ndrec (motive := fun ⦃b⦄ =>
                NDMRealizesS5.genIndex S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s12 = NDMRealizesS5.genIndex b →
                  S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s12 = b)
                (fun h =>
                  False.elim
                    (Eq.mp (Eq.trans Fin.one_eq_mk._simp_1 (OfNat.ofNat_ne_one._simp_1 3)) h))
                (Eq.symm h_2) h)
            (Eq.refl b))
        (Eq.symm h_1) h)
    (fun h_1 =>
      Eq.ndrec (motive := fun ⦃a⦄ => NDMRealizesS5.genIndex a = NDMRealizesS5.genIndex b → a = b)
        (fun h =>
          S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.casesOn (motive := fun t =>
            b = t → S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23 = b) b
            (fun h_2 =>
              Eq.ndrec (motive := fun ⦃b⦄ =>
                NDMRealizesS5.genIndex S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23 = NDMRealizesS5.genIndex b →
                  S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23 = b)
                (fun h =>
                  False.elim
                    (Eq.mp (Eq.trans Fin.mk_eq_zero._simp_1 (OfNat.ofNat_ne_zero._simp_1 2)) h))
                (Eq.symm h_2) h)
            (fun h_2 =>
              Eq.ndrec (motive := fun ⦃b⦄ =>
                NDMRealizesS5.genIndex S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23 = NDMRealizesS5.genIndex b →
                  S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23 = b)
                (fun h =>
                  False.elim
                    (Eq.mp (Eq.trans Fin.mk_eq_one._simp_1 (OfNat.ofNat_ne_one._simp_1 2)) h))
                (Eq.symm h_2) h)
            (fun h_2 =>
              Eq.ndrec (motive := fun ⦃b⦄ =>
                NDMRealizesS5.genIndex S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23 = NDMRealizesS5.genIndex b →
                  S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23 = b)
                (fun h => Eq.refl S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23) (Eq.symm h_2) h)
            (fun h_2 =>
              Eq.ndrec (motive := fun ⦃b⦄ =>
                NDMRealizesS5.genIndex S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23 = NDMRealizesS5.genIndex b →
                  S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23 = b)
                (fun h =>
                  False.elim
                    (Eq.mp
                      (Eq.trans
                        (Fin.mk.injEq 2 NDMRealizesS5.genIndex._proof_3 3
                          NDMRealizesS5.genIndex._proof_4)
                        (eq_false_of_decide (Eq.refl false)))
                      h))
                (Eq.symm h_2) h)
            (Eq.refl b))
        (Eq.symm h_1) h)
    (fun h_1 =>
      Eq.ndrec (motive := fun ⦃a⦄ => NDMRealizesS5.genIndex a = NDMRealizesS5.genIndex b → a = b)
        (fun h =>
          S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.casesOn (motive := fun t =>
            b = t → S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34 = b) b
            (fun h_2 =>
              Eq.ndrec (motive := fun ⦃b⦄ =>
                NDMRealizesS5.genIndex S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34 = NDMRealizesS5.genIndex b →
                  S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34 = b)
                (fun h =>
                  False.elim
                    (Eq.mp (Eq.trans Fin.mk_eq_zero._simp_1 (OfNat.ofNat_ne_zero._simp_1 3)) h))
                (Eq.symm h_2) h)
            (fun h_2 =>
              Eq.ndrec (motive := fun ⦃b⦄ =>
                NDMRealizesS5.genIndex S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34 = NDMRealizesS5.genIndex b →
                  S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34 = b)
                (fun h =>
                  False.elim
                    (Eq.mp (Eq.trans Fin.mk_eq_one._simp_1 (OfNat.ofNat_ne_one._simp_1 3)) h))
                (Eq.symm h_2) h)
            (fun h_2 =>
              Eq.ndrec (motive := fun ⦃b⦄ =>
                NDMRealizesS5.genIndex S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34 = NDMRealizesS5.genIndex b →
                  S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34 = b)
                (fun h =>
                  False.elim
                    (Eq.mp
                      (Eq.trans
                        (Fin.mk.injEq 3 NDMRealizesS5.genIndex._proof_4 2
                          NDMRealizesS5.genIndex._proof_3)
                        (Nat.succ_ne_self._simp_1 2))
                      h))
                (Eq.symm h_2) h)
            (fun h_2 =>
              Eq.ndrec (motive := fun ⦃b⦄ =>
                NDMRealizesS5.genIndex S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34 = NDMRealizesS5.genIndex b →
                  S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34 = b)
                (fun h => Eq.refl S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34) (Eq.symm h_2) h)
            (Eq.refl b))
        (Eq.symm h_1) h)
    (Eq.refl a)
theorems.name: NDMRealizesS5.genIndex_injective
theorems.doc:
`genIndex` is injective: distinct adjacent generators land at distinct
positions of `Fin D`.

theorems.signature: S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator → OnlineMemory.KeyVec NDMRealizesS5.D
theorems.proof_body: fun g i => if i = NDMRealizesS5.genIndex g then 1 else 0
theorems.name: NDMRealizesS5.emenderKey
theorems.doc: Constructive orthonormal key family: one-hot at `genIndex g`.

theorems.source_text:
/-- Constructive value family: one-hot at `genIndex g`. Sharing the encoding
with the key family means the loaded memory's readout at `emenderKey g` recovers
exactly `emenderValue g`, which uniquely identifies the generator. -/
def emenderValue (g : AdjacentGenerator) : ValueVec D :=
  fun i => if i = genIndex g then (1 : Real) else 0
theorems.signature: S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator → OnlineMemory.ValueVec NDMRealizesS5.D
theorems.proof_body: fun g i => if i = NDMRealizesS5.genIndex g then 1 else 0
theorems.name: NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue
theorems.doc:
Constructive value family: one-hot at `genIndex g`. Sharing the encoding
with the key family means the loaded memory's readout at `emenderKey g` recovers
exactly `emenderValue g`, which uniquely identifies the generator.

theorems.source_text:
/-- Inner product of two one-hot generator keys: `1` if the same generator,
otherwise `0`. -/
theorem keyDot_emenderKey (g g' : AdjacentGenerator) :
    keyDot (emenderKey g) (emenderKey g') =
      if g = g' then (1 : Real) else 0 := by
  unfold keyDot emenderKey
  by_cases hgg : g = g'
  · subst hgg
    simp only [if_true]
    -- Σᵢ (if i = genIndex g then 1 else 0) * (if i = genIndex g then 1 else 0)
    -- collapses to the single term at i = genIndex g.
    have h_eq :
        (fun i : Fin D =>
            (if i = genIndex g then (1 : Real) else 0) *
              (if i = genIndex g then (1 : Real) else 0)) =
          fun i : Fin D => if i = genIndex g then (1 : Real) else 0 := by
      funext i
      by_cases h : i = genIndex g
      · simp [h]
      · simp [h]
    rw [h_eq]
    rw [Finset.sum_ite_eq' Finset.univ (genIndex g) (fun _ => (1 : Real))]
    simp
  · -- distinct generators: distinct positions, so every term in the sum is zero.
    simp only [hgg, if_false]
    have hne : genIndex g ≠ genIndex g' := fun h => hgg (genIndex_injective h)
    apply Finset.sum_eq_zero
    intro i _
    by_cases h1 : i = genIndex g
    · subst h1
      simp [hne]
    · simp [h1]

                    (if i = NDMRealizesS5.genIndex g then 1 else 0) *
                      if i = NDMRealizesS5.genIndex g then 1 else 0))
                (Eq.trans (ite_congr (eq_self g) (fun a => Eq.refl 1) fun a => Eq.refl 0)
                  (if_true 1 0))))
            (have h_eq :=
              funext fun i =>
                if h : i = NDMRealizesS5.genIndex g then
                  of_eq_true
                    (Eq.trans

                          (Eq.trans

                                (ite_cond_eq_true 1 0
                                  (Eq.trans (congrArg (fun x => x = NDMRealizesS5.genIndex g) h)
                                    (eq_self (NDMRealizesS5.genIndex g)))))
                              (ite_cond_eq_true 1 0
                                (Eq.trans (congrArg (fun x => x = NDMRealizesS5.genIndex g) h)
                                  (eq_self (NDMRealizesS5.genIndex g)))))
                            (mul_one 1)))
                        (ite_cond_eq_true 1 0
                          (Eq.trans (congrArg (fun x => x = NDMRealizesS5.genIndex g) h)
                            (eq_self (NDMRealizesS5.genIndex g)))))
                      (eq_self 1))
                else
                  of_eq_true
                    (Eq.trans
                      (congr
                        (congrArg Eq
                          (Eq.trans
                            (congr (congrArg HMul.hMul (ite_cond_eq_false 1 0 (eq_false h)))
                              (ite_cond_eq_false 1 0 (eq_false h)))
                            (mul_zero 0)))
                        (ite_cond_eq_false 1 0 (eq_false h)))
                      (eq_self 0));
            Eq.mpr (id (congrArg (fun _a => Finset.univ.sum _a = 1) h_eq))
              (Eq.mpr

                    (Finset.sum_ite_eq' Finset.univ (NDMRealizesS5.genIndex g) fun x => 1)))

                    (congrArg (fun x => x = 1)
                      (ite_cond_eq_true 1 0 (Finset.mem_univ._simp_1 (NDMRealizesS5.genIndex g))))

                    if i = NDMRealizesS5.genIndex g' then 1 else 0))
              (Eq.trans (ite_congr (eq_false hgg) (fun a => Eq.refl 1) fun a => Eq.refl 0)

                          (mul_zero 1)))

/-- The canonical `Fin 4` index per adjacent generator. -/
def generatorToFin4 : AdjacentGenerator → Fin 4
  | .s01 => ⟨0, by decide⟩
  | .s12 => ⟨1, by decide⟩
  | .s23 => ⟨2, by decide⟩
  | .s34 => ⟨3, by decide⟩
theorems.signature: S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator → Fin 4
theorems.proof_body:
fun x =>
  match x with
  | S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s01 => ⟨0, NDMRealizesS5.generatorToFin4._proof_1⟩
  | S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s12 => ⟨1, NDMRealizesS5.generatorToFin4._proof_2⟩
  | S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23 => ⟨2, NDMRealizesS5.generatorToFin4._proof_3⟩
  | S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34 => ⟨3, NDMRealizesS5.generatorToFin4._proof_4⟩
theorems.name: NDMRealizesS5.generatorToFin4
theorems.doc: The canonical `Fin 4` index per adjacent generator.

theorems.source_text:
/-- The inverse of `generatorToFin4`. -/
def fin4ToGenerator : Fin 4 → AdjacentGenerator
  | ⟨0, _⟩ => .s01
  | ⟨1, _⟩ => .s12
  | ⟨2, _⟩ => .s23
  | ⟨3, _⟩ => .s34
  | ⟨n + 4, h⟩ => absurd h (by omega)
theorems.signature: Fin 4 → S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator
theorems.proof_body:
fun x =>
  match x with
  | ⟨0, isLt⟩ => S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s01
  | ⟨1, isLt⟩ => S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s12
  | ⟨2, isLt⟩ => S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23
  | ⟨3, isLt⟩ => S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34
  | ⟨n.succ.succ.succ.succ, h⟩ => absurd h ⋯
theorems.name: NDMRealizesS5.fin4ToGenerator
theorems.doc: The inverse of `generatorToFin4`.

theorems.source_text:
theorem fin4ToGenerator_generatorToFin4 (g : AdjacentGenerator) :
    fin4ToGenerator (generatorToFin4 g) = g := by
  cases g <;> rfl
theorems.signature:
∀ (g : S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator),
  NDMRealizesS5.fin4ToGenerator (NDMRealizesS5.generatorToFin4 g) = g
theorems.proof_body:
fun g =>
  S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.casesOn (motive := fun t =>
    g = t → NDMRealizesS5.fin4ToGenerator (NDMRealizesS5.generatorToFin4 g) = g) g
    (fun h =>
      Eq.symm h ▸
        Eq.refl
          (NDMRealizesS5.fin4ToGenerator
            (NDMRealizesS5.generatorToFin4 S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s01)))
    (fun h =>
      Eq.symm h ▸
        Eq.refl
          (NDMRealizesS5.fin4ToGenerator
            (NDMRealizesS5.generatorToFin4 S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s12)))
    (fun h =>
      Eq.symm h ▸
        Eq.refl
          (NDMRealizesS5.fin4ToGenerator
            (NDMRealizesS5.generatorToFin4 S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23)))
    (fun h =>
      Eq.symm h ▸
        Eq.refl
          (NDMRealizesS5.fin4ToGenerator
            (NDMRealizesS5.generatorToFin4 S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34)))
    (Eq.refl g)

theorems.source_text:
theorem generatorToFin4_fin4ToGenerator (i : Fin 4) :
    generatorToFin4 (fin4ToGenerator i) = i := by
  match i with
  | ⟨0, _⟩ => rfl
  | ⟨1, _⟩ => rfl
  | ⟨2, _⟩ => rfl
  | ⟨3, _⟩ => rfl
  | ⟨n + 4, h⟩ => exact absurd h (by omega)
theorems.signature: ∀ (i : Fin 4), NDMRealizesS5.generatorToFin4 (NDMRealizesS5.fin4ToGenerator i) = i
theorems.proof_body:
fun i =>
  match i with
  | ⟨0, isLt⟩ => Eq.refl (NDMRealizesS5.generatorToFin4 (NDMRealizesS5.fin4ToGenerator ⟨0, isLt⟩))
  | ⟨1, isLt⟩ => Eq.refl (NDMRealizesS5.generatorToFin4 (NDMRealizesS5.fin4ToGenerator ⟨1, isLt⟩))
  | ⟨2, isLt⟩ => Eq.refl (NDMRealizesS5.generatorToFin4 (NDMRealizesS5.fin4ToGenerator ⟨2, isLt⟩))
  | ⟨3, isLt⟩ => Eq.refl (NDMRealizesS5.generatorToFin4 (NDMRealizesS5.fin4ToGenerator ⟨3, isLt⟩))
  | ⟨n.succ.succ.succ.succ, h⟩ => absurd h fun a => NDMRealizesS5.fin4ToGenerator._proof_3 n h

theorems.source_text:
/-- The `Fin 4`-indexed key family is orthonormal. -/
theorem emenderKeyFin_orthonormal : OrthonormalKeys emenderKeyFin := by
  intro i j
  unfold emenderKeyFin
  rw [keyDot_emenderKey]
  by_cases hij : i = j
  · subst hij; simp
  · have hne : fin4ToGenerator i ≠ fin4ToGenerator j := by
      intro h
      apply hij
      have := congrArg generatorToFin4 h
      rw [generatorToFin4_fin4ToGenerator, generatorToFin4_fin4ToGenerator] at this
      exact this
    simp [hij, hne]
theorems.signature: OnlineMemory.OrthonormalKeys NDMRealizesS5.emenderKeyFin
theorems.proof_body:
fun i j =>
  id
    (Eq.mpr
      (id
        (congrArg (fun _a => _a = if i = j then 1 else 0)
          (NDMRealizesS5.keyDot_emenderKey (NDMRealizesS5.fin4ToGenerator i)
            (NDMRealizesS5.fin4ToGenerator j))))
      (if hij : i = j then
        hij ▸
          of_eq_true
            (Eq.trans
              (congr
                (congrArg Eq (ite_cond_eq_true 1 0 (eq_self (NDMRealizesS5.fin4ToGenerator i))))
                (ite_cond_eq_true 1 0 (eq_self i)))
              (eq_self 1))
      else
        have hne := fun h =>
          hij
            (have this := congrArg NDMRealizesS5.generatorToFin4 h;
            Eq.mp (congrArg (fun _a => i = _a) (NDMRealizesS5.generatorToFin4_fin4ToGenerator j))
              (Eq.mp
                (congrArg
                  (fun _a => _a = NDMRealizesS5.generatorToFin4 (NDMRealizesS5.fin4ToGenerator j))
                  (NDMRealizesS5.generatorToFin4_fin4ToGenerator i))
                this));
        of_eq_true
          (Eq.trans
            (congr (congrArg Eq (ite_cond_eq_false 1 0 (eq_false hne)))
              (ite_cond_eq_false 1 0 (eq_false hij)))
            (eq_self 0))))
theorems.name: NDMRealizesS5.emenderKeyFin_orthonormal
theorems.doc: The `Fin 4`-indexed key family is orthonormal.

/-- Reading the value vector `emenderValue g` at index `genIndex g` is `1`. -/
theorem emenderValue_at_genIndex_self (g : AdjacentGenerator) :
    emenderValue g (genIndex g) = 1 := by
  simp [emenderValue]
theorems.signature: ∀ (g : S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator), NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue g (NDMRealizesS5.genIndex g) = 1
theorems.proof_body:
fun g =>
  of_eq_true
    (Eq.trans
      (congrArg (fun x => x = 1) (ite_cond_eq_true 1 0 (eq_self (NDMRealizesS5.genIndex g))))
      (eq_self 1))
