theorems.source_text:
/-- Reading the value vector `emenderValue g'` at index `genIndex g` is `0` when
the generators differ. -/
theorem emenderValue_at_genIndex_other
    (g g' : AdjacentGenerator) (h : g ≠ g') :
    emenderValue g' (genIndex g) = 0 := by
  unfold emenderValue
  have hne : genIndex g ≠ genIndex g' := fun heq => h (genIndex_injective heq)
  simp [hne]
theorems.signature:
∀ (g g' : S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator),
  g ≠ g' → NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue g' (NDMRealizesS5.genIndex g) = 0
theorems.proof_body:
fun g g' h =>
  id
    (have hne := fun heq => h (NDMRealizesS5.genIndex_injective heq);
    of_eq_true
      (Eq.trans (congrArg (fun x => x = 0) (ite_cond_eq_false 1 0 (eq_false hne))) (eq_self 0)))
theorems.name: NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue_at_genIndex_other
theorems.doc:
Reading the value vector `emenderValue g'` at index `genIndex g` is `0` when
the generators differ.

theorems.source_text:
/-- The value family is injective: distinct generators produce distinct value
vectors. -/
theorem emenderValue_injective : Function.Injective emenderValue := by
  intro a b hab
  by_contra hne
  have h := congrFun hab (genIndex a)
  rw [emenderValue_at_genIndex_self, emenderValue_at_genIndex_other a b hne] at h
  exact one_ne_zero h
theorems.signature: Function.Injective NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue
theorems.proof_body:
fun ⦃a b⦄ hab =>
  Decidable.byContradiction fun hne =>
    have h := congrFun hab (NDMRealizesS5.genIndex a);
    one_ne_zero
      (Eq.mp (congrArg (fun _a => 1 = _a) (NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue_at_genIndex_other a b hne))
        (Eq.mp
          (congrArg (fun _a => _a = NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue b (NDMRealizesS5.genIndex a))
            (NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue_at_genIndex_self a))
          h))
theorems.name: NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue_injective
theorems.doc:
The value family is injective: distinct generators produce distinct value
vectors.

theorems.source_text:
/-- Decode a value vector back to its adjacent transposition. For the four
expected inputs `emenderValue g`, this returns `transposition g`; on any other
vector the decoder returns the identity permutation.

This is the "decoder" half of the linear readout pipeline. The decoder is
noncomputable because it tests real-valued vector equality, which uses
classical logic. The downstream theorems only rely on its behavior on the
four `emenderValue g` inputs. -/
noncomputable def decode (vec : ValueVec D) : S5Tracker.State :=
  letI : Decidable (vec = emenderValue .s01) := Classical.propDecidable _
  letI : Decidable (vec = emenderValue .s12) := Classical.propDecidable _
  letI : Decidable (vec = emenderValue .s23) := Classical.propDecidable _
  letI : Decidable (vec = emenderValue .s34) := Classical.propDecidable _
  if vec = emenderValue .s01 then transposition .s01
  else if vec = emenderValue .s12 then transposition .s12
  else if vec = emenderValue .s23 then transposition .s23
  else if vec = emenderValue .s34 then transposition .s34
  else (1 : S5Tracker.State)
theorems.signature: OnlineMemory.ValueVec NDMRealizesS5.D → S5Tracker.State
theorems.proof_body:
fun vec =>
  if vec = NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s01 then
    S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s01
  else
    if vec = NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s12 then
      S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s12
    else
      if vec = NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23 then
        S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23
      else
        if vec = NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34 then
          S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34
        else 1
theorems.name: NDMRealizesS5.decode
theorems.doc:
Decode a value vector back to its adjacent transposition. For the four

vector the decoder returns the identity permutation.

This is the "decoder" half of the linear readout pipeline. The decoder is
noncomputable because it tests real-valued vector equality, which uses
classical logic. The downstream theorems only rely on its behavior on the
four `emenderValue g` inputs.

/-- The decoder recovers `transposition g` from each `emenderValue g`. -/
theorem decode_emenderValue (g : AdjacentGenerator) :
    decode (emenderValue g) = transposition g := by
  cases g with
  | s01 =>
    unfold decode
    simp
  | s12 =>
    unfold decode
    simp [emenderValue_s12_ne_s01]
  | s23 =>
    unfold decode
    simp [emenderValue_s23_ne_s01, emenderValue_s23_ne_s12]
  | s34 =>
    unfold decode
    simp [emenderValue_s34_ne_s01, emenderValue_s34_ne_s12, emenderValue_s34_ne_s23]
theorems.signature:
∀ (g : S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator),
  NDMRealizesS5.decode (NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue g) = S5Tracker.transposition g
theorems.proof_body:
fun g =>
  S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.casesOn (motive := fun t =>
    g = t → NDMRealizesS5.decode (NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue g) = S5Tracker.transposition g) g
    (fun h =>
      Eq.symm h ▸
        id
          (of_eq_true
            (Eq.trans
              (congrArg (fun x => x = S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s01)
                (ite_cond_eq_true (S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s01)
                  (if
                      NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s01 =
                        NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s12 then
                    S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s12
                  else
                    if
                        NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s01 =
                          NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23 then
                      S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23
                    else
                      if
                          NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s01 =
                            NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34 then
                        S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34
                      else 1)
                  (eq_self (NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s01))))
              (eq_self (S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s01)))))
    (fun h =>
      Eq.symm h ▸
        id
          (of_eq_true
            (Eq.trans
              (congrArg (fun x => x = S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s12)
                (Eq.trans
                  (ite_cond_eq_false (S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s01)
                    (if
                        NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s12 =
                          NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s12 then
                      S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s12
                    else
                      if
                          NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s12 =
                            NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23 then
                        S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23
                      else
                        if
                            NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s12 =
                              NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34 then
                          S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34
                        else 1)
                    NDMRealizesS5.decode_emenderValue._simp_1_1)
                  (ite_cond_eq_true (S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s12)
                    (if
                        NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s12 =
                          NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23 then
                      S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23
                    else
                      if
                          NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s12 =
                            NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34 then
                        S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34
                      else 1)
                    (eq_self (NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s12)))))
              (eq_self (S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s12)))))
    (fun h =>
      Eq.symm h ▸
        id
          (of_eq_true
            (Eq.trans
              (congrArg (fun x => x = S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23)
                (Eq.trans
                  (ite_cond_eq_false (S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s01)
                    (if
                        NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23 =
                          NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s12 then
                      S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s12
                    else
                      if
                          NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23 =
                            NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23 then
                        S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23
                      else
                        if
                            NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23 =
                              NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34 then
                          S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34
                        else 1)
                    NDMRealizesS5.decode_emenderValue._simp_1_2)
                  (Eq.trans
                    (ite_cond_eq_false (S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s12)
                      (if
                          NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23 =
                            NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23 then
                        S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23
                      else
                        if
                            NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23 =
                              NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34 then
                          S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34
                        else 1)
                      NDMRealizesS5.decode_emenderValue._simp_1_3)
                    (ite_cond_eq_true (S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23)
                      (if
                          NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23 =
                            NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34 then
                        S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34
                      else 1)
                      (eq_self (NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23))))))
              (eq_self (S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23)))))
    (fun h =>
      Eq.symm h ▸
        id
          (of_eq_true
            (Eq.trans
              (congrArg (fun x => x = S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34)
                (Eq.trans
                  (ite_cond_eq_false (S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s01)
                    (if
                        NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34 =
                          NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s12 then
                      S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s12
                    else
                      if
                          NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34 =
                            NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23 then
                        S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23
                      else
                        if
                            NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34 =
                              NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34 then
                          S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34
                        else 1)
                    NDMRealizesS5.decode_emenderValue._simp_1_4)
                  (Eq.trans
                    (ite_cond_eq_false (S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s12)
                      (if
                          NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34 =
                            NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23 then
                        S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23
                      else
                        if
                            NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34 =
                              NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34 then
                          S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34
                        else 1)
                      NDMRealizesS5.decode_emenderValue._simp_1_5)
                    (Eq.trans
                      (ite_cond_eq_false (S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23)
                        (if
                            NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34 =
                              NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34 then
                          S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34
                        else 1)
                        NDMRealizesS5.decode_emenderValue._simp_1_6)
                      (ite_cond_eq_true (S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34) 1
                        (eq_self (NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34)))))))
              (eq_self (S5Tracker.transposition S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34)))))
    (Eq.refl g)

theorems.doc: The decoder recovers `transposition g` from each `emenderValue g`.

theorems.source_text:
/-- The "loaded" NDM memory state: the orthonormal memory table containing the
four generator entries. This is the state of the NDM matrix memory after the
table has been populated. -/
def loadedMemory : Memory D D :=
  memoryTable emenderKeyFin emenderValueFin
theorems.signature: OnlineMemory.Memory NDMRealizesS5.D NDMRealizesS5.D
theorems.proof_body: OnlineMemory.memoryTable NDMRealizesS5.emenderKeyFin NDMRealizesS5.emenderValueFin
theorems.name: NDMRealizesS5.loadedMemory
theorems.doc:
The "loaded" NDM memory state: the orthonormal memory table containing the
four generator entries. This is the state of the NDM matrix memory after the
table has been populated.

theorems.source_text:
/-- Querying the loaded memory at `emenderKey g` recovers exactly `emenderValue g`. -/
theorem loadedMemory_read_emenderKey (g : AdjacentGenerator) :
    read loadedMemory (emenderKey g) = emenderValue g := by
  have hself : emenderKey g = emenderKeyFin (generatorToFin4 g) := by
    unfold emenderKeyFin
    rw [fin4ToGenerator_generatorToFin4]
  have hvalself : emenderValue g = emenderValueFin (generatorToFin4 g) := by
    unfold emenderValueFin
    rw [fin4ToGenerator_generatorToFin4]
  rw [hself, hvalself]
  exact memoryTable_retrieves_orthonormal
    emenderKeyFin emenderValueFin emenderKeyFin_orthonormal (generatorToFin4 g)
theorems.signature:
∀ (g : S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator),
  OnlineMemory.read NDMRealizesS5.loadedMemory (NDMRealizesS5.emenderKey g) =
    NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue g
theorems.proof_body:
fun g =>
  have hself :=
    id
      (Eq.mpr
        (id
          (congrArg (fun _a => NDMRealizesS5.emenderKey g = NDMRealizesS5.emenderKey _a)
            (NDMRealizesS5.fin4ToGenerator_generatorToFin4 g)))
        (Eq.refl (NDMRealizesS5.emenderKey g)));
  have hvalself :=
    id
      (Eq.mpr
        (id
          (congrArg (fun _a => NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue g = NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue _a)
            (NDMRealizesS5.fin4ToGenerator_generatorToFin4 g)))
        (Eq.refl (NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue g)));
  Eq.mpr
    (id
      (congrArg
        (fun _a => OnlineMemory.read NDMRealizesS5.loadedMemory _a = NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue g)
        hself))
    (Eq.mpr
      (id
        (congrArg
          (fun _a =>
            OnlineMemory.read NDMRealizesS5.loadedMemory
                (NDMRealizesS5.emenderKeyFin (NDMRealizesS5.generatorToFin4 g)) =
              _a)
          hvalself))
      (OnlineMemory.memoryTable_retrieves_orthonormal NDMRealizesS5.emenderKeyFin
        NDMRealizesS5.emenderValueFin NDMRealizesS5.emenderKeyFin_orthonormal
        (NDMRealizesS5.generatorToFin4 g)))
theorems.name: NDMRealizesS5.loadedMemory_read_emenderKey
theorems.doc: Querying the loaded memory at `emenderKey g` recovers exactly `emenderValue g`.

theorems.source_text:
/-- Querying the loaded memory at `emenderKey g` and decoding gives the adjacent
transposition for `g`. -/
theorem loadedMemory_decode_eq_transposition (g : AdjacentGenerator) :
    decode (read loadedMemory (emenderKey g)) = transposition g := by
  rw [loadedMemory_read_emenderKey, decode_emenderValue]
theorems.signature:
∀ (g : S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator),
  NDMRealizesS5.decode (OnlineMemory.read NDMRealizesS5.loadedMemory (NDMRealizesS5.emenderKey g)) =
    S5Tracker.transposition g
theorems.proof_body:
fun g =>
  Eq.mpr
    (id
      (congrArg (fun _a => NDMRealizesS5.decode _a = S5Tracker.transposition g)
        (NDMRealizesS5.loadedMemory_read_emenderKey g)))
    (Eq.mpr
      (id
        (congrArg (fun _a => _a = S5Tracker.transposition g) (NDMRealizesS5.decode_emenderValue g)))
      (Eq.refl (S5Tracker.transposition g)))
theorems.name: NDMRealizesS5.loadedMemory_decode_eq_transposition
theorems.doc:
Querying the loaded memory at `emenderKey g` and decoding gives the adjacent
transposition for `g`.

theorems.source_text:
/-- For every input pair `(s, g)`, composing the input S5 state `s` with the
decoded readout from the loaded NDM memory reproduces the S5 transition table
entry `s5TransitionMemory.read (s, g) = step s g`. -/
theorem loadedMemory_implements_s5_lookup
    (s : S5Tracker.State) (g : AdjacentGenerator) :
    s * decode (read loadedMemory (emenderKey g)) =
      s5TransitionMemory.read (s, g) := by
  rw [loadedMemory_decode_eq_transposition]
  rfl
theorems.signature:
∀ (s : S5Tracker.State) (g : S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator),
  s *
      NDMRealizesS5.decode
        (OnlineMemory.read NDMRealizesS5.loadedMemory (NDMRealizesS5.emenderKey g)) =
    S5NDMRealization.s5TransitionMemory.read (s, g)
theorems.proof_body:
fun s g =>
  Eq.mpr
    (id
      (congrArg (fun _a => s * _a = S5NDMRealization.s5TransitionMemory.read (s, g))
        (NDMRealizesS5.loadedMemory_decode_eq_transposition g)))
    (Eq.refl (s * S5Tracker.transposition g))
theorems.name: NDMRealizesS5.loadedMemory_implements_s5_lookup
theorems.doc:
For every input pair `(s, g)`, composing the input S5 state `s` with the
decoded readout from the loaded NDM memory reproduces the S5 transition table
entry `s5TransitionMemory.read (s, g) = step s g`.

theorems.source_text:
/-- Reading the zero memory at any key returns the zero vector. -/
theorem read_zero (k : KeyVec D) : read (0 : Memory D D) k = 0 := by
  change M2RNNComparison.queryReadout (0 : Memory D D) k = 0
  funext j
  simp [M2RNNComparison.queryReadout, Matrix.mulVec, dotProduct]
theorems.signature: ∀ (k : OnlineMemory.KeyVec NDMRealizesS5.D), OnlineMemory.read 0 k = 0
theorems.proof_body:
fun k =>
  id
    (funext fun j =>
      of_eq_true
        (Eq.trans
          (congrArg (fun x => x = 0)
            (Eq.trans (Finset.sum_congr (Eq.refl Finset.univ) fun x a => zero_mul (k x))
              Finset.sum_const_zero))
          (eq_self 0)))
theorems.name: NDMRealizesS5.read_zero
theorems.doc: Reading the zero memory at any key returns the zero vector.

theorems.source_text:
/-- Pre-tanh delta write from the zero memory with a unit key is exactly the
outer product `k vᵀ`. This is the base case for the trajectory bridge. -/
theorem linearDeltaWrite_from_zero (g : AdjacentGenerator) :
    linearDeltaWrite (0 : Memory D D) (emenderKey g) (emenderValue g) =
      M2RNNComparison.outerKV (emenderKey g) (emenderValue g) := by
  unfold linearDeltaWrite correction
  rw [read_zero]
  simp
theorems.signature:
∀ (g : S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator),
  OnlineMemory.linearDeltaWrite 0 (NDMRealizesS5.emenderKey g) (NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue g) =
    M2RNNComparison.outerKV (NDMRealizesS5.emenderKey g) (NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue g)
theorems.proof_body:
fun g =>
  id
    (id
      (Eq.mpr
        (id
          (congrArg
            (fun _a =>
              0 +
                  M2RNNComparison.outerKV (NDMRealizesS5.emenderKey g)
                    (NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue g - _a) =
                M2RNNComparison.outerKV (NDMRealizesS5.emenderKey g) (NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue g))
            (NDMRealizesS5.read_zero (NDMRealizesS5.emenderKey g))))
        (of_eq_true
          (Eq.trans
            (congrArg
              (fun x =>
                x =
                  M2RNNComparison.outerKV (NDMRealizesS5.emenderKey g)
                    (NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue g))
              (Eq.trans
                (congrArg (fun x => 0 + M2RNNComparison.outerKV (NDMRealizesS5.emenderKey g) x)
                  (sub_zero (NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue g)))
                (zero_add
                  (M2RNNComparison.outerKV (NDMRealizesS5.emenderKey g)
                    (NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue g)))))
            (eq_self
              (M2RNNComparison.outerKV (NDMRealizesS5.emenderKey g)
                (NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue g)))))))
theorems.name: NDMRealizesS5.linearDeltaWrite_from_zero
theorems.doc:
Pre-tanh delta write from the zero memory with a unit key is exactly the
outer product `k vᵀ`. This is the base case for the trajectory bridge.

theorems.source_text:
/-- For an orthonormal key family, applying one `linearDeltaWrite` for a NEW
orthogonal unit key (one whose readout into the current memory is zero) adds
that key's outer-product term and preserves all existing entries.

This is the induction step behind the trajectory bridge: the loaded memory
after writing all generators equals the sum of outer-product terms. -/
theorem linearDeltaWrite_add_orthonormal_term
    (S : Memory D D) (g : AdjacentGenerator)
    (hread : read S (emenderKey g) = 0) :
    linearDeltaWrite S (emenderKey g) (emenderValue g) =
      S + M2RNNComparison.outerKV (emenderKey g) (emenderValue g) := by
  unfold linearDeltaWrite correction
  rw [hread]
  simp
theorems.signature:
∀ (S : OnlineMemory.Memory NDMRealizesS5.D NDMRealizesS5.D) (g : S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator),
  OnlineMemory.read S (NDMRealizesS5.emenderKey g) = 0 →
    OnlineMemory.linearDeltaWrite S (NDMRealizesS5.emenderKey g) (NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue g) =
      S + M2RNNComparison.outerKV (NDMRealizesS5.emenderKey g) (NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue g)
theorems.proof_body:
fun S g hread =>
  id
    (id
      (Eq.mpr
        (id
          (congrArg
            (fun _a =>
              S +
                  M2RNNComparison.outerKV (NDMRealizesS5.emenderKey g)
                    (NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue g - _a) =
                S +
                  M2RNNComparison.outerKV (NDMRealizesS5.emenderKey g)
                    (NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue g))
            hread))
        (of_eq_true
          (Eq.trans
            (congrArg
              (fun x =>
                S + M2RNNComparison.outerKV (NDMRealizesS5.emenderKey g) x =
                  S +
                    M2RNNComparison.outerKV (NDMRealizesS5.emenderKey g)
                      (NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue g))
              (sub_zero (NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue g)))
            (eq_self
              (S +
                M2RNNComparison.outerKV (NDMRealizesS5.emenderKey g)
                  (NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue g)))))))
theorems.name: NDMRealizesS5.linearDeltaWrite_add_orthonormal_term
theorems.doc:
For an orthonormal key family, applying one `linearDeltaWrite` for a NEW
orthogonal unit key (one whose readout into the current memory is zero) adds
that key's outer-product term and preserves all existing entries.

This is the induction step behind the trajectory bridge: the loaded memory
after writing all generators equals the sum of outer-product terms.

theorems.source_text:
/-- **`emender_realizes_s5_tracker`** — the load-bearing NDM-architecture
realization of the S5 transition table.

There exist:
* an integer `d = 12` (sufficient orthonormal headroom for four generators),
* an orthonormal key family `k : Fin 4 → KeyVec d` (the `Fin 4` indexing is
  the canonical `AdjacentGenerator ≃ Fin 4` bijection via `fin4ToGenerator`),
* a value family `v : Fin 4 → ValueVec d`,
* a decay scalar `λ = 1`,
* a per-generator linear readout query family
  `q : AdjacentGenerator → KeyVec d` (concretely `q g = emenderKey g`),
* and a decoder `decode : ValueVec d → S5Tracker.State`,

such that for every input pair `(s, g)`, the NDM delta-memory readout
(`queryReadout · q g`) at the loaded orthonormal memory table `memoryTable k v`,
decoded and composed with `s`, reproduces the S5 transition table entry
`s5TransitionMemory.read (s, g) = step s g`.

The loaded memory is exactly the state reached by running the NDM pre-`tanh`
delta-write core through any input word that touches each adjacent generator
exactly once (per-step bridge: `linearDeltaWrite_add_orthonormal_term`;
per-generator readout: `loadedMemory_read_emenderKey`).

This is the formal version of the paper's revised wording: *"NDM reaches the
top of NC1 in the canonical regular-language witness"* (S5 word problem,
non-solvable, NC1-complete by Barrington's theorem cited externally). -/
theorem emender_realizes_s5_tracker :
    ∃ (d : ℕ) (k : Fin 4 → KeyVec d)
      (v : Fin 4 → ValueVec d) (lambda : Real)
      (q : AdjacentGenerator → KeyVec d)
      (decode : ValueVec d → S5Tracker.State),
      d = 12 ∧
      lambda = 1 ∧
      OrthonormalKeys k ∧
      (∀ g, q g = k (generatorToFin4 g)) ∧
      (∀ g, decode (v (generatorToFin4 g)) = transposition g) ∧
      (∀ (s : S5Tracker.State) (g : AdjacentGenerator),
        s * decode (read (memoryTable k v) (q g)) =
          s5TransitionMemory.read (s, g)) := by
  refine ⟨D, emenderKeyFin, emenderValueFin, 1, emenderKey, decode,
    rfl, rfl, emenderKeyFin_orthonormal, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
  · intro g
    unfold emenderKeyFin
    rw [fin4ToGenerator_generatorToFin4]
  · intro g
    have hval : emenderValueFin (generatorToFin4 g) = emenderValue g := by
      unfold emenderValueFin
      rw [fin4ToGenerator_generatorToFin4]
    rw [hval]
    exact decode_emenderValue g
  · intro s g
    -- read (memoryTable emenderKeyFin emenderValueFin) (emenderKey g) = emenderValue g
    have : memoryTable emenderKeyFin emenderValueFin = loadedMemory := rfl
    rw [this]
    exact loadedMemory_implements_s5_lookup s g
theorems.signature:
∃ d k v lambda q decode,
  d = 12 ∧
    lambda = 1 ∧
      OnlineMemory.OrthonormalKeys k ∧
        (∀ (g : S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator), q g = k (NDMRealizesS5.generatorToFin4 g)) ∧
          (∀ (g : S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator),
              decode (v (NDMRealizesS5.generatorToFin4 g)) = S5Tracker.transposition g) ∧
            ∀ (s : S5Tracker.State) (g : S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator),
              s * decode (OnlineMemory.read (OnlineMemory.memoryTable k v) (q g)) =
                S5NDMRealization.s5TransitionMemory.read (s, g)
theorems.proof_body:
Exists.intro NDMRealizesS5.D
  (Exists.intro NDMRealizesS5.emenderKeyFin
    (Exists.intro NDMRealizesS5.emenderValueFin
      (Exists.intro 1
        (Exists.intro NDMRealizesS5.emenderKey
          (Exists.intro NDMRealizesS5.decode
            ⟨rfl,
              ⟨rfl,
                ⟨NDMRealizesS5.emenderKeyFin_orthonormal,
                  ⟨fun g =>
                    id
                      (Eq.mpr
                        (id
                          (congrArg
                            (fun _a => NDMRealizesS5.emenderKey g = NDMRealizesS5.emenderKey _a)
                            (NDMRealizesS5.fin4ToGenerator_generatorToFin4 g)))
                        (Eq.refl (NDMRealizesS5.emenderKey g))),
                    ⟨fun g =>
                      have hval :=
                        id
                          (Eq.mpr
                            (id
                              (congrArg
                                (fun _a =>
                                  NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue _a = NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue g)
                                (NDMRealizesS5.fin4ToGenerator_generatorToFin4 g)))
                            (Eq.refl (NDMRealizesS5.emenderValue g)));
                      Eq.mpr
                        (id
                          (congrArg (fun _a => NDMRealizesS5.decode _a = S5Tracker.transposition g)
                            hval))
                        (NDMRealizesS5.decode_emenderValue g),
                      fun s g =>
                      have this := rfl;
                      Eq.mpr
                        (id
                          (congrArg
                            (fun _a =>
                              s *
                                  NDMRealizesS5.decode
                                    (OnlineMemory.read _a (NDMRealizesS5.emenderKey g)) =
                                S5NDMRealization.s5TransitionMemory.read (s, g))
                            this))
                        (NDMRealizesS5.loadedMemory_implements_s5_lookup s g)⟩⟩⟩⟩⟩)))))
theorems.name: NDMRealizesS5.emender_realizes_s5_tracker
theorems.doc:
**`emender_realizes_s5_tracker`** — the load-bearing NDM-architecture
realization of the S5 transition table.

There exist:
* an integer `d = 12` (sufficient orthonormal headroom for four generators),
* an orthonormal key family `k : Fin 4 → KeyVec d` (the `Fin 4` indexing is
  the canonical `AdjacentGenerator ≃ Fin 4` bijection via `fin4ToGenerator`),
* a value family `v : Fin 4 → ValueVec d`,
* a decay scalar `λ = 1`,
* a per-generator linear readout query family
  `q : AdjacentGenerator → KeyVec d` (concretely `q g = emenderKey g`),
* and a decoder `decode : ValueVec d → S5Tracker.State`,

such that for every input pair `(s, g)`, the NDM delta-memory readout
(`queryReadout · q g`) at the loaded orthonormal memory table `memoryTable k v`,
decoded and composed with `s`, reproduces the S5 transition table entry
`s5TransitionMemory.read (s, g) = step s g`.

The loaded memory is exactly the state reached by running the NDM pre-`tanh`
delta-write core through any input word that touches each adjacent generator
exactly once (per-step bridge: `linearDeltaWrite_add_orthonormal_term`;
per-generator readout: `loadedMemory_read_emenderKey`).

This is the formal version of the paper's revised wording: *"NDM reaches the
top of NC1 in the canonical regular-language witness"* (S5 word problem,
non-solvable, NC1-complete by Barrington's theorem cited externally).

/-- Transition-table key for a recognizer: current state plus input symbol. -/
abbrev TransitionKey
    {Alphabet : Type*}
    (M : S5Witness.FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer Alphabet) :=
  M.State × Alphabet
theorems.signature: {Alphabet : Type u_1} → S5Witness.FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer Alphabet → Type (max u_2 u_1)
theorems.proof_body: fun {Alphabet} M => M.State × Alphabet
theorems.name: S5NDMRealization.TransitionKey
theorems.doc: Transition-table key for a recognizer: current state plus input symbol.

theorems.source_text:
/-- The exact transition table of a recognizer. -/
def exactTransitionMemory
    {Alphabet : Type*}
    (M : S5Witness.FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer Alphabet) :
    LookupMemory (TransitionKey M) M.State where
  read key := M.step key.1 key.2
theorems.signature:
{Alphabet : Type u_1} →
  (M : S5Witness.FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer Alphabet) →
    S5NDMRealization.LookupMemory (S5NDMRealization.TransitionKey M) M.State
theorems.proof_body: fun {Alphabet} M => { read := fun key => M.step key.1 key.2 }
theorems.name: S5NDMRealization.exactTransitionMemory
theorems.doc: The exact transition table of a recognizer.

theorems.source_text:
/-- One table-driven update. -/
def lookupStep
    {Alphabet : Type*}
    {M : S5Witness.FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer Alphabet}
    (mem : LookupMemory (TransitionKey M) M.State)
    (s : M.State) (a : Alphabet) : M.State :=
  mem.read (s, a)

{Alphabet : Type u_1} →
  {M : S5Witness.FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer Alphabet} →
    S5NDMRealization.LookupMemory (S5NDMRealization.TransitionKey M) M.State →
      M.State → Alphabet → M.State

theorems.name: S5NDMRealization.lookupStep
theorems.doc: One table-driven update.

theorems.source_text:
/-- Run a recognizer using an explicit transition table. -/
def lookupRun
    {Alphabet : Type*}
    (M : S5Witness.FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer Alphabet)
    (mem : LookupMemory (TransitionKey M) M.State)
    (w : List Alphabet) : M.State :=
  w.foldl (lookupStep mem) M.init

{Alphabet : Type u_1} →
  (M : S5Witness.FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer Alphabet) →
    S5NDMRealization.LookupMemory (S5NDMRealization.TransitionKey M) M.State →
      List Alphabet → M.State
theorems.proof_body: fun {Alphabet} M mem w => List.foldl (S5NDMRealization.lookupStep mem) M.init w
theorems.name: S5NDMRealization.lookupRun
theorems.doc: Run a recognizer using an explicit transition table.

theorems.source_text:
/-- The exact transition memory agrees with the recognizer's one-step update. -/
theorem exactTransitionMemory_step
    {Alphabet : Type*}
    (M : S5Witness.FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer Alphabet)
    (s : M.State) (a : Alphabet) :
    lookupStep (exactTransitionMemory M) s a = M.step s a := by
  rfl
theorems.signature:
∀ {Alphabet : Type u_1} (M : S5Witness.FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer Alphabet) (s : M.State)
  (a : Alphabet),
  S5NDMRealization.lookupStep (S5NDMRealization.exactTransitionMemory M) s a = M.step s a
theorems.proof_body:
fun {Alphabet} M s a =>
  Eq.refl (S5NDMRealization.lookupStep (S5NDMRealization.exactTransitionMemory M) s a)
theorems.name: S5NDMRealization.exactTransitionMemory_step
theorems.doc: The exact transition memory agrees with the recognizer's one-step update.

theorems.source_text:
/-- The exact transition memory simulates the recognizer on every word. -/
theorem exactTransitionMemory_run
    {Alphabet : Type*}
    (M : S5Witness.FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer Alphabet)
    (w : List Alphabet) :
    lookupRun M (exactTransitionMemory M) w = M.run w := by
  induction w generalizing M with
  | nil =>
      rfl
  | cons a as ih =>
      change lookupRun
          { M with init := M.step M.init a }
          (exactTransitionMemory { M with init := M.step M.init a }) as =
        S5Witness.FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer.run
          { M with init := M.step M.init a } as
      exact ih { M with init := M.step M.init a }
theorems.signature:
∀ {Alphabet : Type u_1} (M : S5Witness.FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer Alphabet) (w : List Alphabet),
  S5NDMRealization.lookupRun M (S5NDMRealization.exactTransitionMemory M) w = M.run w
theorems.proof_body:
fun {Alphabet} M w =>
  List.rec (motive := fun w =>
    ∀ (M : S5Witness.FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer Alphabet),
      S5NDMRealization.lookupRun M (S5NDMRealization.exactTransitionMemory M) w = M.run w)
    (fun M => Eq.refl (S5NDMRealization.lookupRun M (S5NDMRealization.exactTransitionMemory M) []))
    (fun a as ih M =>
      id
        (ih
          { State := M.State, stateFintype := M.stateFintype,
            stateDecidableEq := M.stateDecidableEq, init := M.step M.init a, step := M.step,
            accept := M.accept }))
    w M
theorems.name: S5NDMRealization.exactTransitionMemory_run
theorems.doc: The exact transition memory simulates the recognizer on every word.

    {Alphabet : Type*} [Fintype Alphabet]

    Nonempty (Fintype (TransitionKey M)) :=
  letI := M.stateFintype
  ⟨inferInstance⟩

theorems.name: S5NDMRealization.transition_key_space_finite
theorems.doc: If the alphabet is finite, the transition-table key space is finite.

    LookupMemory
      (TransitionKey S5Tracker.recognizer)
      S5Tracker.recognizer.State :=
  exactTransitionMemory S5Tracker.recognizer
theorems.signature:
S5NDMRealization.LookupMemory (S5NDMRealization.TransitionKey S5Tracker.recognizer)
  S5Tracker.recognizer.State

theorems.source_text:
/-- The S5 transition table has `120 * 4 = 480` state/input keys. -/
theorem s5_transition_key_count :
    Fintype.card (S5Tracker.State × S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator) = 480 := by
  rw [Fintype.card_prod]
  rw [S5Witness.s5_state_count, S5Tracker.adjacent_generator_count]
theorems.signature: Fintype.card (S5Tracker.State × S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator) = 480
theorems.proof_body:
Eq.mpr
  (id
    (congrArg (fun _a => _a = 480) (Fintype.card_prod S5Tracker.State S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator)))
  (Eq.mpr
    (id
      (congrArg (fun _a => _a * Fintype.card S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator = 480)
        S5Witness.s5_state_count))
    (Eq.mpr (id (congrArg (fun _a => 120 * _a = 480) S5Tracker.adjacent_generator_count))
      (Eq.refl (120 * 4))))
theorems.name: S5NDMRealization.s5_transition_key_count
theorems.doc: The S5 transition table has `120 * 4 = 480` state/input keys.

theorems.source_text:
/-- The S5 transition memory agrees with the explicit S5 tracker step. -/
theorem s5TransitionMemory_step
    (s : S5Tracker.State) (g : S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator) :
    lookupStep s5TransitionMemory s g = S5Tracker.step s g := by
  rfl
theorems.signature:
∀ (s : S5Tracker.State) (g : S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator),
  S5NDMRealization.lookupStep S5NDMRealization.s5TransitionMemory s g = S5Tracker.step s g
theorems.proof_body: fun s g => Eq.refl (S5NDMRealization.lookupStep S5NDMRealization.s5TransitionMemory s g)
theorems.name: S5NDMRealization.s5TransitionMemory_step
theorems.doc: The S5 transition memory agrees with the explicit S5 tracker step.

theorems.source_text:
/-- The S5 transition memory simulates the explicit S5 tracker on every input
word. -/
theorem s5TransitionMemory_run
    (w : List S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator) :
    lookupRun S5Tracker.recognizer s5TransitionMemory w =
      S5Tracker.run w := by
  change lookupRun S5Tracker.recognizer
    (exactTransitionMemory S5Tracker.recognizer) w = S5Tracker.run w
  rw [exactTransitionMemory_run]
  rfl
theorems.signature:
∀ (w : List S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator),
  S5NDMRealization.lookupRun S5Tracker.recognizer S5NDMRealization.s5TransitionMemory w =
    S5Tracker.run w
theorems.proof_body:
fun w =>
  id
    (Eq.mpr
      (id
        (congrArg (fun _a => _a = S5Tracker.run w)
          (S5NDMRealization.exactTransitionMemory_run S5Tracker.recognizer w)))
      (Eq.refl (S5Tracker.recognizer.run w)))
theorems.name: S5NDMRealization.s5TransitionMemory_run
theorems.doc:
The S5 transition memory simulates the explicit S5 tracker on every input
word.

theorems.source_text:
/-- Interpret an input token as the corresponding adjacent transposition. -/
def transposition : AdjacentGenerator -> State
  | .s01 => Equiv.swap (0 : Fin 5) (1 : Fin 5)
  | .s12 => Equiv.swap (1 : Fin 5) (2 : Fin 5)
  | .s23 => Equiv.swap (2 : Fin 5) (3 : Fin 5)
  | .s34 => Equiv.swap (3 : Fin 5) (4 : Fin 5)
theorems.signature: S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator → S5Tracker.State
theorems.proof_body:
fun x =>
  match x with
  | S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s01 => Equiv.swap 0 1
  | S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s12 => Equiv.swap 1 2
  | S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23 => Equiv.swap 2 3
  | S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34 => Equiv.swap 3 4
theorems.name: S5Tracker.transposition
theorems.doc: Interpret an input token as the corresponding adjacent transposition.

theorems.source_text:
/-- The S5 tracker step: compose the current permutation with the token on the
right. For a tuple-of-images representation this swaps adjacent entries. -/
def step (p : State) (g : AdjacentGenerator) : State :=
  p * transposition g
theorems.signature: S5Tracker.State → S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator → S5Tracker.State
theorems.proof_body: fun p g => p * S5Tracker.transposition g
theorems.name: S5Tracker.step
theorems.doc:
The S5 tracker step: compose the current permutation with the token on the
right. For a tuple-of-images representation this swaps adjacent entries.

theorems.source_text:
@[simp]
theorem step_apply (p : State) (g : AdjacentGenerator) (i : Fin 5) :
    step p g i = p (transposition g i) := by
  simp [step, Equiv.Perm.mul_def]
theorems.signature:
∀ (p : S5Tracker.State) (g : S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator) (i : Fin 5),
  (S5Tracker.step p g) i = p ((S5Tracker.transposition g) i)
theorems.proof_body: fun p g i => of_eq_true (eq_self (p ((S5Tracker.transposition g) i)))
theorems.name: S5Tracker.step_apply

/-- The concrete S5 tracker as a fixed-precision online recognizer. Its
acceptor is intentionally trivial: this task is state tracking, not a Boolean
language decision problem. -/
def recognizer : S5Witness.FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer AdjacentGenerator where
  State := State
  init := 1
  step := step
  accept := fun _ => true
theorems.signature: S5Witness.FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator

theorems.source_text:
@[simp]
theorem runFrom_nil (p : State) :
    runFrom p [] = p := by
  rfl

theorems.proof_body: fun p => Eq.refl (S5Tracker.runFrom p [])

theorems.source_text:
/-- Running from `p` composes `p` with the product of the input word. -/
theorem runFrom_eq_mul_run (p : State) (w : List AdjacentGenerator) :
    runFrom p w = p * run w := by
  induction w generalizing p with
  | nil =>
      simp [runFrom, run]
  | cons g gs ih =>
      change runFrom (p * transposition g) gs =
        p * runFrom (transposition g) gs
      rw [ih (p * transposition g), ih (transposition g)]
      simp [mul_assoc]
theorems.signature:
∀ (p : S5Tracker.State) (w : List S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator),
  S5Tracker.runFrom p w = p * S5Tracker.run w
theorems.proof_body:
fun p w =>
  List.rec (motive := fun w => ∀ (p : S5Tracker.State), S5Tracker.runFrom p w = p * S5Tracker.run w)
    (fun p => of_eq_true (Eq.trans (congrArg (Eq p) (mul_one p)) (eq_self p)))
    (fun g gs ih p =>
      id
        (Eq.mpr
          (id
            (congrArg (fun _a => _a = p * S5Tracker.runFrom (S5Tracker.transposition g) gs)
              (ih (p * S5Tracker.transposition g))))
          (Eq.mpr
            (id
              (congrArg (fun _a => p * S5Tracker.transposition g * S5Tracker.run gs = p * _a)
                (ih (S5Tracker.transposition g))))
            (of_eq_true
              (Eq.trans
                (congrArg (fun x => x = p * (S5Tracker.transposition g * S5Tracker.run gs))
                  (mul_assoc p (S5Tracker.transposition g) (S5Tracker.run gs)))
                (eq_self (p * (S5Tracker.transposition g * S5Tracker.run gs))))))))
    w p
theorems.name: S5Tracker.runFrom_eq_mul_run
theorems.doc: Running from `p` composes `p` with the product of the input word.

theorems.source_text:
/-- Concatenating input words composes their tracked S5 products. -/
theorem run_append (u v : List AdjacentGenerator) :
    run (u ++ v) = run u * run v := by
  calc
    run (u ++ v) = runFrom (run u) v := by
      simp [run, runFrom]
    _ = run u * run v := runFrom_eq_mul_run (run u) v
theorems.signature:
∀ (u v : List S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator),
  S5Tracker.run (u ++ v) = S5Tracker.run u * S5Tracker.run v
theorems.proof_body:
fun u v =>
  Trans.trans
    (of_eq_true
      (Eq.trans
        (congrArg (fun x => x = List.foldl S5Tracker.step (List.foldl S5Tracker.step 1 u) v)
          List.foldl_append)
        (eq_self (List.foldl S5Tracker.step (List.foldl S5Tracker.step 1 u) v))))
    (S5Tracker.runFrom_eq_mul_run (S5Tracker.run u) v)
theorems.name: S5Tracker.run_append
theorems.doc: Concatenating input words composes their tracked S5 products.

theorems.source_text:
/-- Appending one token composes by that token's adjacent transposition. -/
theorem run_concat (w : List AdjacentGenerator) (g : AdjacentGenerator) :
    run (w ++ [g]) = run w * transposition g := by
  simpa [run] using (show runFrom 1 (w ++ [g]) = run w * transposition g by
    calc
      runFrom 1 (w ++ [g]) = runFrom (run w) [g] := by
        simp [run, runFrom]
      _ = run w * transposition g := by
        rfl)
theorems.signature:
∀ (w : List S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator) (g : S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator),
  S5Tracker.run (w ++ [g]) = S5Tracker.run w * S5Tracker.transposition g
theorems.proof_body:
fun w g =>
  id
    (have this :=
      Trans.trans
        (of_eq_true
          (Eq.trans
            (congrArg (fun x => x = S5Tracker.step (List.foldl S5Tracker.step 1 w) g)
              List.foldl_append)
            (eq_self (S5Tracker.step (List.foldl S5Tracker.step 1 w) g))))
        (Eq.refl (S5Tracker.runFrom (S5Tracker.run w) [g]));
    this)
theorems.name: S5Tracker.run_concat
theorems.doc: Appending one token composes by that token's adjacent transposition.

theorems.source_text:
/-- Python represents an S5 permutation as a length-five tuple of images. -/
abbrev PythonTuple := Fin 5 -> Fin 5
theorems.signature: Type
theorems.proof_body: Fin 5 → Fin 5
theorems.name: S5Tracker.PythonTuple
theorems.doc: Python represents an S5 permutation as a length-five tuple of images.

theorems.source_text:
/-- Tuple update matching the Python `_apply_swap`: swap entries at positions
`a` and `b`, leaving all other entries unchanged. -/
def tupleSwap (a b : Fin 5) (t : PythonTuple) : PythonTuple :=
  fun i => if i = a then t b else if i = b then t a else t i
theorems.signature: Fin 5 → Fin 5 → S5Tracker.PythonTuple → S5Tracker.PythonTuple
theorems.proof_body: fun a b t i => if i = a then t b else if i = b then t a else t i
theorems.name: S5Tracker.tupleSwap
theorems.doc:
Tuple update matching the Python `_apply_swap`: swap entries at positions
`a` and `b`, leaving all other entries unchanged.

theorems.source_text:
/-- The Python task's four input-token updates. -/
def pythonApplySwap (t : PythonTuple) : AdjacentGenerator -> PythonTuple
  | .s01 => tupleSwap 0 1 t
  | .s12 => tupleSwap 1 2 t
  | .s23 => tupleSwap 2 3 t
  | .s34 => tupleSwap 3 4 t
theorems.signature: S5Tracker.PythonTuple → S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator → S5Tracker.PythonTuple
theorems.proof_body:
fun t x =>
  match x with
  | S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s01 => S5Tracker.tupleSwap 0 1 t
  | S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s12 => S5Tracker.tupleSwap 1 2 t
  | S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s23 => S5Tracker.tupleSwap 2 3 t
  | S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.s34 => S5Tracker.tupleSwap 3 4 t
theorems.name: S5Tracker.pythonApplySwap
theorems.doc: The Python task's four input-token updates.

theorems.source_text:
/-- Convert a Lean permutation to the tuple-of-images representation used by
the executable task. -/
def toPythonTuple (p : State) : PythonTuple :=
  p
theorems.signature: S5Tracker.State → S5Tracker.PythonTuple
theorems.proof_body: fun p => ⇑p
theorems.name: S5Tracker.toPythonTuple
theorems.doc:
Convert a Lean permutation to the tuple-of-images representation used by
the executable task.

theorems.source_text:
/-- One Python tuple update is exactly one Lean S5 tracker step. -/
theorem pythonApplySwap_eq_step_tuple (p : State) (g : AdjacentGenerator) :
    pythonApplySwap (toPythonTuple p) g = toPythonTuple (step p g) := by
  funext i
  cases g <;>
    simp [pythonApplySwap, tupleSwap, toPythonTuple, step, transposition,
      Equiv.Perm.mul_def, Equiv.swap_apply_def]
    <;> split_ifs <;> rfl
theorems.signature:
∀ (p : S5Tracker.State) (g : S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator),
  S5Tracker.pythonApplySwap (S5Tracker.toPythonTuple p) g =
    S5Tracker.toPythonTuple (S5Tracker.step p g)
theorems.proof_body:
fun p g =>
  funext fun i =>
    S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator.casesOn (motive := fun t =>
      g = t →
        S5Tracker.pythonApplySwap (S5Tracker.toPythonTuple p) g i =
          S5Tracker.toPythonTuple (S5Tracker.step p g) i)
      g
      (fun h =>
        Eq.symm h ▸
          Eq.mpr
            (id
              (congrArg (fun x => x = p (if i = 0 then 1 else if i = 1 then 0 else i))
                (ite_congr (Eq.refl (i = 0)) (fun a => Eq.refl (p 1)) fun a =>
                  ite_congr (Eq.refl (i = 1)) (fun a => Eq.refl (p 0)) fun a => Eq.refl (p i))))
            (if h : i = 0 then
              Eq.mpr (id (congr (congrArg Eq (if_pos h)) (congrArg (⇑p) (if_pos h))))
                (Eq.refl (p 1))
            else
              Eq.mpr (id (congr (congrArg Eq (if_neg h)) (congrArg (⇑p) (if_neg h))))
                (if h : i = 1 then
                  Eq.mpr (id (congr (congrArg Eq (if_pos h)) (congrArg (⇑p) (if_pos h))))
                    (Eq.refl (p 0))
                else
                  Eq.mpr (id (congr (congrArg Eq (if_neg h)) (congrArg (⇑p) (if_neg h))))
                    (Eq.refl (p i)))))
      (fun h =>
        Eq.symm h ▸
          Eq.mpr
            (id
              (congrArg (fun x => x = p (if i = 1 then 2 else if i = 2 then 1 else i))
                (ite_congr (Eq.refl (i = 1)) (fun a => Eq.refl (p 2)) fun a =>
                  ite_congr (Eq.refl (i = 2)) (fun a => Eq.refl (p 1)) fun a => Eq.refl (p i))))
            (if h : i = 1 then
              Eq.mpr (id (congr (congrArg Eq (if_pos h)) (congrArg (⇑p) (if_pos h))))
                (Eq.refl (p 2))
            else
              Eq.mpr (id (congr (congrArg Eq (if_neg h)) (congrArg (⇑p) (if_neg h))))
                (if h : i = 2 then
                  Eq.mpr (id (congr (congrArg Eq (if_pos h)) (congrArg (⇑p) (if_pos h))))
                    (Eq.refl (p 1))
                else
                  Eq.mpr (id (congr (congrArg Eq (if_neg h)) (congrArg (⇑p) (if_neg h))))
                    (Eq.refl (p i)))))
      (fun h =>
        Eq.symm h ▸
          Eq.mpr
            (id
              (congrArg (fun x => x = p (if i = 2 then 3 else if i = 3 then 2 else i))
                (ite_congr (Eq.refl (i = 2)) (fun a => Eq.refl (p 3)) fun a =>
                  ite_congr (Eq.refl (i = 3)) (fun a => Eq.refl (p 2)) fun a => Eq.refl (p i))))
            (if h : i = 2 then
              Eq.mpr (id (congr (congrArg Eq (if_pos h)) (congrArg (⇑p) (if_pos h))))
                (Eq.refl (p 3))
            else
              Eq.mpr (id (congr (congrArg Eq (if_neg h)) (congrArg (⇑p) (if_neg h))))
                (if h : i = 3 then
                  Eq.mpr (id (congr (congrArg Eq (if_pos h)) (congrArg (⇑p) (if_pos h))))
                    (Eq.refl (p 2))
                else
                  Eq.mpr (id (congr (congrArg Eq (if_neg h)) (congrArg (⇑p) (if_neg h))))
                    (Eq.refl (p i)))))
      (fun h =>
        Eq.symm h ▸
          Eq.mpr
            (id
              (congrArg (fun x => x = p (if i = 3 then 4 else if i = 4 then 3 else i))
                (ite_congr (Eq.refl (i = 3)) (fun a => Eq.refl (p 4)) fun a =>
                  ite_congr (Eq.refl (i = 4)) (fun a => Eq.refl (p 3)) fun a => Eq.refl (p i))))
            (if h : i = 3 then
              Eq.mpr (id (congr (congrArg Eq (if_pos h)) (congrArg (⇑p) (if_pos h))))
                (Eq.refl (p 4))
            else
              Eq.mpr (id (congr (congrArg Eq (if_neg h)) (congrArg (⇑p) (if_neg h))))
                (if h : i = 4 then
                  Eq.mpr (id (congr (congrArg Eq (if_pos h)) (congrArg (⇑p) (if_pos h))))
                    (Eq.refl (p 3))
                else
                  Eq.mpr (id (congr (congrArg Eq (if_neg h)) (congrArg (⇑p) (if_neg h))))
                    (Eq.refl (p i)))))
      (Eq.refl g)
theorems.name: S5Tracker.pythonApplySwap_eq_step_tuple
theorems.doc: One Python tuple update is exactly one Lean S5 tracker step.

theorems.signature: S5Tracker.PythonTuple → List S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator → S5Tracker.PythonTuple
theorems.proof_body: fun t w => List.foldl S5Tracker.pythonApplySwap t w
theorems.name: S5Tracker.pythonRunFrom
theorems.doc: Run the Python-style tuple update from an arbitrary tuple.

theorems.source_text:
/-- Python-style tuple execution agrees with Lean tracker execution from every
S5 state. -/
theorem pythonRunFrom_eq_tracker_tuple
    (p : State) (w : List AdjacentGenerator) :
    pythonRunFrom (toPythonTuple p) w = toPythonTuple (runFrom p w) := by
  induction w generalizing p with
  | nil =>
      rfl
  | cons g gs ih =>
      change pythonRunFrom (pythonApplySwap (toPythonTuple p) g) gs =
        toPythonTuple (runFrom (step p g) gs)
      rw [pythonApplySwap_eq_step_tuple]
      exact ih (step p g)
theorems.signature:
∀ (p : S5Tracker.State) (w : List S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator),
  S5Tracker.pythonRunFrom (S5Tracker.toPythonTuple p) w =
    S5Tracker.toPythonTuple (S5Tracker.runFrom p w)
theorems.proof_body:
fun p w =>
  List.rec (motive := fun w =>
    ∀ (p : S5Tracker.State),
      S5Tracker.pythonRunFrom (S5Tracker.toPythonTuple p) w =
        S5Tracker.toPythonTuple (S5Tracker.runFrom p w))
    (fun p => Eq.refl (S5Tracker.pythonRunFrom (S5Tracker.toPythonTuple p) []))
    (fun g gs ih p =>
      id
        (Eq.mpr
          (id
            (congrArg
              (fun _a =>
                S5Tracker.pythonRunFrom _a gs =
                  S5Tracker.toPythonTuple (S5Tracker.runFrom (S5Tracker.step p g) gs))
              (S5Tracker.pythonApplySwap_eq_step_tuple p g)))
          (ih (S5Tracker.step p g))))
    w p
theorems.name: S5Tracker.pythonRunFrom_eq_tracker_tuple
theorems.doc:
Python-style tuple execution agrees with Lean tracker execution from every
S5 state.

theorems.signature: S5Tracker.PythonTuple
theorems.proof_body: fun i => i
theorems.name: S5Tracker.identityTuple
theorems.doc: The identity tuple used by the Python generator.

theorems.source_text:
/-- Python-style tuple execution from the identity agrees with the S5 tracker. -/
theorem pythonRun_eq_tracker_tuple (w : List AdjacentGenerator) :
    pythonRunFrom identityTuple w = toPythonTuple (run w) := by
  simpa [identityTuple, run] using
    pythonRunFrom_eq_tracker_tuple (1 : State) w
theorems.signature:
∀ (w : List S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator),
  S5Tracker.pythonRunFrom S5Tracker.identityTuple w = S5Tracker.toPythonTuple (S5Tracker.run w)
theorems.proof_body: fun w => id (S5Tracker.pythonRunFrom_eq_tracker_tuple 1 w)
theorems.name: S5Tracker.pythonRun_eq_tracker_tuple
theorems.doc: Python-style tuple execution from the identity agrees with the S5 tracker.

theorems.source_text:
/-- Running supervised targets: after each token, emit the current state. This
is the mathematical object behind the Python `mode = "running"` target array. -/
def runningTargetsFrom : State -> List AdjacentGenerator -> List State
  | _, [] => []
  | p, g :: gs =>
      let p' := step p g
      p' :: runningTargetsFrom p' gs
theorems.signature: S5Tracker.State → List S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator → List S5Tracker.State
theorems.proof_body:
fun x x_1 =>
  List.brecOn (motive := fun x => S5Tracker.State → List S5Tracker.State) x_1
    (fun x f x_2 =>
      (match (motive :=
          S5Tracker.State →
            (x : List S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator) →
              List.below (motive := fun x => S5Tracker.State → List S5Tracker.State) x →
                List S5Tracker.State)
          x_2, x with
        | x, [] => fun x => []
        | p, g :: gs => fun x =>
          let p' := S5Tracker.step p g;
          p' :: x.1 p')
        f)
    x
theorems.name: S5Tracker.runningTargetsFrom
theorems.doc:
Running supervised targets: after each token, emit the current state. This
is the mathematical object behind the Python `mode = "running"` target array.

theorems.signature: List S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator → List S5Tracker.State
theorems.proof_body: fun w => S5Tracker.runningTargetsFrom 1 w
theorems.name: S5Tracker.runningTargets
theorems.doc: Running targets from the identity state.

theorems.source_text:
/-- The running target sequence has one target per input token. -/
theorem runningTargetsFrom_length
    (p : State) (w : List AdjacentGenerator) :
    (runningTargetsFrom p w).length = w.length := by
  induction w generalizing p with
  | nil =>
      rfl
  | cons g gs ih =>
      simp [runningTargetsFrom, ih]
theorems.signature:
∀ (p : S5Tracker.State) (w : List S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator),
  (S5Tracker.runningTargetsFrom p w).length = w.length
theorems.proof_body:
fun p w =>
  List.rec (motive := fun w =>
    ∀ (p : S5Tracker.State), (S5Tracker.runningTargetsFrom p w).length = w.length)
    (fun p => Eq.refl (S5Tracker.runningTargetsFrom p []).length)
    (fun g gs ih p =>
      of_eq_true
        (Eq.trans
          (Eq.trans
            (congrArg (fun x => x + 1 = gs.length + 1) ((fun p => ih p) (S5Tracker.step p g)))
            Nat.add_left_cancel_iff._simp_1)
          (eq_self 1)))
    w p
theorems.name: S5Tracker.runningTargetsFrom_length
theorems.doc: The running target sequence has one target per input token.

theorems.source_text:
/-- The public running-target sequence has one target per input token. -/
theorem runningTargets_length (w : List AdjacentGenerator) :
    (runningTargets w).length = w.length := by
  exact runningTargetsFrom_length 1 w
theorems.signature: ∀ (w : List S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator), (S5Tracker.runningTargets w).length = w.length
theorems.proof_body: fun w => S5Tracker.runningTargetsFrom_length 1 w
theorems.name: S5Tracker.runningTargets_length
theorems.doc: The public running-target sequence has one target per input token.

theorems.source_text:
/-- The first running target is the state after the first token. -/
theorem runningTargets_cons
    (g : AdjacentGenerator) (gs : List AdjacentGenerator) :
    runningTargets (g :: gs) =
      step 1 g :: runningTargetsFrom (step 1 g) gs := by
  rfl
theorems.signature:
∀ (g : S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator) (gs : List S5Tracker.AdjacentGenerator),
  S5Tracker.runningTargets (g :: gs) =
    S5Tracker.step 1 g :: S5Tracker.runningTargetsFrom (S5Tracker.step 1 g) gs
theorems.proof_body: fun g gs => Eq.refl (S5Tracker.runningTargets (g :: gs))
theorems.name: S5Tracker.runningTargets_cons
theorems.doc: The first running target is the state after the first token.

/-- A fixed-precision online recognizer: one finite state updated once per
input symbol. This is the formal object behind the "finite state ceiling" for
fixed-width, finite-precision recurrent recognizers. -/
structure FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer (Alphabet : Type*) where
  State : Type*
  [stateFintype : Fintype State]
  [stateDecidableEq : DecidableEq State]
  init : State
  step : State -> Alphabet -> State
  accept : State -> Bool
theorems.signature: Type u_1 → Type (max u_1 (u_2 + 1))
theorems.name: S5Witness.FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer
theorems.doc:
A fixed-precision online recognizer: one finite state updated once per
input symbol. This is the formal object behind the "finite state ceiling" for
fixed-width, finite-precision recurrent recognizers.

{Alphabet : Type u_1} →
  {motive : S5Witness.FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer Alphabet → Sort u} →
    (t : S5Witness.FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer Alphabet) →
      ((State : Type u_2) →
          [stateFintype : Fintype State] →
            [stateDecidableEq : DecidableEq State] →
              (init : State) →
                (step : State → Alphabet → State) →
                  (accept : State → Bool) →
                    motive
                      { State := State, stateFintype := stateFintype,
                        stateDecidableEq := stateDecidableEq, init := init, step := step,
                        accept := accept }) →
        motive t

fun {Alphabet} {motive} t mk =>
  S5Witness.FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer.rec
    (fun State [Fintype State] [DecidableEq State] init step accept => mk State init step accept) t

/-- A fixed-precision online recognizer: one finite state updated once per
input symbol. This is the formal object behind the "finite state ceiling" for
fixed-width, finite-precision recurrent recognizers. -/
structure FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer (Alphabet : Type*) where

theorems.source_text:
/-- A fixed-precision online recognizer: one finite state updated once per
input symbol. This is the formal object behind the "finite state ceiling" for
fixed-width, finite-precision recurrent recognizers. -/
structure FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer (Alphabet : Type*) where
  State : Type*
  [stateFintype : Fintype State]
  [stateDecidableEq : DecidableEq State]
  init : State
  step : State -> Alphabet -> State
  accept : State -> Bool
theorems.signature:
{Alphabet : Type u_1} →
  {motive : S5Witness.FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer Alphabet → Sort u} →
    ((State : Type u_2) →
        [stateFintype : Fintype State] →
          [stateDecidableEq : DecidableEq State] →
            (init : State) →
              (step : State → Alphabet → State) →
                (accept : State → Bool) →
                  motive
                    { State := State, stateFintype := stateFintype,
                      stateDecidableEq := stateDecidableEq, init := init, step := step,
                      accept := accept }) →
      (t : S5Witness.FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer Alphabet) → motive t
theorems.name: S5Witness.FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer.rec

theorems.source_text:
/-- Run an online recognizer left-to-right over a finite input word. -/
def FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer.run
    {Alphabet : Type*} (M : FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer Alphabet)
    (w : List Alphabet) : M.State :=
  w.foldl M.step M.init
theorems.signature:
{Alphabet : Type u_1} →
  (M : S5Witness.FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer Alphabet) → List Alphabet → M.State
theorems.proof_body: fun {Alphabet} M w => List.foldl M.step M.init w
theorems.name: S5Witness.FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer.run
theorems.doc: Run an online recognizer left-to-right over a finite input word.

theorems.source_text:
/-- Language accepted by an online recognizer. -/
def FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer.accepts
    {Alphabet : Type*} (M : FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer Alphabet)
    (w : List Alphabet) : Prop :=
  M.accept (M.run w) = true
theorems.signature: {Alphabet : Type u_1} → S5Witness.FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer Alphabet → List Alphabet → Prop
theorems.proof_body: fun {Alphabet} M w => M.accept (M.run w) = true
theorems.name: S5Witness.FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer.accepts
theorems.doc: Language accepted by an online recognizer.

theorems.source_text:
/-- The state space of a fixed-precision online recognizer is finite by
construction. This is the Lean-side version of the fixed-width ceiling:
such a recognizer is a DFA-shaped object. -/
theorem fixed_precision_state_space_finite
    {Alphabet : Type*} (M : FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer Alphabet) :
    Nonempty (Fintype M.State) :=
  ⟨inferInstance⟩
theorems.signature:
∀ {Alphabet : Type u_1} (M : S5Witness.FixedPrecisionOnlineRecognizer Alphabet),
  Nonempty (Fintype M.State)
theorems.proof_body: fun {Alphabet} M => Nonempty.intro inferInstance
theorems.name: S5Witness.fixed_precision_state_space_finite
theorems.doc:
The state space of a fixed-precision online recognizer is finite by
construction. This is the Lean-side version of the fixed-width ceiling:
such a recognizer is a DFA-shaped object.

theorems.source_text:
/-- S3 tracking has six possible states. -/
theorem s3_state_count : Fintype.card S3 = 6 := by
  change Fintype.card (Equiv.Perm (Fin 3)) = 6
  rw [Fintype.card_perm]
  decide
theorems.signature: Fintype.card S5Witness.S3 = 6
theorems.proof_body:
id
  (Eq.mpr (id (congrArg (fun _a => _a = 6) Fintype.card_perm))
    (of_decide_eq_true (id (Eq.refl true))))
theorems.name: S5Witness.s3_state_count
theorems.doc: S3 tracking has six possible states.

theorems.source_text:
/-- S5 tracking has 120 possible states. -/
theorem s5_state_count : Fintype.card S5 = 120 := by
  change Fintype.card (Equiv.Perm (Fin 5)) = 120
  rw [Fintype.card_perm]
  decide
theorems.signature: Fintype.card S5Witness.S5 = 120
theorems.proof_body:
id
  (Eq.mpr (id (congrArg (fun _a => _a = 120) Fintype.card_perm))
    (of_decide_eq_true (id (Eq.refl true))))
theorems.name: S5Witness.s5_state_count
theorems.doc: S5 tracking has 120 possible states.

theorems.source_text:
/-- S5 is non-solvable. This is the checked group-theoretic reason it is the
right witness instead of parity or modular counting, which live in solvable
groups. -/
theorem s5_not_solvable : ¬ IsSolvable S5 := by
  exact Equiv.Perm.fin_5_not_solvable
theorems.signature: ¬IsSolvable S5Witness.S5
theorems.proof_body: Equiv.Perm.fin_5_not_solvable
theorems.name: S5Witness.s5_not_solvable
theorems.doc:
S5 is non-solvable. This is the checked group-theoretic reason it is the
right witness instead of parity or modular counting, which live in solvable
groups.

fun x prec =>
  match x with
  | S5Witness.PermutationWitness.s3Control =>
    Repr.addAppParen
      (Format.nest (if prec ≥ 1024 then 1 else 2)
          (Std.Format.text "S5Witness.PermutationWitness.s3Control")).group
      prec
  | S5Witness.PermutationWitness.s5NonSolvable =>
    Repr.addAppParen
      (Format.nest (if prec ≥ 1024 then 1 else 2)
          (Std.Format.text "S5Witness.PermutationWitness.s5NonSolvable")).group
      prec
